comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: EMPIRICAL FINDING 2026-06-10 (oauth-claude-code.mdx review + running-broker probe): caveat (b) Claude token refresh is SOLVED NATIVELY by agent-vault. Running broker = agent-vault 0.32.0 commit e01a925 = the doc's exact pinned commit, so the guide is authoritative. Proxy auto-refreshes the Claude OAuth access token within 5min of expiry using a stored refresh token (calls https://console.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token). NO sidecar / NO API-key switch needed. BUT: this requires the OAuth CREDENTIAL TYPE (key=ANTHROPIC, Token URL=https://console.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token, Client ID=9d1c250a-e61b-44d9-88ed-5944d1962f5e, Auth Method=None, Access+Refresh tokens from ~/.claude/.credentials.json), created via the DASHBOARD Credentials tab on :14321 (published on docker host). The CLI 'agent-vault vault credential set' only does flat key=value (set/get/list/delete, no oauth flags) = the STATIC non-refreshing type, which is what the canary used (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=accessToken) and why lead creds expire. Built-in 'anthropic' catalog template is api-key/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (NOT oauth) -> do not use it; closes the STOP note. ACTIONS: (1) bin/agent-vault-provision Claude leg must move off CLI to dashboard OAuth credential (or dashboard HTTP API to stay scripted); (2) services Bearer->ANTHROPIC on api.anthropic.com/platform.claude.com/mcp-proxy.anthropic.com -- reconcile vs canary's claude-api/platform/mcp-proxy host set; (3) GitHub Basic-PAT leg unaffected. Informs ADR-026 D2.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: ADR-026 D2 RESOLVED AFFIRMATIVELY — live brokered substitution PROVEN E2E 2026-06-10. After dashboard OAuth-credential paste (key=CLAUDE_OAUTH), a request from bc-shopsystem-scenarios to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with NO client Authorization header returned a real authenticated 200 completion (model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, content 'Pong!', usage input=8/output=4). Broker intercepted over MITM HTTPS_PROXY (av_agt_<token>:fleet@agent-vault:14322), substituted Bearer CLAUDE_OAUTH, Anthropic accepted. Stronger than canary SB-3 (which only reached 404=transport-ok); this is full auth round-trip = substitution + OAuth credential type both verified live. Vault fleet final state: services github-git(basic:GITHUB_USERNAME/GITHUB_TOKEN), github-api(bearer:GITHUB_TOKEN), claude-api/claude-platform/claude-mcp-proxy(bearer:CLAUDE_OAUTH) on api.anthropic.com/platform.claude.com/mcp-proxy.anthropic.com; creds CLAUDE_OAUTH+GITHUB_TOKEN+GITHUB_USERNAME. Old static CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN removed. Host-set reconcile (doc vs canary) already applied in dashboard. REMAINING for this bead: (1) bin/agent-vault-provision Claude leg still uses CLI static-token path — must move to dashboard OAuth-credential (or dashboard HTTP API) so re-provisioning reproduces the refreshing credential, not the static one; (2) SB-2/4/6 live-fleet flip = relaunch all BCs brokered (gated on lead-5fji auto-clone fix).

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text:
IMAGE GATE SATISFIED 2026-06-10 — lead-if8q closed (v0.2.5 release verified on artifact surface). The fleet-flip image prerequisite is now MET:
- bc-launcher v0.2.5 tagged (commit 1b0ae92, parent 7325384 = lead-5fji auto-clone fix; both defects shipped: :14322 av_agt_<token>:<vault> proxy URL + GIT_SSL_CAINFO on clone exec).
- ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:v0.2.5 BUILT + PUSHED (GHCR manifest HTTP 200, pullable with auth), Claude Code present on PATH (lead-79kp resolved at image-config level).

So the auto-clone fix is now consumable by the fleet via the published v0.2.5 bc-base. REMAINING fleet-flip work is the ACTUAL RELAUNCH of all BCs brokered on v0.2.5 (SB-2/4/6 live-fleet flip) — plus the still-open bin/agent-vault-provision Claude-leg dashboard-OAuth migration noted above. The auto-clone bug (formerly caveat (a)) is no longer a blocker.

VISIBILITY CAVEAT for the flip: the GHCR bc-base package MAY remain PRIVATE until the publish-bc-base.yml 'Set package visibility to public' step is fixed (pre-existing CI defect, every release v0.2.0-v0.2.5, gh api PATCH /user/packages/container/shopsystem-bc-base/visibility -> HTTP 404; BC tracks it as bclaunch-lhh P2). This is NON-BLOCKING for the brokered fleet: the brokered launch pulls bc-base WITH AUTH (broker substitutes github creds), and the v0.2.5 manifest is already confirmed pullable-with-auth (HTTP 200). Only an unauthenticated pull would be affected. No lead-side mirror bead warranted; recording here as the fleet-flip operational note.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: LIVE FLEET-FLIP CANARY 2026-06-10 (scenarios relaunched brokered on bc-base v0.2.5): PROVEN — brokered auto-clone works WITHOUT the old --agent-vault-broker full-URL workaround ('Cloned shopsystem-scenarios into /workspace', no host creds) = lead-5fji fix validated live (SB-2). claude IS baked in v0.2.5 (lead-79kp confirmed: ~/.local/bin/claude->2.1.170; earlier 'no claude' was my diagnostic error — ran as root + find -type f skipped the symlink). BUT the flip hit TWO real blockers, both filed: (1) lead-3q12 — container runtime HTTPS_PROXY defaults to :14321 (control API); launcher derives the :14322+token MITM proxy only for the clone, so claude's runtime Anthropic calls fail (CONNECT 405). (2) lead-2s6y — brokered claude-AGENT boot is blocked: no-host-mount means claude's onboarding/login state isn't seeded, so claude sits at the first-run theme+login wizard and never reaches the agent; the prior canary only proved SB-3 via curl, never claude-the-app booting brokered. Both now block lead-5jbc. Other 3 BCs (launcher/messaging/templates) were NOT flipped — still online on their old non-brokered images. scenarios stopped clean. Lead-host bc-container upgraded to v0.2.5; bc-base :latest re-tagged to v0.2.5 (pulled, digest d0db831). Fleet flip CANNOT proceed until lead-3q12 + lead-2s6y land.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text:
FLEET-FLIP UNBLOCKED 2026-06-10 — both blockers (lead-2s6y baked-logged-in-state + lead-3q12 runtime HTTPS_PROXY :14322 derivation) DELIVERED + LIVE-VERIFIED in v0.2.6 (tag 3b87ca6->commit fadfcc6; bc-base v0.2.6 on GHCR, anonymous HTTP 200 = package IS public, the visibility-404 is cosmetic; lead-host bc-container upgraded to v0.2.6, local bc-base :latest re-tagged v0.2.6) and now CLOSED. Brokered fleet-flip is PROVEN CLEAN end-to-end: a PLAIN 'bc-container launch' (v0.2.6 CLI, env-file ADDR/TOKEN/VAULT + AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM, NO --agent-vault-broker, NO manual seed) boots claude straight to the agent REPL (no wizard), recognized as Claude Max, derives runtime HTTPS_PROXY=http://av_agt_<token>:fleet@agent-vault:14322, and completes a full claude->broker->Anthropic round-trip (answered FLIPREADY). 'scenarios' is FLIPPED and running brokered.

REMAINING ROLLOUT (this bead stays OPEN): relaunch the other three BCs (messaging, templates, bc-launcher) on PLAIN v0.2.6 brokered (same plain-launch recipe, no full-URL workaround needed).

KNOWN FOLLOW-UP (non-blocking): lead-j351 (P2, related) — the launcher's 60s readiness gate times out on brokered boot (claude boots but >60s, so the startup prompt isn't injected). Agent boots fine; only automated startup-prompt injection is missed. Does not block the manual/relaunch flip.

title: bc-base Dockerfile (bc-launcher) does not install Claude Code -> published image can't run a BC agent
description: Discovered 2026-06-09 relaunching the fleet on the v0.2.1 image. The bc-launcher docker/bc-base/Dockerfile is 'FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.11' and installs the shopsystem CLIs + beads + tmux/git but NEVER installs the Claude Code CLI. Result: the published bc-base:latest/:v0.2.1 (1.98GB) has NO 'claude' on PATH -> the BC tmux 'agent' session gets 'claude: command not found', no agent runs, the launcher's 60s readiness gate times out and the startup prompt is never injected. The last known-good image (ghcr :prev-pathkeyed, ~2.7GB) carried claude at /home/vscode/.local/bin/claude -> /home/vscode/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.157 (the official installer location) but ships old path-keyed shop-msg. FIX (routes to bc-launcher BC): add a RUN step to docker/bc-base/Dockerfile that installs Claude Code (official install script) as the vscode user, then re-release. Until then the published image is non-functional for BCs; the fleet runs on a local interim image (Dockerfile.bc-base-rebuild: prev-pathkeyed + reinstalled shop-msg@v0.2.1). So lead-c2kp's build/publish CONTRACT exists but does not yet produce a RUNNABLE BC image.
notes: Fix applied + VALIDATED 2026-06-09. Added claude install (official native installer, as vscode) + ENV PATH to bc-launcher docker/bc-base/Dockerfile; pushed to bc-launcher main. Local full build of the corrected Dockerfile = runnable: claude 2.1.170, shop-msg 0.2.1, bd 1.0.3. REMAINING (outward, awaiting go-ahead): cut v0.2.2 -> publish-bc-base republishes the runnable public image; then fleet can move off the local interim image and agent-vault bake can rest on the published image.
close_reason: FIXED + PUBLISHED + VERIFIED 2026-06-09. Added Claude Code install to bc-launcher docker/bc-base/Dockerfile (15beb33), cut v0.2.2; publish-bc-base built+pushed. Published ghcr bc-base:v0.2.2/:latest pulls runnable: claude 2.1.170 + messaging 0.2.1 + bd 1.0.3. Local :latest converged to the published image; interim Dockerfile.bc-base-rebuild retired. (Run shows top-line failure only from the cosmetic visibility step, lead-c1au.)

title: Initiative: agent-vault credential substrate — eliminate host-filesystem credential coupling for the fleet
description: SEPARATE TRACK from the experiment-discipline initiative (lead-odqd) and from the fabro/substrate track (lead-f6ta). agent-vault was a PROVING CASE for the experiment discipline (spike lead-jkwo, CONFIRM) but its implementation is its own concern. Goal: deliver agent OAuth/credentials via the agent-vault broker so NO real credential and NO host-filesystem coupling reaches a BC. Open design decisions before implementation (per dave 2026-06-09): (A) how the broker SERVER is handled/owned/started; (B) ELIMINATE bind mounts entirely (spike's proposal still mounts a placeholder cred + CA + leaves gh/gitconfig mounted); plus the new gap — postgres (messaging) + agent-vault broker are now TWO supporting servers that must be brought up coherently. Children: lead-v4ih (Phase-2 ADR+scenarios) pending the design decisions.
notes: 2026-06-10 SWITCHOVER PROVEN E2E via canary (scenarios BC). bc-launcher v0.2.4 shipped + bc-base rebuilt (agent-vault 0.32.0 + gh + claude); broker fully provisioned (lead creds for now); brokered git+claude+no-host-creds all proven in-container. Remaining before fleet-wide: the bc-launcher auto-clone bug fix (new bead), durable creds/refresh, fleet migration. See lead-5jbc for the working canary recipe + full state.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: CORE OBJECTIVE ACHIEVED 2026-06-10: the fleet runs with NO host-filesystem credential coupling. All 4 BCs flipped to brokered v0.2.6 (lead-5jbc closed) — HTTPS_PROXY=:14322 MITM, zero host-cred mounts, brokered git + Claude (full claude->broker->Anthropic round-trip proven), placeholder-only creds in-container, durable auto-refreshing Claude OAuth in the vault. Enabling work: ADR-026 (broker) + v0.2.5 (auto-clone) + v0.2.6 (baked claude logged-in seed + runtime proxy derivation). REMAINING follow-ups (none block the core; tracked separately): lead-j351 (P2, 60s readiness gate < brokered boot → manual session-start inject post-flip), lead-vhfa (P1, pre-emit gate false-blocks tag-deliverable maintenance), bclaunch A3 (partial agent-vault triple falls back to :14321 instead of failing closed), lead-lhh/bclaunch-lhh (cosmetic publish-bc-base visibility-404). Recommend keeping lead-mdng open until those land or folding them into a maintenance pass; the substrate itself is DONE and live.

title: Spike: agent-runtime seam-b kill-or-confirm (external NOTIFY wake)
description: Under lead-odqd / eval lead-8mho. agent-runtime is the only candidate competitive with fabro on simplicity (single Go binary, no daemon) and ties on self-host, but its seam-b REFUTED verdict is still code-read. Spike: attempt to construct ANY agent-runtime configuration (custom long-poll tool_call, or external wrapper loop) that wakes on a live postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY inbound and re-enters the flow. KILL criterion: if only a fully-external wrapper works, agent-runtime is a flow-step library, not a substrate — confirm REFUTED and close. Read-only against live postgres, throwaway spike- prefixed BC, never the live BCs. Also test the 30s hardcoded HTTP-tool timeout (tool_http.go:24) against a real synchronous shop-msg send+read round. See findings/substrate-candidate-comparison-vs-fabro.md.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE (dave): not continuing down the agent-runtime substrate path. The KILL criterion is met by decision — agent-runtime treated as REFUTED as a substrate. This closure IS the substrate-eval conclusion; unblocks lead-sr36 (record it) and lead-odqd (broader fabro/agent-vault capability, never depended on this spike's tooling).
close_reason: Closed

title: Journal bootstrap ORCHESTRATION: trigger sc08 in each BC (on bring-up) + lead-driven fleet sweep into persistent snapshot — capability is inert without it
description:
GAP (dave, 2026-06-08): sc08 (lead-fm9x/lead-7hsl) pinned + implemented the bootstrap CAPABILITY but NOTHING triggers it, so the journal feature delivers ~no value yet. Per ADR-018 isolation the rebuild-from-committed-features can ONLY run INSIDE each BC shop (only BC X holds BC X's committed @scenario_hash-tagged features/; lead has no repos/, messaging cannot read other BCs' repos). sc08 was dispatched ONLY to shopsystem-messaging and unit-tested in tmp_path — no real journal populated, no other BC has the capability, no trigger exists.

THREE UNPINNED PIECES:
1. TRIGGER — what invokes the bootstrap in each BC and when. Cleanest: on BC bring-up (a bc-launcher/template startup step, so every BC rebuilds its journal from committed features/ when it comes online). Alt: a lead->BC on-demand 'bootstrap' command.
2. FLEET REACH — the rebuild code (messaging's journal lib) must ship to + run in EVERY BC (scenarios, bc-launcher, templates, messaging), via the shop-msg/journal package each BC depends on. Today only messaging has it.
3. PERSISTENCE — ADR-023 left the journal store shape BC-internal/unverified. The BC journal can be ephemeral-and-rederived-on-bring-up PROVIDED the LEAD snapshot persists (D3, lead-resident) and a pull-sweep runs; nail this so 'is X done?' answers from the persistent snapshot with NO BC up.

PROPOSED DESIGN: (a) BC bootstraps its journal on bring-up (sc08 invoked in the BC startup sequence — bc-launcher/templates own this); (b) lead-driven fleet sweep: for each BC -> bring up -> it bootstraps -> lead pulls (sc05, d0a74c6e8ecb8eb3) into its persistent lead-resident snapshot; (c) thereafter the lead answers completion lookups from the snapshot standalone, sc04 keeps it current. ROUTE: PO authors the on-bring-up-bootstrap trigger scenario + the lead fleet-sweep orchestration scenario; architect decides ownership (bc-launcher/templates = BC startup hook; messaging = store + pull; lead = sweep) + whether the BC journal persists or is rederived. Related: lead-fm9x, lead-7hsl, sc08, sc05, sc04, ADR-023 D2/D3, ADR-024, bring-up-bc skill (lead-side bring-up already exists).
notes: RETIREMENT SCOPE CORRECTED (2026-06-08): messaging clarify on lead-h4q2 was correct -- the journal retirement is SEVEN pins (full catalog.journal removal), not four. Re-dispatched as lead-tydd (request_bugfix, ADR-009 clarify-resolution). Messaging ends with ZERO journal footprint: all 7 feature files + step defs + the CompletionState/LeadSnapshot/ScenarioJournal/SystemStateView module removed. Retired set: 1b21dbb(sc01) 528c08b(sc02) d1f0cf45(sc08) d01313bf(sc03) 307967dd(sc04) d0a74c6e(sc05) 03a396b8(sc07). sc06 f58a7dc3 never reached messaging (ADR-024 D2). sc04/05/06/07 lead-held copies STAY for lead-side impl (ADR-025 D5). lead-9zx1 now depends on lead-tydd (replacing lead-h4q2 as the messaging-retire blocker); lead-h4q2 superseded. ADR-025 amended (7-pin consequence note).
close_reason: Re-home DELIVERED: scenario journal is now a FILE owned by shopsystem-scenarios (journal-query + journal-rebuild CLI, src/scenarios/journal.py, commit 13cebb6); messaging journal footprint fully retired (catalog.journal + 7 pins, commit 99c4c95) — messaging is a pure message bus again. ADR-025 supersedes ADR-023 D2/D3 + ADR-024 D1 (journal=file, scenarios-owned, bus is not a store). Lead canonical committed b41fc96; sc03 dropped. REMAINING lead-side aggregate view filed as separate follow-up (sc04-07 stay lead-held).

title: Journal bootstrap/backfill: BC rebuilds authoritative journal from as-committed @scenario_hash features/ (= failure-recovery rebuild); lead snapshot bootstraps via sc05
description:
GAP found 2026-06-08 reviewing the shipped lead-5p07 journal (dave: 'have we taken bootstrapping/reconciling the journal?'). The 7 MVP scenarios are strictly work_done-EVENT-driven: sc03 appends only when 'a work_done landed'; sc05 pulls an already-populated journal; sc06 treats 'no BC journal entry AND no work_done ever landed' as outstanding. CONSEQUENCE: on day one every BC journal + lead snapshot is EMPTY, so the entire already-built corpus (every committed @scenario_hash-tagged scenario across all BCs) shows as OUTSTANDING — a false-negative avalanche that defeats the motivating use case (answer 'is THIS already-built scenario done?', e.g. the 6 stale-done beads just hand-reconciled).

MISSING PRIMITIVE (one BC-side scenario): a BC (re)builds its AUTHORITATIVE journal from its as-committed @scenario_hash-tagged features/ tree, IDEMPOTENTLY. Bootstrap-completion predicate = @scenario_hash tag committed + suite green (the gated loop's commit-time demonstration), NOT 'a work_done landed' (sc03's trigger). This IS the failure-recovery rebuild (ADR-023 secondary job a, deferred) — one operation serves both bootstrap and recovery; it's the reason the BC journal is authoritative. Lead-side bootstrap then RIDES ON sc05 (pull each now-populated journal) — no new lead primitive.

ALSO refine sc06: 'outstanding' should key on 'not recorded in any BC journal', not 'no work_done has ever landed' (the latter is redundant/misleading once bootstrap exists and would keep marking bootstrapped completions outstanding).

ROUTE: PO authors the bootstrap scenario(s) + the sc06 refinement; architect decides ownership riding ADR-023 decomposition (likely shopsystem-scenarios owns the features/-walk 'which tagged scenarios are present' primitive; shopsystem-messaging owns journal-populate-from-that-set). Vehicle: assign_scenarios for the net-new bootstrap behavior; the sc06 refinement is request_bugfix (tightens a pinned scenario). Related: lead-5p07, ADR-023 D(secondary job a), features/scenario-journal/03,05,06.
notes: CLOSED 2026-06-08 (autonomous, stakeholder-authorized). Core deliverable — journal bootstrap/backfill (sc08 / lead-7hsl) — landed in shopsystem-messaging (commit 036c3a3): BC rebuilds its authoritative journal from as-committed @scenario_hash-tagged features/, idempotent + non-destructive, on the committed predicate alone (no work_done). Hash reconciled: d1f0cf457019db29 == scenarios hash of the bare sc08 block == on-disk @scenario_hash tag. Realizes the ADR-023 secondary-job-a failure-recovery rebuild (one op serves bootstrap + recovery). Lead-side bootstrap rides on sc05 (no new lead primitive). sc06 outstanding-predicate tightening DEFERRED to lead-8z1o (P3, independent follow-up, ADR-024 D2) as a confirmed no-op-for-now — detached from this umbrella as a non-blocker, revisit only if a concrete false-pos/neg surfaces post-sc08.
close_reason: Closed

title: BC scenario-completion journal + lead-side system implementation-state tracking
description: Reconciliation is too much per-dispatch labor (see ADR-018/019). Capability: (1) each BC maintains a journal of its COMPLETED scenarios keyed by canonical block-only hash (ADR-019/scenario-117); (2) the lead has a vehicle to REQUEST a BC's journal; (3) the lead TRACKS it locally as a materialized view of full-system implementation state, so 'completed vs outstanding' is a lookup, not a manual reconciliation pass. Umbrella/intent bead — PO authors brief+PDR+Gherkin first; Architect then verifies pre-state, decides BC decomposition (scenarios vs messaging vs templates/bc-launcher ownership), applies discriminator, dispatches.
notes:
STATUS 2026-06-08 (design complete, nothing dispatched).
ARTIFACTS: brief 009, PDR-015, ADR-023 (proposed; D3 corrected by router -> snapshot is LEAD-OWNED/lead-resident state, messaging only SHIPS the shop-msg CLI code; BC journals authoritative, lead snapshot is rebuildable mirror), 7 scenarios features/scenario-journal/01..07 (all hash-verified).
DECOMPOSITION (ADR-023): lead-onfq=shopsystem-scenarios (sc06 enumeration+KEY, assign_scenarios, READY); lead-9b3w=shopsystem-messaging (sc03 BC journal + sc05 serve, assign_scenarios, dep lead-onfq); lead-if3j=shopsystem-messaging lead-facing shop-msg (sc01,02,04,05-lead,07, assign_scenarios, dep lead-onfq+lead-9b3w). All block this umbrella.
RETIREMENT GAP: detect-only chosen (sc07 orphan-flag = lead-side detector). BC-side ENFORCEMENT gate filed separately as lead-yagt (P2, symmetric to sc116).
BLOCKED ON: all 4 target BCs offline; first outward action = bring up shopsystem-scenarios + send lead-onfq. Held for stakeholder go-ahead.
NOT YET COMMITTED to git (artifacts on disk; beads in Dolt).
close_reason: Feature COMPLETE & reconciled (ADR-018 D6, artifact surface). All 7 scenario-journal scenarios (features/scenario-journal/01-07) landed across the 3 closed units per ADR-023 decomposition: scenarios(lead-onfq: 06 enumeration/KEY, f58a7dc39c4e718a) + messaging-BC(lead-9b3w: 03 journal d01313bf5090bee6, 05 BC-serve d0a74c6e8ecb8eb3) + messaging-lead-facing(lead-if3j: 01 1b21dbb923413455, 02 528c08b5a0a6d024, 04 307967ddfb53fc45, 05-lead-reconcile d0a74c6e8ecb8eb3 shared-not-duplicated, 07 03a396b8dc08041e). All hashes recompute via scenarios hash over bare blocks; 05 reconcile-by-entry realized+demonstrated in lead-9b3w. Retire-and-replace/orphan-anomaly detector (sc07) live. Follow-ups: lead-yagt (BC-side retirement-removal enforcement, symmetric to sc116); recurrences recorded on lead-fnj5 (promote close-step) and lead-hdn3.

title: [blocker - user action] ghcr bc-base package denies write to bc-launcher Actions (permission_denied: write_package)
description: Build #4 (run 26969080245) BUILT the image successfully (all installs resolved) but the ghcr PUSH failed: 'denied: permission_denied: write_package'. The rebuild-bc-base.yml workflow is correct: permissions: packages:write, logs in with github.actor + GITHUB_TOKEN, pushes ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest. Root cause: the shopsystem-bc-base ghcr package pre-exists (referenced in Dockerfile.bc-base-rebuild FROM) — created earlier outside this CI — and does NOT grant the dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher repo's Actions WRITE access. GITHUB_TOKEN can only push to a package its repo is linked to. FIX (USER, GitHub admin): bc-base package settings -> Manage Actions access -> add dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher with Write role (or set package to inherit repo access). Not scriptable via the standard API; requires the package owner. Gates Phase 3 publish (lead-u5lq).
notes: RESOLVED by user 2026-06-04: granted dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher Actions Write access to the shopsystem-bc-base ghcr package. Re-firing build #5.
close_reason: Closed

title: Re-author + re-dispatch scenario 40b (shopsystem-scenarios) to carry the FAITHFUL credential-binding Then-clause (lead-1j1h family contract)
description:
Surfaced reconciling lead-4ibl COMPLETE (shopsystem-messaging) on 2026-06-02. 40b (the shopsystem-scenarios Scenario in features/bc-launcher/40-utility-release-emits-repository-dispatch-to-bc-launcher.gherkin, lines 29-34) was already dispatched via lead-8dx7 (assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-scenarios, hash c842c945fc628381) carrying the WEAK co-presence credential Then-clause: 'And that step references the secret BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN as the dispatch token'.

lead-1j1h family-contract decision (architect, 2026-06-02): all three utilities must pin the FAITHFUL binding contract, and 40b NEEDS A REVISED RE-DISPATCH (do NOT ride on BC self-strengthening — non-deterministic; the scenarios BC could faithfully implement the literal weak co-presence prose and pass, re-opening the hole). messaging produced the reference impl (conftest then_4ibl_dispatch_carries_credential, commit ab27f87): the Authorization value must RESOLVE to a secrets.* ref (inline or via env binding), step-targeted, adversarially proven.

PO RE-AUTHORING OBLIGATION (lead-po owns Gherkin; architect does not author). Re-author the 40b Then-clause to the architect-decided faithful contract (PO renders exact Gherkin): the credential clause must assert the Authorization/token value on the bc-launcher repository_dispatch step RESOLVES to the secret BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN (a secrets.* reference, inline or via the step's env binding) — not mere co-presence of 'secrets.' and 'Authorization'. OUT-OF-BAND carve-out unchanged (GitHub-settings permission not asserted in-suite).

CONSEQUENCE: re-author changes the 40b hash (retires c842c945fc628381). Architect then re-dispatches the strengthened 40b to shopsystem-scenarios under a FRESH work_id; capability still declarative-EMIT so vehicle stays assign_scenarios. On re-dispatch the architect MUST enumerate + cite the conflicting prior @scenario_hash c842c945fc628381 as retired/superseded in the dispatch description. ADR-020 addressing-migration + lead-0217 registry-drift: re-verify shopsystem-scenarios registry root before re-dispatch (was found clobbered to /workspace once).

NOTE: 40a (lead-tycv) takes the same faithful clause; this bead is the 40b sibling of lead-tycv/lead-usc7.
notes: SUPERSESSION CANDIDATE 2026-06-09 (architect): pins the cross-repo repository_dispatch fan-in rebuild trigger, which ADR-022 D2/D3 RETIRES (centralized poll/bump in bc-launcher, lead-czwo). Likely dead work -> close as superseded ONCE ADR-022 moves proposed->accepted. Do NOT complete/re-dispatch. Off the bc-base critical path; lead-c2kp does NOT depend on this.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by ADR-022 (accepted 2026-06-09): retired repository_dispatch fan-in trigger (scenarios 38/40); centralized version-bump-then-rebuild demonstrated by v0.2.1. Live successor: lead-czwo.

title: Family contract: declarative credential-authorization assertion must bind Authorization header to a secrets.* ref, not co-presence (40-family repository_dispatch EMIT)
description:
Surfaced by shopsystem-messaging in the lead-4ibl work_done (status=blocked). When implementing the declarative-shape 40a credential clause ('that step references the secret BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN' as the in-suite proxy for 'authorized credential'), messaging found its step-def then_4ibl_dispatch_carries_credential (tests/conftest.py ~L11816) asserts only the UNCORRELATED co-presence of two substrings in the workflow text: 'secrets.' AND 'Authorization'. An adversarial mutation of .github/workflows/release.yml proved the gap: a dispatch with a HARDCODED plaintext bearer token ('Authorization: Bearer ghp_HARDCODED') still PASSES as long as 'secrets.' appears anywhere -- even in a comment or in an unrelated env var that never reaches the curl call. The assertion never ties the secret reference to the Authorization header on the dispatch call, so it would NOT catch a regression to a hardcoded/empty credential -- the exact escape this scenario exists to pin. Messaging's other 4 mutations (wrong target repo, branch-not-tag trigger, missing Authorization, removed dispatch step) ARE caught; this is the one faithfulness hole.

This is a FAMILY-WIDE contract concern (affects 40a/messaging, 40b/scenarios already-landed, 40c/templates) about HOW the declarative 'authorized credential' proxy is asserted. lead-architect contract decision needed: the in-suite assertion must establish that the Authorization Bearer value RESOLVES to a secrets.* reference -- e.g. the env var bound to the Authorization header is itself a ${{ secrets.BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN }} expression -- not mere co-presence of the two strings. Whether the secret is actually configured with dispatch permission in GitHub settings remains OUT-OF-BAND (per the lead-usc7 / 40b comment). Decision should be reflected in the lead-usc7/lead-tycv re-author Gherkin (the Then-clause wording for the credential proxy) AND/OR an ADR-021 addendum, so all three utilities pin the faithful form. Blocks lead-tycv (40a re-dispatch) and lead-usc7 (40c re-author) -- both should adopt the decided assertion shape. Note: messaging reports the committed release.yml workflow itself (3ce4371) is CORRECT (uses secrets.BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN); this is a TEST/contract faithfulness gap, not a workflow defect.
notes: SUPERSESSION CANDIDATE 2026-06-09 (architect): pins the cross-repo repository_dispatch fan-in rebuild trigger, which ADR-022 D2/D3 RETIRES (centralized poll/bump in bc-launcher, lead-czwo). Likely dead work -> close as superseded ONCE ADR-022 moves proposed->accepted. Do NOT complete/re-dispatch. Off the bc-base critical path; lead-c2kp does NOT depend on this.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by ADR-022 (accepted 2026-06-09). These pin the cross-repo repository_dispatch fan-in rebuild trigger (scenarios 38/40), which ADR-022 retires in favor of centralized version-bump-then-rebuild in bc-launcher (demonstrated by the v0.2.1 release). Dead work — not completed. Live successor for automatic propagation: lead-czwo.

title: Re-author scenario 40a (shopsystem-messaging) to declarative-shape repository_dispatch contract (ADR-021 D2 trigger-EMIT)
description:
Sibling of lead-usc7 (40c/templates re-author) and lead-avrk (40b/scenarios re-author, CLOSED). Reconciliation of lead-4ibl work_done from shopsystem-messaging (status=blocked, scenario_hashes=[]) established that the canonical Scenario 40a in features/bc-launcher/40-utility-release-emits-repository-dispatch-to-bc-launcher.gherkin (lines 15-20, dispatched hash a83760dcc40c57e6) is still LIVE-RUN worded ('runs to successful completion', 'performs a repository_dispatch API call', 'carries a credential authorized to dispatch'). Messaging SELF-REFRAMED to declarative-shape during implementation: its work_done demonstration shows it implemented against .github/workflows/release.yml as a STATIC YAML artifact ('static YAML parsing, no DB', 'the lead-4ibl scenario itself PASSES in isolation'), NOT a live runner dispatch -- because a no-Actions-runner BDD harness cannot exercise a live workflow run (same finding as lead-jx4u/40c, lead-avrk/40b). The committed workflow at messaging commit 3ce4371 correctly uses secrets.BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN.

DISPATCHED-vs-LANDED DIVERGENCE: the dispatched body was live-run (hash a83760dcc40c57e6); what landed in messaging is a declarative-shape implementation. The canonical features/ 40a body is now STALE and must be re-authored to match the family declarative shape decided on lead-usc7:
  Given the shopsystem-messaging source repository
  When its release workflow file under '.github/workflows/' is inspected
  Then the workflow declares a trigger on push of tags matching 'v*'
  And the workflow contains a step that performs a 'repository_dispatch' targeting the 'dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher' repository (REST repository-dispatches API OR peter-evans/repository-dispatch action)
  And that step references the secret 'BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN' as the dispatch token
(mirror the 40b form already landed at lines 29-34 of the same file).

PO RE-AUTHORING OBLIGATION (lead-po owns the Gherkin; architect does not re-author). After re-author, architect re-dispatches the realizable 40a to shopsystem-messaging under a fresh work_id (new hash); the live-run a83760dcc40c57e6 is retired. NOTE: must ALSO resolve the credential-assertion faithfulness gap (see sibling bead) before re-dispatch, since 'And that step references the secret BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN' is the in-suite proxy and messaging proved the naive co-presence assertion is non-faithful.
notes: SUPERSESSION CANDIDATE 2026-06-09 (architect): pins the cross-repo repository_dispatch fan-in rebuild trigger, which ADR-022 D2/D3 RETIRES (centralized poll/bump in bc-launcher, lead-czwo). Likely dead work -> close as superseded ONCE ADR-022 moves proposed->accepted. Do NOT complete/re-dispatch. Off the bc-base critical path; lead-c2kp does NOT depend on this.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by ADR-022 (accepted 2026-06-09): retired repository_dispatch fan-in trigger (scenarios 38/40); centralized version-bump-then-rebuild demonstrated by v0.2.1. Live successor: lead-czwo.

title: Re-author scenario 40b (shopsystem-scenarios) to declarative-shape repository_dispatch contract (ADR-021 D2 trigger-EMIT)
description:
Sibling of lead-usc7 (which covers the 40c/templates re-author and names the whole 40-*.gherkin family). The held 40b payload /tmp/payloads/lead-8dx7.yaml (hash 419ca1df153c1ec9) and the canonical Scenario 'on a version-tag release of shopsystem-scenarios, its release workflow emits a repository_dispatch...' in features/bc-launcher/40-utility-release-emits-repository-dispatch-to-bc-launcher.gherkin both use LIVE-RUN wording ('runs to successful completion', 'performs a repository_dispatch API call', 'carries a credential authorized to dispatch'). A no-Actions-runner BDD harness cannot faithfully exercise a live workflow run -- the same finding the lead-architect already decided on the 40c clarify (lead-jx4u). shopsystem-scenarios BC is now STOOD UP (registered + bc-shopsystem-scenarios container running), so this is the only blocker to dispatching the realizable 40b.

PO RE-AUTHORING OBLIGATION (lead-po owns the Gherkin; architect does not re-author). Reframe the shopsystem-scenarios Scenario in 40-*.gherkin to the declarative shape decided on lead-usc7:
  Given the shopsystem-scenarios source repository
  When the .github/workflows/<release-workflow>.yml file is inspected
  Then it declares a trigger on push of tags matching 'v*'
  And it contains a step that performs a repository_dispatch targeting the 'dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher' repository (REST call to the GitHub repository-dispatches API, or a 'peter-evans/repository-dispatch' action use)
  And that step references the secret 'BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN' as the dispatch token

Out-of-band carve-out (same as 40c): whether BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN is actually configured with dispatch permission is OUT-OF-BAND; 'references the named secret' is the in-suite proxy for 'authorized credential'.

CONSEQUENCE: re-authoring changes the scenario_hash from 419ca1df153c1ec9, so lead-architect must dispatch the reframed body (assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-scenarios; capability still absent/unpinned -> vehicle unchanged) once lead-po lands it. This supersedes the held lead-8dx7 payload. NOTE (ADR-020 addressing migration): the shopsystem-scenarios registry entry will need re-keying when the ADR-020 addressing migration lands; re-verify the registry root before re-dispatch.
notes: DISPATCH-COMPLETE (lead-architect): the declarative-shape 40b reframe in features/bc-launcher/40-utility-release-emits-repository-dispatch-to-bc-launcher.gherkin (the shopsystem-scenarios Scenario, lines 29-34) is dispatched via lead-8dx7 -> assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-scenarios. Confirmed declarative-shape (workflow-file-inspection, not live-run). Re-author obligation discharged; closing so the lead-8dx7 depends-on predecessor reaches dispatch_state=closed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Re-author scenario 40c to declarative-shape repository_dispatch contract (ADR-021 D2 trigger-EMIT)
description:
lead-jx4u clarify (shopsystem-templates) surfaced that scenario 40c (features/bc-launcher/40-utility-release-emits-repository-dispatch-to-bc-launcher.gherkin, dispatched hash 88217562d2896927) describes a LIVE workflow execution ('runs to successful completion', 'performs a repository_dispatch API call', 'carries a credential authorized to dispatch'), which a no-Actions-runner BDD harness cannot faithfully realize. lead-architect decided the verification contract (responded on lead-jx4u): reframe to a DECLARATIVE-SHAPE pin against .github/workflows/<file>.yml, mirroring templates-142 / devcontainer-15..17.

PO RE-AUTHORING OBLIGATION (lead-po owns the Gherkin; architect does not re-author):
Reframe all three Scenarios in 40-*.gherkin (messaging/scenarios/templates) to the declarative shape:
  Given the <utility> source repository
  When the .github/workflows/<release-workflow>.yml file is inspected
  Then it declares a trigger on push of tags matching 'v*'
  And it contains a step that performs a repository_dispatch targeting the 'dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher' repository (REST call to the GitHub repository-dispatches API, or a 'peter-evans/repository-dispatch' action use)
  And that step references the secret 'BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN' as the dispatch token

Secret name decided: BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN (ties to ADR-021 D2 / lead-aj5f private-repo dispatch token).
Out-of-band carve-out: whether BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN is actually configured in GitHub settings with dispatch permission is OUT-OF-BAND, not in the BDD; 'references the named secret' is the in-suite proxy for 'authorized credential'.

CONSEQUENCE: re-authoring changes the scenario_hash from 88217562..., so the lead-architect must RE-DISPATCH the reframed 40c to shopsystem-templates (and confirm whether scenario 38 BUILD-side needs the same declarative reframe). This bead blocks closure of the ADR-021 trigger-EMIT dispatch loop.

## Context
Owner ADR bead: lead-c2kp (ADR-021). Clarify: lead-jx4u. Affected canonical: features/bc-launcher/40-*.gherkin (and possibly 38-*.gherkin).
notes: SUPERSESSION CANDIDATE 2026-06-09 (architect): pins the cross-repo repository_dispatch fan-in rebuild trigger, which ADR-022 D2/D3 RETIRES (centralized poll/bump in bc-launcher, lead-czwo). Likely dead work -> close as superseded ONCE ADR-022 moves proposed->accepted. Do NOT complete/re-dispatch. Off the bc-base critical path; lead-c2kp does NOT depend on this.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by ADR-022 (accepted 2026-06-09): retired repository_dispatch fan-in trigger (scenarios 38/40); centralized version-bump-then-rebuild demonstrated by v0.2.1. Live successor: lead-czwo.

title: shopsystem-bc-base image: no owner, no build/publish contract, no rebuild-on-utility-update trigger
description: User-raised 2026-06-02: when shopsystem-* utilities (shop-msg/scenarios/shop-templates/bc-launcher) update, the image bc-container launches from must rebuild+republish so newly-launched BCs get current CLIs. Findings: bc-launcher hardcodes BC_IMAGE='ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest' (controller.py:32); shopsystem-bc-base appears in NO features/adr/pdr/briefs and has NO owner BC in the manifest. Contrast: shopsystem-devcontainer image HAS CI scenarios (features/devcontainer/12-17: trigger-on-Dockerfile-push, build, push, tags) and shopsystem-templates image HAS publish-on-tag (features/templates/142-145). Both existing triggers are SAME-repo; rebuild-on-utility-update is CROSS-repo (trigger originates in the util repos) -> needs repository_dispatch / scheduled bump / pin-bump automation. This is the same drift class as lead-xq0 (venv drift after BC work) and the reason the lead-pw41 canonicalization fix isn't live in the BC base image. Needs: (1) architect ADR deciding bc-base OWNERSHIP + cross-repo rebuild MECHANISM + pin-vs-float policy; (2) PO scenarios mirroring 142/13 for bc-base build + rebuild-on-utility-update; (3) dispatch to the owning BC. Relates lead-xq0, lead-aj5f.
notes: TRULY RESOLVED now (2026-06-09): with lead-79kp fixed + v0.2.2 published, the bc-base build/publish contract produces a RUNNABLE image (claude+messaging+bd). The earlier-noted gap is closed.
close_reason: RESOLVED 2026-06-09. bc-base now has: owner = shopsystem-bc-launcher (ADR-021, accepted); build/publish contract = docker/bc-base/Dockerfile + publish-bc-base.yml, DEMONSTRATED by the v0.2.1 release (build+push succeeded; ghcr :latest + :v0.2.1 = 200, public); rebuild path = version-bump-then-tag (ADR-022, accepted). Remaining piece — AUTOMATIC rebuild-on-utility-update — is carried by lead-czwo (manual tag-cut already works).

title: Registry routing identity should be abstract <system>/<name>, not a filesystem shop_root path
description: ROOT-CAUSE redesign (user-identified 2026-06-02). The messaging registry addresses shops by canonical name at the CLI (scenarios 11-14 removed --bc-root/--lead-root) BUT still stores a filesystem 'shop_root' path as the routing/collision key and bd-facade cwd. This path is mutable and drifts (BC self-registers /workspace; re-registration from a wrong CWD; lead-root collision), which (1) MISDELIVERS lead->BC messages when a BC's shop_root collides with the lead's path (observed: lead-pw41 landed in the lead inbox), and (2) crashes shop-msg respond with FileNotFoundError when shop_root points at a nonexistent path. Scenarios 39-47 are symptom-management for path-as-identity. FIX: make the routing identity an abstract address '<system>/<bc-name|lead>' (e.g. shopsystem/messaging, shopsystem/lead), stable and location-independent; demote shop_root to an optional non-identity field (bd cwd) or remove it. Subsumes lead-0217, lead-3lw6, lead-gf4h. Needs architect PDR/ADR (addressing MODEL decision; also intersects the ADR-019 open question on registry ownership) BEFORE PO authors the completing scenarios. Owned by shopsystem-messaging (registry lives there).
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE: routing-identity redesign is the SHIPPED, Accepted, reconciled behavior — ADR-020 (Accepted 2026-06-02) + lead-n6yz (messaging ff024c8, scenarios 50-57, 152 passed). 'registry add' takes no path; 'registry list' emits abstract <system>/<name>. Residual (lead-mirror path-era subjects) re-anchored to lead-y6gu. Was wrongly proposed as Tier-A keystone; keystone dissolved.
close_reason: Closed

title: BC-container startup readiness: scripted setup + beads-functional + DB-reachability gate + health check
description: Container readiness gap observed 2026-06-02: messaging BC launched but bd was unusable (issue_prefix missing despite 'bd dolt pull' running), and shop-msg respond crashed on a nonexistent registry-root path. Current scenarios pin mechanical steps (clone, pull ran, container running=liveness) but NOT readiness as an outcome. lead-po authored features/bc-launcher/32-35: (32) beads functional not just pulled; (33) launch gates on messaging DB reachability; (34) idempotent readiness barrier before agent engage; (35) container health reflects readiness not liveness (base-image HEALTHCHECK). Cross-BC span (launcher / base-image / templates) — needs architect decomposition + PDR before dispatch. PO recommends a PDR establishing 'readiness' as a first-class observable gate between container-up and agent-engage.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE: BC-container readiness BEHAVIOR shipped + reconciled — scenarios 32-35 committed in lead mirror, dispatch lead-ieph reconciled and CLOSED (hashes 1f1d178b/e6543853/11778d98/fc3535c2 matched @ c3ccdf7, tree clean). The PO-recommended readiness PDR was NOT written, but scenarios already pin the behavior; not gold-plating with a low-value PDR. File a fresh thin bead later if formalization is ever wanted.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reconciliation gap: lead-pw41 work_done confirms defect (b) concretely but defect (a) construction-site fix unverified
description:
## Gap

Reconciling lead-pw41 work_done from shopsystem-messaging (commit d2290dd, BDD 152/0).

The dispatch (request_bugfix lead-pw41) + ADR-019 named TWO defects:
- (a) Feature-line wrap at the CONSTRUCTION site cli.py:880-903 _build_scenario_payload (the --scenario-file builder), specifically _compute_scenario_hash(sentinel_tagged) at line 895 hashing a Feature-inclusive string. FIX: hash over scenario-block-only form so dispatched scenarios[].hash == on-disk @scenario_hash: tag (scenario 117).
- (b) subprocess shell-out at cli.py:364-378 _compute_scenario_hash; FIX: in-process from scenarios.hash import compute_scenario_hash.

## What work_done confirmed vs left open

CONFIRMED (b): summary cites cli.py:366-377 now describing in-process delegation, no subprocess shell-out on the hash path (line 85 import / 341 subprocess.run remain for unrelated paths). Concrete, located, consistent.

UNVERIFIED (a): the only construction-site evidence is a bare adversarial-probe-list entry 'block-only canonicalization (Feature line excluded) PASS'. The summary's only LOCATED code claim is at the (b) site (366-377) and is characterized as a 'docstring fix'. There is no before/after or location citation that the Feature-line wrap at 880-903 / line 895 in _build_scenario_payload was actually removed. Per ADR-018 the lead cannot read BC code to confirm; the artifact-surface evidence the BC would need to provide is: feed a scenario body through the --scenario-file dispatch path and show dispatched scenarios[].hash == scenarios hash of the bare block == on-disk @scenario_hash: tag (the 117 wire/disk equality), demonstrated end-to-end through the construction path, not just _compute_scenario_hash in isolation.

The three reported scenario_hashes (5b9a8f19802ae15e, a927aab99071a688, 17e9342e3cf69969) are BC-owned messaging canonicalization scenarios, NOT lead-pinned (grep of lead features/ finds none), so they cannot serve as lead-side proof that (a) landed at the construction site.

## Resolution path

Nudge / clarify shopsystem-messaging to demonstrate (a) at the construction site: a --scenario-file dispatch whose emitted scenarios[].hash equals scenarios hash of the bare block, OR a diff-citation that line 895's _compute_scenario_hash(sentinel_tagged) no longer wraps a Feature: line. lead-pw41 itself closed because (b) verified + BDD green + commit on origin/main; this bead carries the (a)-at-construction-site verification that the demonstration did not close.
close_reason: Already done (artifact surface, ADR-018). The (a)-construction-site demonstration this bead awaited is satisfied: all 7 --scenario-file dispatches in the lead-5p07 chain (2026-06-08) emitted scenarios[].hash == bare-block 'scenarios hash' == on-disk @scenario_hash tag (e.g. lead-onfq sc06 f58a7dc39c4e718a end-to-end). ADR-023 finding 3 located the fix: cli.py:965-968 block-only construction + cli.py:107 in-process. Defect (a) verified fixed.

title: [bug] BC self-registers shopsystem-templates with container-internal /workspace path, polluting lead registry
description:
Observed 2026-06-01: when bc-shopsystem-templates ran 'shop-msg send respond ...' from inside its container (cwd=/workspace), the cwd-walk-up resolver derived the BC name and called registry add against /workspace, overwriting the canonical lead-host registry entry that pointed to /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates.

Symptoms: 'shop-msg read outbox --bc shopsystem-templates --work-id lead-xjsq' returned 'no outbox response found for work_id=lead-xjsq in bc=/workspace' even though pending outbox showed the row existed. Outbox rows then landed under BOTH bc=/workspace (BC-internal write) and bc=/workspaces/.../shopsystem-templates (canonical lead-side write), creating duplicate-emit detection noise. The BC's own re-emit summary acknowledged the issue: 'Prior --force complete landed under a mis-pointed registry key (/workspace); this is the re-emit at the correct registry key.'

Two cures:
(a) BC-side: shop-msg respond/send from a BC container should never call registry add; only consume existing registry entries. The cwd-walk-up resolver should be READ-ONLY against the registry when invoked from a non-lead shop.
(b) Per-shop registry isolation: BCs use their OWN copy of the registry (their container postgres), not the lead's. Architectural shift; bigger lift.

Recommend (a). Manifests of this issue: any time a BC writes to outbox while the lead's registry pointed to the same canonical name, the path projection silently flips. Cross-link lead-mxxm (which hardened the absent-path crash) and lead-yi0k (which pinned name.md slug source-of-truth — same domain-boundary discipline).
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE: structurally subsumed by ADR-020 D1/D3 ('there is no path to write'; CWD walk-up derives only a name, registry resolves the abstract address). Shipped registry has no shop_root column / no path positional — /workspace self-registration overwrite is now impossible. ADR-020 Consequences explicitly names lead-0217 as subsumed; lead-n6yz landed.
close_reason: Closed

title: BC primer lacks pin for between-item autonomous execution — BCs pause/ask between inbox items instead of draining
description:
Observed 2026-06-01 on bc-shopsystem-templates: BC consumed lead-8hxz cleanly (implementer → reviewer → work_done emit), then on next inbox check (lead-xjsq, lead-6669 already pending), the BC's router PAUSED and asked 'which message would you like me to dispatch first, or shall I take them in order?' This is exactly the prodding pattern the user has flagged.

Distinct from lead-lez1 (which fixed session-START prompt submission and is empirically verified on fresh launches). This is BETWEEN-ITEM autonomous discipline that lives in the BC primer prose.

The lead primer pins this via 'Standing rule: end-of-turn continuation' + 'Standing rule: idle-detection checklist' + 'Standing rule: choice suppression' (refreshed in lead-6gaj). The BC primer needs analogous pinning.

Symptoms compound with the launcher bug for in-flight BCs: even when the user types direction at the buffer, the BC's Claude Code TUI may have focus on the subagent panel (↑/↓ select · Enter view), so Enter doesn't submit. Hence 'requires prodding to get to work.'

Proposed fix:
- Pin in canonical bc-primer.md (shopsystem-templates package data) analogous standing rules:
  - End-of-turn continuation: after work_done emit, drain next inbox item without check-in.
  - Idle-detection checklist: only idle when 'shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name>' is empty AND no in-flight reviewer/implementer task.
  - Choice suppression: do not surface procedural choices to the BC's session-lead. Pick from inbox in arrival order or by ADR-013 dependency precedence and act.

Routes to shopsystem-templates BC as a request_bugfix. Will need PO scenarios analogous to the lead-side discipline.

Related: lead-dl6 (lead-side end-of-turn / idle-detection / choice-suppression discipline that lead-6gaj refreshed).

DISPATCHED 2026-06-12 as request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, work_id lead-hdn3, scenarios 167/168/169 (hashes b73e3176eeddd58f / 4aa9c618e80c9db0 / 16ad870377ac7514). Predecessor lead-5wrc dispatch_state reconciled to closed via promote --set-closed; dispatch landed (read-back confirmed). dispatch_state=dispatched.
close_reason: Reconciled WS: BC shopsystem-templates work_done (id 264 era) reports scenario_hashes [b73e3176eeddd58f,4aa9c618e80c9db0,16ad870377ac7514] = exact match to the three dispatched bodies in the BC inbox request_bugfix record; bead metadata scenario_hashes_pinned matches; scenario 167 recomputed via 'scenarios hash' = b73e3176eeddd58f (contract-tool confirmation). Between-item drain acceptance pin satisfied: 167 completion-as-trigger/no check-in after work_done emit, 168 literal 'shop-msg pending inbox' + idle only when empty AND no in-flight task (earned not default), 169 arrival-order/ADR-013 precedence + no which-item-first choice surfaced. BC demonstrated non-tautology (probes FAIL pre-edit 3c3cbba, PASS merged aaa7436), pure-additive (zero deletions), gate clean (red e96191b precedes green 66a5a90, reachable origin/main). Installed shop-templates v0.2.0 package data is stale (pre-PRIME-DIRECTIVE) so not the verification surface; reconciled against contract hashes + work_done demonstration per ADR-018.

title: [decision] Make shopsystem-bc-launcher github repo public, or document credential mounting for shop-shell builds
description:
Surfaced 2026-06-01 while building the prototype Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell. The git clone of bc-launcher failed inside the container: 'fatal: could not read Username for https://github.com'. The other three BC repos (shopsystem-scenarios, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-messaging) cloned fine — they're public. Only bc-launcher is private.

This blocks any clean docker-only onboarding flow that pre-installs bc-launcher in the shell image.

Two coherent paths:

(a) MAKE bc-launcher PUBLIC. Pros: matches the framework's open-source posture, simplifies all subsequent image builds, removes a class of friction. Cons: anyone can clone (probably fine — there's nothing secret in it).

(b) DOCUMENT credential mounting. Pros: keeps repo private. Cons: shop-shell builds need build-time secrets (--secret id=github_token mounted to ~/.netrc), users must obtain a token, onboarding flow gains a step.

User has not stated a preference; routing this bead for a decision. Default lean: (a) — the framework is open-source-shaped by design.

Related: lead-?? (publish shopsystem-shell image) which blocks on this.
notes: DECIDED 2026-06-02 (user): path (a) — make shopsystem-bc-launcher PUBLIC. Executed via 'gh repo edit dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher --visibility public'; verified visibility=PUBLIC. Unblocks docker-only onboarding: bc-base image build (lead-c2kp/ADR-021), shop-shell build, and dispatch of bc-launcher scenarios 32-41. Clone no longer needs credential mounting.
close_reason: Closed

title: Repin shop-templates VCS dep to ab59ff8 (post-lead-6gaj) so installed package serves ADR-018 D1/D2 content
description:
EMPIRICAL FINDING from lead-6gaj reconciliation (2026-05-30): templates BC main HEAD ab59ff8 carries the ADR-018 D1/D2 rewrite (10x 'artifact', 13x 'contract surface/ADR-018' in lead-architect.md) but the only published tag is v0.1.0 = aee31ba (pre-rewrite). lead's pyproject.toml line 14 pins shop-templates @ git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-templates@v0.1.0, so 'shop-templates show lead-architect' on this lead host serves the STALE template (0 'artifact' / 0 'ADR-018' / 'hypothesis is fact' phrasing present).

This is the same staleness mechanism the BC's work_done flagged as failing acceptance criterion #4 against scenarios 124-128.

OPTIONS to remediate:
(a) Bump pyproject.toml dep to '@<commit-sha-ab59ff8>' (or a new tag v0.1.1 if the BC cuts one) and 'pip install -e . --force-reinstall'.
(b) Have shopsystem-templates BC cut v0.1.1 tag at ab59ff8; bump pin to v0.1.1.

Note: this only refreshes the INSTALLED package. The .claude/agents/lead-architect.md inline copy is still stale by separate mechanism — see lead-720l.

title: (BUG-DOWNSTREAM) bc-container launch f76fe4e does not auto-submit injected startup prompt; lead-xsmn SRC-ALREADY-CORRECT claim refuted empirically
description:
The lead-architect empirically re-tested at bc-launcher origin/main HEAD f76fe4e (the commit lead-xsmn's work_done attributes the fix to). The launch-path bug PERSISTS at f76fe4e — the BC's SRC-ALREADY-CORRECT claim is contradicted by observable behavior.

## Empirical re-test procedure

1. `pip install --force-reinstall --no-deps git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher.git@f76fe4e` (lead .venv).
2. Confirmed installed package is at the f76fe4e commit (pip show: shopsystem-bc-launcher 0.1.0).
3. `BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME=/home/dstengle bc-container launch shopsystem-docs --repo-url https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-docs.git --shopmsg-dsn postgresql://postgres:postgres@shopsystem-messaging-postgres-1:5432/shopsystem --network shopsystem`
4. Launcher printed "Injected startup prompt: '...'" (success path).
5. `bc-container monitor shopsystem-docs` after 5s AND after 20s: prompt visible in tmux input buffer with `❯` prefix, agent NOT engaged (no `●` response line, no `✻` thinking indicator).
6. `bc-container inject shopsystem-docs ""` → agent immediately engages, processes the buffered prompt. Same empty-inject workaround that the router used this morning at caf62f5.

## Tmux observable evidence at step 5

```
❯ Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md: arm Monitor on
  shop-msg watch --bc shopsystem-docs, then drain pending inbox via shop-msg
  pending inbox --bc shopsystem-docs, then await user direction.
```

(Prompt sits as input buffer content, not as a submitted user turn.)

## Tmux observable evidence after empty-inject

```
❯ Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md: arm Monitor on
  shop-msg watch --bc shopsystem-docs, then drain pending inbox via shop-msg
  pending inbox --bc shopsystem-docs, then await user direction.

● I'll complete the session-start sequence...
✻ Thundering… (5s · ↑ 222 tokens · thought for 1s)
```

## What this means for lead-xsmn

lead-xsmn's BDD run (117 pass) and FakeDockerDriver model are internally consistent — that's the testing layer. The wire-to-tmux behavior of `send-keys '<text>' Enter` in the live container at f76fe4e does NOT submit Claude Code's input buffer despite the discrete `Enter` arg. The bug lives downstream of bc-launcher's send-keys invocation. Hypotheses (uninvestigated; this bead is to schedule that investigation):

(i) tmux `send-keys` with an `Enter` token does not produce the same key sequence Claude Code's input buffer expects to submit a multi-line prompt. (Bracketed-paste mode? Some readline mode that swallows the carriage return until a subsequent input event?)
(ii) Race condition: send-keys runs before Claude Code's input handler is ready to receive the Enter, so the Enter is lost while the text is buffered.
(iii) Claude Code's TTY input layer has changed shape between versions, and the assumption baked into bc-launcher (that one trailing `Enter` token submits) is stale.

The empty-inject workaround being effective suggests (ii) — the first send-keys lays down the text, and a SECOND send-keys (even of empty content with `Enter`) is what actually triggers submission. If true, the bc-launcher fix might be as simple as two send-keys calls back-to-back (text first, then Enter alone).

## Why this is downstream of lead-xsmn's scenarios

Scenarios 27, 28, 29 pin the contract from the FakeDockerDriver/Reviewer surface — they assert "the BC's send-keys driver produces a text+Enter token pair." The real bug is that this token pair is not sufficient in the live tmux+Claude environment. The scenarios do not (and ADR-018 suggests they cannot) pin the end-to-end "Claude actually engages" behavior because Claude Code is not the BC's surface to control.

## Required follow-up

1. Investigate hypotheses (i)/(ii)/(iii) in the live environment (live tmux session, capture send-keys protocol bytes, test two-send-keys-call hypothesis).
2. If (ii) confirmed: bc-launcher emits text-then-Enter as two separate send-keys invocations (or uses tmux `send-keys -t <pane> '<text>' \; send-keys -t <pane> Enter` with semicolon separation).
3. Re-dispatch as a follow-up bugfix against bc-launcher with new scenarios that pin the two-call shape OR pin an end-to-end engagement assertion at the integration-test level.
4. Until then: lead-xsmn STAYS OPEN. Closing it would silently mark "auto-submit" as solved when it is not.

## Cross-references

- Blocks lead-xsmn (cannot close lead-xsmn until this is resolved).
- Related lead-hyee (sibling inject-path bug); inject-path empirical behavior NOT re-tested here.
notes:
DIAGNOSTIC INVESTIGATION 2026-05-30 (lead-architect): root cause narrowed to **single tmux send-keys call carrying both text and trailing Enter as separate args** when text length exceeds a threshold (~70 chars in observed sessions, likely tied to display-wrap or paste-buffering). Empirical evidence (live experiments on bc-shopsystem-templates @ launcher f76fe4e):

Probe 1 (idle agent, 39-char prompt, single tmux send-keys -t agent 'text' 'Enter'): COMMITTED. Agent engaged within 4s.
Probe 2 (idle agent, 240-char startup-prompt, single tmux send-keys -t agent 'text' 'Enter' — exactly the shape at controller.py:565-566): NOT COMMITTED. Prompt sits as input-buffer content with ❯ prefix.
Probe 3 (idle agent, same 240-char prompt, but split: send-keys 'text' then a SEPARATE send-keys 'Enter'): COMMITTED. Agent engaged.
Probe 4 (idle agent, same 240-char prompt, single call with 'C-m' instead of 'Enter'): NOT COMMITTED. Same buggy behavior. Confirms it is NOT the symbolic-vs-raw key name.
Probe 5 (idle, send-keys -l literal mode for text then separate send-keys 'Enter'): COMMITTED. Two-call shape works regardless of -l.
Probe 7 (idle, 240-char single call, waited 15s+): still NOT COMMITTED. Refutes the race hypothesis — it does not eventually self-resolve.
Probe end-to-end on bc-shopsystem-docs (fresh launch via bc-container launch @ f76fe4e): launcher's send-keys yielded buffered-but-uncommitted prompt; manual 'docker exec ... tmux send-keys -t agent Enter' (second discrete call) committed it and agent engaged (Monitor event + registry warning observable). Verifies the two-call fix end-to-end against the real launcher path.

ROOT-CAUSE LAYER: bc-launcher's send-keys construction. tmux send-keys concatenates multiple key-args into a single keystream sent to the pane, so 'text' 'Enter' in one call writes <text-bytes><CR> to Claude Code's TTY as a single contiguous block. Claude Code (TUI/Ink-based) appears to consume the leading <text> into its input buffer and the trailing <CR> is absorbed by the same input-buffer mode without triggering submission — likely because Claude Code treats single-event multi-byte payloads above some length as a paste (bracketed-paste-style behavior even without the explicit BPM escape, since terminals/TUIs commonly heuristically detect 'fast' multi-char input as paste). A separate send-keys invocation triggers a fresh write() syscall on the pty after a slight kernel-scheduling gap, which Claude Code then sees as a discrete keypress event.

PROPOSED FIX (bc-launcher): both invocations on lines 565-566 (launch) and 601 (inject) must split into two send-keys calls:

  tmux send-keys -t agent <text>
  tmux send-keys -t agent Enter

In Python driver terms, this is two exec_run calls with separate argv lists, NOT a single call with both. The fix is symmetric across launch and inject paths.

WHY THE BC'S BDD COVERAGE MISSED IT (lead-xsmn scenarios 27/28/29): FakeDockerDriver pins 'an exec_run with argv containing both text and Enter as discrete elements is the contract.' Per ADR-018 this is the BC's contract surface, but the live tmux+Claude semantics are a higher integration level. Scenarios must be re-written to pin 'two separate exec_run invocations, the first carrying text, the second carrying Enter alone.' The single-call shape is the buggy artifact.

NOT YET TESTED but unnecessary for the fix: (a) embedded literal newline byte inside text (alternative shape rejected anyway because Claude expects discrete keypress events); (b) precise length threshold (probably display-width / soft-wrap boundary, but irrelevant to the fix recommendation).

FOLLOW-UP BEAD TO FILE: P1 request_bugfix candidate against shopsystem-bc-launcher: split the single send-keys call into two for both launch and inject paths. Scenarios 27 and 28 must be re-pinned to pin two distinct exec_run calls (the current FakeDockerDriver-asserted single call satisfies the buggy code).
Follow-up bead FILED: lead-lez1 (P1 BUG) — request_bugfix candidate carrying the two-call fix and re-pinned scenarios 27/28. Blocks lead-9q0f, lead-xsmn, lead-hyee. Router to dispatch via shop-msg send request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher when ready.
DOWNSTREAM ROOT-CAUSE FIX DISPATCHED 2026-05-30 via lead-lez1.

The follow-up bead lead-lez1 was dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher carrying scenarios 30 (launch path) and 31 (inject path) that pin the two-discrete-send-keys-invocations shape. Scenarios 27 (wire 0e733774844ed9f3) and 28 (wire 17518db1dc1c9001) are scheduled for retirement on landing. Scenario 29 (wire 5ef728039884a9a2) stays unchanged.

Wire hashes:
- 30 (launch): 6477b2ab3720ac53
- 31 (inject): ad68aaf60377706e

DO NOT CLOSE on dispatch. Closure on work_done arriving for lead-lez1 AND lead-side empirical re-test passing (bc-container launch shopsystem-docs ... shows agent engaged with the agent-active indicator at +5s without empty-inject workaround).
close_reason: Closed as the diagnostic that surfaced lead-lez1 AND resolves via the lead-lez1 fix. lead-9q0f's empirical re-test at f76fe4e refuted the lead-xsmn SRC-ALREADY-CORRECT claim (launch-path bug persisted); that refutation drove root-cause analysis (probes 1-7) producing scenarios 30/31 and the lead-lez1 dispatch. Now resolved: at 9cbb9c1, bc-container launch shopsystem-templates with default startup prompt produces an ENGAGED agent (Monitor armed, pending inbox drained, prose 'Session-start sequence complete' emitted) at +15s without manual empty-inject. See lead-lez1 closure rationale.

title: bc-container inject <bc> <text> injects without committing Enter (parallel to lead-xsmn launch-path bug)
description:
Discovered while authoring scenarios for lead-xsmn. The launch-path bug (lead-xsmn) and the inject-path bug share the same root cause: tmux send-keys is being invoked with a literal newline appended to the text payload rather than 'Enter' as a discrete key argument. Scenario 07 (features/bc-launcher/07-inject-sends-prompt-to-tmux-session.gherkin) only pins 'text + newline byte sent via tmux send-keys', which is satisfied by the buggy implementation. The empirical workaround the user discovered for the launch-path bug ('bc-container inject <bc> ""' as forced Enter) only works because the empty-text inject path apparently routes through a different send-keys invocation; non-empty inject suffers the same submission failure.

Fix is parallel to lead-xsmn: tmux send-keys '<text>' Enter (Enter as a separate argument). Lead-side pin: scenario 28 (features/bc-launcher/28-inject-submits-prompt-autonomously.gherkin) supersedes scenario 07. Hash: f625ac1baf0521ab.

Not independently reproduced as of authoring — the parallel-bug assertion is by code-path inference from the documented workaround. Implementer should empirically reproduce on the BC host before fixing.
notes:
RESOLVED IN-FLIGHT 2026-05-30 (lead-architect): the resolution for this bug is carried as scenario 28 in the request_bugfix dispatch for lead-xsmn (BC: shopsystem-bc-launcher).
- Resolving dispatch work_id: lead-xsmn
- Scenario 28 wire hash on the BC: 17518db1dc1c9001
- Scenario 28 block hash: f625ac1baf0521ab
- When work_done arrives for lead-xsmn, this bead closes in the same motion (same root cause, single tmux-send-keys-with-Enter fix on the BC).
- Dependency: this bead depends on lead-xsmn (see bd dep).
WORK_DONE FOR PARENT lead-xsmn RECEIVED 2026-05-30 — DO NOT CLOSE.

The launch-path resolution claimed by lead-xsmn was empirically refuted at bc-launcher@f76fe4e (see lead-xsmn notes). lead-hyee stays open by parallelism: the inject-path bug it tracks shares the same root cause (text+Enter via tmux send-keys), and the launch-path empirical re-test shows that root cause is NOT actually resolved at f76fe4e.

The empty-inject workaround (bc-container inject <bc> "") was tested as part of the launch-path re-test and was effective — agent engages after empty inject. This is consistent with lead-hyee's original framing (non-empty inject suffers the same submission failure as launch-path).

Cross-link: related to lead-9q0f (P1 BUG-DOWNSTREAM, filed 2026-05-30).

When lead-9q0f closes (the actual downstream fix lands and is empirically re-tested), lead-hyee can close in the same motion.

ROOT-CAUSE FOLLOW-UP DISPATCHED 2026-05-30 via lead-lez1.

The parallel inject-path bug is resolved in the same motion as lead-xsmn / lead-9q0f via lead-lez1. lead-lez1 dispatches request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher carrying scenario 31 (inject two-discrete-send-keys-invocations, wire ad68aaf60377706e) which supersedes scenario 28 (wire 17518db1dc1c9001) — the original inject pin from lead-xsmn that the empirically-buggy single-call shape satisfied.

The code-location fix: controller.py:601 splits into two exec_run calls (text-only first, then Enter-only). Same shape as the launch-path fix at lines 565-566 (carried by scenario 30).

DO NOT CLOSE on dispatch. Closure when lead-lez1 closes (work_done arrives + lead-side empirical re-test confirms both launch and inject paths commit on their own).
close_reason: Closed as sibling of lead-lez1 (root-cause fix verified empirically at bc-launcher 9cbb9c1). lead-hyee was the parallel inject-path bug — same root cause (single tmux send-keys with literal newline rather than discrete Enter key) — resolved by the same two-discrete-invocations fix in controller.py inject path (lines 622-631 per BC work_done). BC mutation test confirmed inject scenario 31 (ad68aaf60377706e) turns RED on collapse, GREEN on restore. See lead-lez1 closure rationale for full evidence chain.

description:

title: mechanism: shop-msg respond / consume asymmetry — degraded emit unrecoverable
description:
Captured 2026-05-29 via lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery mechanism_observation from shopsystem-templates BC.

PROBLEM:
shop-msg respond collision-checks on the LEAD-INBOX row, but shop-msg consume outbox only flips consumed=TRUE on the BC-OUTBOX marker. So when a BC sends a degraded/test/erroneous response (clarify vs work_done; partial scenario_hashes; placeholder summary), they cannot amend or re-emit:
  - shop-msg respond <type> --work-id <id> ... → "refusing to overwrite existing response for work_id=<id>"
  - shop-msg consume outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id> --message-type <type> → succeeds but does NOT release the lead-inbox slot
  - retry of respond → same collision refusal

Source citations from the observation:
  - shopsystem-messaging/src/shop_msg/storage.py:444-466 (consume_outbox_message: UPDATE only the BC-outbox marker)
  - shopsystem-messaging/src/shop_msg/storage.py:846-860 (insert_bc_response: SELECT on lead-inbox row → CollisionError)

LOAD-BEARING IMPACT:
Any role-discipline failure where a BC realizes their response was wrong is currently unrecoverable except by direct DB manipulation, which the BC template forbids. Documented occurrence: lead-cw7 — Reviewer ran a small test invocation of 'shop-msg respond work_done ... --summary "test"' to probe --scenario-hash repeatable semantics, landed a degraded row, could not deliver the real response (95 passing hashes, full reviewer summary including adversarial-probe outcomes and pre-emit verification evidence). Recovery vehicle was a mechanism_observation under a fresh work_id ('lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery') inlining the real content — workable here only because the work_id substring fits attribution heuristics.

CROSS-LINKS:
  - lead-cw7 (CLOSED, reconciled via observation — see lead-cw7 close reason + lead-plt for emit-fidelity defect that surfaced under this exact lockout)
  - lead-plt P2 (BC work_done emit fidelity — summary placeholder + incomplete scenario_hashes from lead-cw7) — directly downstream of this; if recovery were possible, plt would be unnecessary
  - lead-o6tp P1 (move procedural pre-emit checks from template prose to CLI enforcement) — related move toward CLI-layer enforcement; this bead is about the inverse case (CLI is TOO strict to recover from)

CANDIDATE FIXES (BC-proposed, lead-po to author scenarios before dispatch):
  (a) Add a --force flag to shop-msg respond {clarify,work_done,mechanism_observation} that DELETEs the existing lead-inbox row before insert (gated to keep default safe).
  (b) Make shop-msg consume outbox also release the lead-inbox slot (single-source-of-truth: a consumed response is no longer authoritative; the BC may re-emit).
  (c) Add shop-msg respond amend as a distinct verb.
  Also: BC role templates should be tightened to warn that ANY --status / --summary / --scenario-hash test invocation lands a permanent row.

NEXT STEPS:
  1. Lead-po: author scenarios pinning the chosen recovery semantics (one of a/b/c, or a hybrid).
  2. Lead-architect: dispatch request_bugfix (capability-exists-unpinned: respond + consume both work today, the asymmetry is just not pinned) to shopsystem-messaging.
  3. Do NOT dispatch yet — needs scenario authoring first.
notes: 2026-05-29 (post-carrier): DISPATCHED. Vehicle=request_bugfix; target=shopsystem-messaging; work-id=lead-nn5f. Pre-state verified empirically: messaging HEAD=b2649b6 on origin/main (carrier lead-xscs landed); tree clean; consume_outbox_message in storage.py @b2649b6 only flips BC-outbox marker, leaves lead-inbox row intact (confirmed by reading 4 pinned consume_outbox.feature scenarios — they assert ONLY pending outbox effects, silent on lead-inbox). 5 scenarios dispatched (hashes: 2d71c0ab6106d844, 2df50687c51c2ad2, e0345303cb4ebc6f, d64a23dc1ecbd7c9, afcbe25bb23eca94). Scope ADDITIVE — no prior pinned hash retired/superseded; the new bundle extends consume + --force surfaces. Cited @scenario_hash enumeration (edfe8c68149298fb, 40fb3c3e71063966, a2e50c164ead0531, abefd734c20344db for consume; 33663625, 60887336, 399c3616, 3d85417a, 1fb95794, bebe85d1 for force) in dispatch description with explicit no-retirement note. Inbox deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox.
