title: mechanism_observation lead-2ca: carried scenarios[].hash uses Feature-line-included canonicalization; @scenario_hash tag uses scenario-block-only
description:
Mechanism observation from shopsystem-messaging during lead-m32 dispatch.

OBSERVED (load-bearing):
- lead-m32 request_bugfix carried scenarios[0].hash = 07d405ae455c5553, computed over the gherkin field whose first line is the Feature: header.
- Canonical 'scenarios hash' CLI hashes the SCENARIO BLOCK ALONE (tag + Scenario Outline + steps + Examples, no Feature line) and yields 641bae76c069bf5b for the identical body.
- Feeding the carried gherkin verbatim (Feature line included) reproduces 07d405ae455c5553; feeding only the scenario block reproduces 641bae76c069bf5b.
- The preserved feature-file hashes (2426b67df01d4168, 3df3c5993a4f60e5) only reproduce under the scenario-block-only representation.
- Therefore the @scenario_hash tag written into features/ must be 641bae... (scenario-block hash) NOT scenarios[].hash (07d405...). A literal copy of scenarios[].hash into the @scenario_hash tag would break lead-side hash-register reconciliation.
- Catchable here only because the dispatch description stated the correct target (641bae76c069bf5b) in prose.

EVIDENCE:
- features/watch_listen_drop_reconnect.feature:19
- src/shop_msg/storage.py:1031

PROPOSED (BC's view): align carried gherkin representation with canonical scenarios-hash unit (scenario block only), OR document the two hashing units explicitly in the message schema so BCs do not copy scenarios[].hash into the @scenario_hash tag.

RELATION TO lead-wgv:
This is a fresh, in-the-wild instance of the surface lead-wgv P2 already names ('scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form'). lead-2ca specifies the two units precisely: (i) Feature-line-included (what shop-msg send computes onto scenarios[].hash) vs (ii) scenario-block-only (what 'scenarios hash' CLI computes and what @scenario_hash tags use). The Slice-16 anti-rationalization in the Architect template names this exact failure mode.

DECISION: keep this bead as the fresh occurrence with the precise hash-unit naming; cross-link to lead-wgv as the umbrella canonicalization-ambiguity bead. Resolution proceeds on lead-wgv.

CROSS-LINKS:
- lead-wgv (umbrella, P2 OPEN — scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity)
- lead-m32 (parent dispatch where observed)
notes: RELATED canonical-tag finding (lead-r9r5/lead-y0nu, 2026-06-12): scenarios hash strips @scenario_hash: only when ALONE on its line, never strips @bc:. So canonical features/ form = @scenario_hash: alone, @bc: added at assign_scenarios dispatch. Single-line @bc+@scenario_hash is CIRCULAR (never reproduces). lead-y0nu's earlier single-line fix was imperfect but the already-dispatched copies (msg 26-29, journal 09-10) are self-consistent with the BC register — leave them. Part of this hash-canonicalization cluster.

title: [ADR-candidate] move procedural pre-emit checks from template prose to shop-msg respond CLI enforcement
description:
User observation 2026-05-29: the framework has been pinning procedural consistency checks (clean tree before emit; commit on origin/main before emit; scenario_hashes match committed features) as PROSE in canonical templates (bc-reviewer.md, bc-implementer.md, bc-primer.md). The "check" only happens if the agent reads and follows the prose. This is fragile (lead-cw7 had to be filed specifically because agents weren't following the prose — three documented occurrences before the fix landed) and verbose (4 scenarios × 2 roles × N preconditions × N BC re-pours per check).

Counter-design: enforce the checks in shop-msg respond itself. The CLI knows the work_id and can run git checks before constructing the message. Refuse the call if preconditions fail. Template prose becomes a one-line pointer ("the CLI enforces these; you don't need to check manually"). Scenarios shift to messaging BC pinning CLI behavior, which is mechanically verifiable.

CONCRETE LIST — which recent scenarios should move:

(1) lead-cw7 + lead-8lm (8 scenarios across 105-108 and 109-112) — clean working tree + work_id commit on origin/main. Should be a single shop-msg respond work_done precondition check, applied to both Reviewer-emitted (assign_scenarios) and Implementer-emitted (request_maintenance / non-scenario request_bugfix). The CLI runs `git status --porcelain` and `git log` against the BC repo it's invoked from; refuses with named-file error if not clean or refuses with missing-commit error if no work_id reference on origin/main.

(2) lead-83l (4 scenarios 113-116) — scenario_hashes pre-emit recompute. Should be: `shop-msg respond work_done --scenario-hash <h>` verifies each hash against `scenarios hash` recomputed locally before sending. Refuses if mismatch (stale hash), missing (payload omits a feature/-present scenario), or orphan (payload carries hash not in features/).

(3) lead-ke1 (imperative session-start in bc-primer.md) — PARTIALLY already CLI-driven via lead-9sq (--startup-prompt default in bc-container launch). The autonomous-engagement is still primer prose because Claude Code requires a user prompt to act and the prompt content references the primer.

WHAT STAYS PROSE (cannot be CLI-checked, are agent judgment):
- Continuous-action / end-of-turn continuation (lead-dl6)
- Idle-detection checklist (lead-dl6)
- Choice-suppression (lead-dl6)
- Sufficiency checks on inbound messages (these require natural-language interpretation)

PROPOSAL:
(a) Author a new messaging BC scenario set pinning shop-msg respond work_done preconditions (clean-tree, commit-on-origin-main, scenario_hashes-match).
(b) Dispatch to shopsystem-messaging.
(c) Once landed, RETRO-RETIRE the lead-cw7 / lead-8lm / lead-83l template scenarios (105-116). Replace with one-line prose: "the CLI enforces these preconditions; if your emit attempt is refused, fix the underlying state and try again."
(d) Establish ADR formalizing: "procedural preconditions are CLI-layer; judgment behaviors are template-prose-layer." Author and ratify.

OPEN QUESTION: should lead-83l (just dispatched, queued at templates BC) be CANCELLED in favor of the messaging BC scenarios, or allowed to land and then retired? Architect judgment.
notes:
2026-05-29 REVISION per user correction: do NOT put the checks in shop-msg. shop-msg's domain is messaging protocol (postgres deposit/read/consume/watch); git status / commit / hash recompute is orthogonal policy enforcement. Coupling shop-msg to git is the same wrong-layer mistake the lead-767 round-3 conversation just rejected for resolve_lead_shop() vs fxhyg-prefix matching.

REVISED PROPOSAL: enforcement lives in the role-template-package layer (shop-templates), not the messaging layer. Two candidate shapes:

(a) WRAPPER COMMAND — e.g., `bc-emit work-done --scenario-hash X --summary "..."` that:
  1. runs preflight: git status --porcelain, git log for work_id on origin/main, scenarios hash recompute per --scenario-hash.
  2. if all green, invokes `shop-msg respond work_done` with validated args.
  3. if any fail, exits non-zero with named errors (which file is dirty; which work_id has no reachable commit; which hash is stale).
  Template prose: "use `bc-emit work-done`, not `shop-msg respond work_done`, for emits. The wrapper is the only emit path that survives lead-side reconciliation; the bare CLI is for forced-recovery only."

(b) STANDALONE PREFLIGHT — e.g., `bc-preflight work-done <work_id>` that:
  1. runs all the same checks, exits 0 or non-zero with named errors.
  2. emits no message. Pure observation.
  Template prose: "before `shop-msg respond work_done`, run `bc-preflight work-done <id>`; if it fails, fix the underlying state and try again."

shop-msg respond stays pure messaging primitive in both cases. shop-templates package gains the new bc-emit or bc-preflight surface.

My lean: (a) WRAPPER COMMAND. Reasons:
- Atomic: agent can't accidentally skip the check; running the wrapper IS the action.
- Names the policy in the same place as the action (one command surface).
- Bare shop-msg respond stays available for forced-recovery (--force is the existing escape valve).
- (b) requires agent discipline to remember to run preflight; that's the same trust problem the prose-pinning had.

Replaces the original proposal's "shop-msg respond ... runs git checks" line throughout.

The retro-restructure of lead-cw7 (105-108) / lead-8lm (109-112) / lead-83l (113-116) into a single bc-emit scenario set still holds — but the new home is the shop-templates BC, pinning `bc-emit work-done` behavior, not shopsystem-messaging.

OPEN QUESTION: is lead-83l (just dispatched, queued at templates BC) still useful prose-pinning, or should it be cancelled in favor of authoring bc-emit scenarios? Architect judgment after current work_done arrives.
PDR-010 drafted; supersedes lead-o6tp's narrower 'pre-emit-checks-via-CLI' framing
2026-06-10 ADR-036 authored & accepted (adr/036-procedural-preconditions-are-cli-layer-judgment-behaviors-are-template-prose.md), ratifying this layering principle: mechanically-checkable procedural preconditions (clean-tree, work_id-commit-on-origin-main, scenario_hashes-match = scenarios 105-116) move to a CLI-layer 'bc-emit work-done' WRAPPER in shopsystem-templates (NOT shop-msg respond — honors your layer correction; shop-msg stays pure transport); judgment behaviors (continuous-action, idle-checklist, choice-suppression, inbound sufficiency) STAY template prose. Open question RESOLVED: lead-83l is LAND-THEN-RETIRE not cancel — cancel is moot (lead-83l already landed at templates origin/main 9e7138c, CLOSED); retire-after-wrapper-lands avoids an enforcement-gap window (ADR-036 D4). DEFERRED-DISPATCH PLAN (NOT done tonight per lead-yxsr BC flakiness): (1) PO authors bc-emit wrapper precondition scenarios; (2) Architect assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates once BC verified live; (3) retro-retire 105-116 to one-line pointer prose AFTER step 2 lands. Follow-up dispatch bead filed. Bead stays OPEN (BC code fix remains).
Follow-up dispatch bead: lead-m56e (blocked-by lead-yxsr).

title: [FIXTURE] fxhyg meta-test leaks shop_type=lead synthetic rows that out-sort shopsystem- in resolve_lead_shop()
description:
## Mechanism observation (surfaced in lead-767 clarify)

shopsystem-messaging's BC Implementer root-caused two AC6-pin redness items (respond_force_payload_wins scenario_hash:33663625b12f56fd; respond_force_scoped_per_triple scenario_hash:1fb957942f332206) by direct registry inspection:

The fixture-hygiene meta-test 'features/test_fixture_hygiene_production_registry.feature' (scenario_hash:27c7804d2392736c) seeds synthetic rows with shop_type='lead' and names 'fxhyg-<uuid>-shopsystem-product' / 'fxhyg-<uuid>-shopsystem product' pointing at /workspaces/shopsystem-product. Teardown leaks these rows.

storage.resolve_lead_shop() does 'ORDER BY name LIMIT 1' over shop_type='lead' rows. Because 'fxhyg-' sorts before 'shopsystem-', shop-msg respond routes --force writes to /workspaces/shopsystem-product while read-back/probes target the session lead root — surfacing as 'no inbox message found' / clarify row 'deleted'.

## Distinction from lead-rgk4

lead-rgk4 is the **session-lead shared-state** leak (one session-scoped lead root never per-test-cleaned, so any lead-inbox-readback scenario accumulates rows across tests). THIS bead is a DIFFERENT mechanism: a meta-test seeds **production-name proxies** as shop_type='lead' that out-sort the real session lead in resolve_lead_shop(). Both are fixture-hygiene, but the layers and fixes differ.

## Architect's resolution (per ADR philosophy — pin invariants at the boundary they protect)

The meta-test owns the leak. fxhyg fixture **must not** seed shop_type='lead' synthetic rows past its teardown. Two acceptable fixes (BC's choice):

(b1) Seed fxhyg lead proxies with shop_type other than 'lead' (e.g., 'lead-proxy', 'fixture-proxy').
(b2) Explicit teardown in the meta-test removes the synthetic rows.

Tightening resolve_lead_shop() against fxhyg-prefix leaks (the BC's option (a)) was rejected: it's defensive coding against test pollution at the wrong layer. resolve_lead_shop() is a domain primitive; its contract is 'return the single shop_type=lead row', not 'filter out test pollution'.

## Scope boundary vs lead-767

lead-767 (umbrella --force collision semantics) is sized as **respond --force capability**, not fixture hygiene. The two redness items the BC reported are correctly-authored pins whose redness is fixture pollution, not capability defect. lead-767 work_done acceptance does not block on full-suite green of these two pins; they are green in isolation and in the 14-test respond-domain subset.

## Acceptance

1. test_fixture_hygiene_production_registry.feature meta-test does not leak shop_type='lead' rows past teardown (either by not seeding them, or by explicit teardown).
2. After the fix, the respond-domain subset and full suite agree on AC6-(ii) and AC6-(iv) outcomes (both green).
3. resolve_lead_shop() contract is **not changed**; its 'ORDER BY name LIMIT 1' over shop_type='lead' rows is the documented behavior.

## Cross-links

- lead-767 (where this was surfaced; out of scope for that umbrella)
- lead-rgk4 (sibling fixture-hygiene gap, different mechanism)
- lead-0bw (related ephemeral postgres fixture work)
notes:
Dispatched 2026-05-29 as request_maintenance to shopsystem-messaging, work_id=lead-2122. Pre-state verified empirically on messaging main e4a03ef: _fxhyg_seed_production_row at conftest.py:5074 bypasses _register_shop cleanup; conftest.py:5710-5718 seeds two shop_type=lead synthetics (fxhyg-<uuid>-shopsystem-product, fxhyg-<uuid>-shopsystem product) under /workspaces/shopsystem-product; resolve_lead_shop() at storage.py:776 lexical-sorts fxhyg- ahead of shopsystem-. BC picks mechanism (DELETE-in-teardown, _register_shop routing, or non-lead shop_type). Awaiting work_done.
2026-05-29 work_done(blocked) RECONCILED: BC reported FIX COMPLETE AND VERIFIED but COMMIT BLOCKED on cross-work entanglement. Fix: _FXHYG_SEEDED_NAMES tracking set at _fxhyg_seed_production_row chokepoint + function-scoped autouse _fxhyg_registry_cleanup fixture that DELETEs every fxhyg synthetic row at each test teardown; resolve_lead_shop() body UNCHANGED (AC4 honored). Verified empirically (BC probe): AC1 (no fxhyg shop_type=lead rows post-teardown), AC2 (no fxhyg synthetic out-sorts real lead), AC3 (full pytest 111 passed including AC6 pins 33663625b12f56fd, 1fb957942f332206 and hygiene 27c7804d2392736c). Lead-side entanglement verification: read conftest diff (2986 lines); fxhyg block ~1638-2400 textually distinct from lead-767 --force block ~2793+, BUT fxhyg block depends on uncommitted lead-0bw ephemeral-DB infra (lines 13-150) and uncommitted lead-6nt _snapshot_production_name / _per_test_registry_restore (lines 169-260). storage.py adds allow_multi_type (lead-767), cli.py adds _walk_up_resolve_shop (lead-b3z) and existing_lead_inbox_message_type import. NO coherent green committable unit excludes lead-767. BC's 'leave main green' refusal was CORRECT under the contract. Status remains IN_PROGRESS pending lead-pfmb carrier push. Carrier scope updated to sequence BOTH lead-2ca and lead-2122 commits when lead-767 signs off. Outbox row consumed. NOT closing.
Carrier lead-xscs dispatched for lead-767; lead-2122 fxhyg registry fix lands on origin/main as a side-effect of the carrier commit (entangled in conftest.py).
close_reason: Carrier b2649b6 carries the _fxhyg_registry_cleanup fixture; reachable on origin/main; verified via git show diff filter.

title: [FIXTURE] shared session-lead root leaks lead-inbox state into newly-authored lead-inbox-readback scenarios (full-suite-only failures)
description:
## Mechanism observation from lead-767

During lead-767 implementation (shop-msg respond --force), the BC Implementer authored two new scenarios that read lead-inbox state back via 'shop-msg read inbox --lead' or direct lead-inbox query (criterion 6(ii) fidelity and 6(iv) per-triple scope). Both PASS in isolation and in the respond-domain subset (40/40 green), but FAIL under the full suite with 'no inbox message found for work_id=...' / 'clarify row ... was deleted' — even though the work_id is unique and the --force INSERT committed.

## Root mechanism

Same documented cross-test DB-state-leakage class flagged for the 4 pre-existing failures (see tests/conftest.py:725 and the '...delivers_the_response_to_the_lead_inbox_1' failure). Root cause:

- tests/conftest.py:246 — '_ensure_session_lead' is ONE session-scoped lead root that is never cleaned per-test.
- Every respond-domain step writes BC responses into that single shared (bc=session_lead, direction='inbox') namespace.
- Any scenario that WRITES then READS BACK lead-inbox rows is fragile once the table accumulates state across tests; isolation hides it.

## Why this is now load-bearing

Leakage no longer bites only legacy scenarios. It now catches CORRECT, newly-authored lead-inbox-readback scenarios. The next BC dispatch that adds a readback scenario in this domain will see a full-suite failure that reproduces only in aggregate and will be tempted to mis-diagnose it as a regression in their src change (it is not). The discriminator 'pass in isolation, fail in suite' must be applied each time — which is exactly the trap a fixture-hygiene fix would remove.

## Proposed direction (from BC, not done in lead-767)

Per-test isolation of the lead namespace for lead-inbox-readback scenarios:
- function-scoped finalizer that deletes (bc=session_lead, direction='inbox', work_id) for the work_ids the test used; OR
- per-test unique lead root for readback scenarios.

Distinct from the respond --force capability; sized as its own fixture-hygiene item.

## Evidence

- tests/conftest.py:246 (session-scoped lead root)
- tests/conftest.py:711 (no per-test cleanup)
- features/respond_force_payload_wins.feature:1 (one of the new readback scenarios)

## Cross-links

- lead-767 (umbrella request_bugfix where this was observed)
- lead-2id (child P1 — recovery path; the surface the readback scenarios exercise)
- lead-b3z (child P2 — error-message clarity)
- lead-0bw (related — prior fixture-hygiene work on ephemeral postgres for BDD; sibling concern in same conftest layer)

## Disposition

Filing as informational. Open follow-up; not blocking lead-767 acceptance (the new scenarios CORRECTLY pin the capability under isolation; suite-level brittleness is a fixture-hygiene gap, not a capability defect).
notes:
2026-05-29: work_done(blocked) reconciled.

FIX VERIFIED BY BC:
- AC2 implemented: function-scoped autouse fixture _per_test_session_lead_inbox_cleanup (conftest.py, after _per_test_registry_restore) deletes (bc=session_lead, direction=inbox) rows per test (dispatch mechanism (a)).
- AC3 honored: full suite 111 passed; both named scenarios green full-suite (test_respond_work_done_force_replacement_is_what_read_inbox_lead_returns [respond_force_payload_wins.feature] and test_respond_work_done_force_leaves_a_differentmessage_type_leadinbox_row_intact [respond_force_scoped_per_triple.feature]); legacy ..._delivers_the_response_to_the_lead_inbox_1 also green.

DIAGNOSTIC FINDING (worth keeping):
AC1 was ALREADY satisfied by lead-2122's uncommitted registry fix. Empirical contradiction-test by BC: original symptom's root cause was registry/name pollution (lead-2122 territory), not lead-inbox row accumulation. _fetch_lead_inbox_payload (conftest.py:379) keys by (bc,work_id,message_type), so work-id-keyed readbacks are insensitive to accumulation. AC2 remains real (count-based readbacks like given_two_bc_responses_unconsumed cannot tolerate accumulation; conftest.py:3868 routes around the session lead with a per-test prime_lead_root precisely because of this), and addressed by the new fixture.

COMMIT BLOCKED: Same entanglement as lead-2ca/lead-2122. conftest.py interleaves lead-rgk4 (_per_test_session_lead_inbox_cleanup), lead-2122 (fxhyg), lead-0bw (ephemeral DB hooks), lead-6nt (snapshot/restore), lead-767 (--force, NOT signed off) plus lead-2ca shared storage.py / cli.py hunks. lead-rgk4 NOT on origin/main HEAD (e4a03ef). Dirty set verified: M compose.yml, M src/catalog/schemas.py, M src/shop_msg/cli.py, M src/shop_msg/storage.py, M tests/conftest.py + untracked features/respond_force_*.feature etc. Hunks textually-distinct (cleanup fixture is its own block adjacent to _per_test_registry_restore) but cannot isolate a green committable unit excluding lead-767.

SEQUENCING: Folded into lead-pfmb carrier (now 3 items: lead-2ca + lead-2122 + lead-rgk4, all behind lead-767 sign-off).

STATUS: stays IN_PROGRESS until commit lands via lead-pfmb.

Outbox row consumed.
Carrier lead-xscs dispatched for lead-767; lead-rgk4 function-scoped autouse cleanup lands on origin/main as a side-effect of the carrier commit (entangled in conftest.py).
close_reason: Carrier b2649b6 carries the _per_test_session_lead_inbox_cleanup fixture; reachable on origin/main; verified via git show diff filter.

title: [bug] request_bugfix carrier: tighten lead-m32 backoff-values scenario per BC clarify (pin 1/2/4/8/16s budget contract)
description:
## Carrier dispatch resolving lead-m32 clarify

Resolves the architectural clarify from shopsystem-messaging against lead-m32: should the bounded-reconnect backoff VALUES (1/2/4/8/16s, ~31s budget) be pinned as a committed contract, or are they implementation detail behind the attempt count?

## Architectural answer: YES — pin the values

The clarify's analysis is correct on both counts:

1. The ~31s self-heal budget is named load-bearing in the lead-m32 contract rationale (transient drops self-heal without router-side re-arm within this window — point 2 of the hybrid decision). It is not accidental implementation; it is the half of the hybrid that justifies the bounded reconnect at all.

2. The current scenarios pin attempt COUNT (5 LISTEN_DROP lines) and exhaustion behavior (exit-2, stderr line) and no-re-drain, but the LISTEN_DROP step assertion matches only the 'LISTEN_DROP attempt=' prefix. A future refactor to flat 1s backoff (5s budget) or 5s-start backoff would pass all three current scenarios while silently shrinking the self-heal window the hybrid was designed to absorb.

Adopt the proposed tightening verbatim: add a Then step to the failure-path Scenario Outline (4dc858e1414bd682) asserting the five emitted LISTEN_DROP lines carry backoffs in order 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s.

## Acceptance criteria

- The failure-path Scenario Outline (currently @scenario_hash:4dc858e1414bd682) gains a Then step of the form:
      And the watcher LISTEN_DROP lines report backoffs 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s in order
  asserting the five LISTEN_DROP stdout lines emitted on the exhaustion path carry exactly 'backoff=1s', 'backoff=2s', 'backoff=4s', 'backoff=8s', 'backoff=16s' in that order.
- The new step MUST pass against the existing implementation without behavior change in storage.py:1013 / storage.py:1135 (the backoff values are already what the lead-m32 dispatch specified; this clarify is about pinning, not changing).
- The new step MUST be empirically non-vacuous: a mutation to e.g. a flat 1s backoff schedule MUST fail this step. Demonstrate the mutation-kill in the work_done narrative (same shape as the re-drain mutation kill the original lead-m32 work_done already demonstrated for no-re-drain).
- The retired hash 4dc858e1414bd682 is superseded by the new hash carrying the additional Then step; the BC's features/ directory tracks the new hash only.

## Pre-existing failures (preserved from lead-m32 work_done)

The 4 collision/routing failures the BC flagged are independently confirmed pre-existing (reproduce with the new feature file removed; pass in isolation = cross-test DB state leakage in lead-e9x routing tests). They are unrelated to this tightening and are NOT acceptance gates for this work_id. They are tracked separately and remain in scope of whatever beads owns the lead-e9x routing test fixture hygiene.

## Original lead-m32 scope (preserved)

The hybrid bounded-reconnect + exit-on-exhaustion contract from lead-m32 stands. Implementation is faithful (both watch fns share the reconnect loop; drain sits outside it so no-re-drain holds structurally per the BC's re-drain mutation kill). This carrier dispatches the additional Then step ONLY.

## @scenario_hash enumeration (pre-state)

Per the lead-architect template's @scenario_hash enumeration requirement, the BC's features/ directory must be re-grepped before this dispatch composes. The dispatch retires/supersedes hash 4dc858e1414bd682 specifically; other lead-m32 hashes (the two passing-path scenarios pinning READY+reconnect and no-re-drain) remain untouched. The dispatch text below names 4dc858e1414bd682 explicitly so the BC Implementer can confirm scope.

## Cross-link

Follow-up to lead-m32 (clarify resolution). Per lead-d6o protocol gap, clarify resolution requires a fresh carrier work_id; this is it.

## Dispatch vehicle

request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging (tightening the existing unpinned backoff-values behavior; capability and implementation already exist, the scenario simply doesn't pin the values).
notes: Carrier lead-xscs dispatched for lead-767; the lead-2ca commit is entangled in the same conftest.py/storage.py/cli.py bundle and lands on origin/main as a side-effect of the carrier commit.
close_reason: Carrier b2649b6 also carries lead-2ca's recommit; reachable on origin/main.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: Dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging. Carries the architectural answer to lead-m32 clarify + the tightened failure-path Scenario Outline. Pre-existing implementation already emits the 1/2/4/8/16s backoffs (storage.py _listen_backoff_seconds), so no source change expected — only scenario tightening. Mutation-kill required in work_done (flat-1s backoff causes new step to fail).

title: request_bugfix umbrella: shop-msg respond collision semantics (lead-2id + lead-b3z)
description:
Umbrella request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging covering two related defects in shop-msg respond collision semantics, both surfaced during lead-cw7 reconciliation.

CONCRETE OBSERVED FAILURE (lead-cw7):
- BC's bc-reviewer emitted a test work_done (summary='test', 2/4 scenario_hashes) into the lead inbox while exercising the precondition guard.
- BC tried to recover by 'shop-msg consume outbox' on its own outbox marker, then 're-emit' the real complete work_done (95 hashes, full adversarial-probe trail).
- 'consume outbox' only flipped the BC-side outbox marker to consumed=TRUE; the lead-inbox row (storage.py:846-860, insert_bc_response collision check) still triggered CollisionError on the second insert attempt.
- Net result: the real complete work_done was never delivered. The degraded test payload is what lead-cw7 reconciliation saw, producing downstream bead lead-plt.

CHILD CONCERN 1 — lead-2id (P1, recovery path):
shop-msg respond collision is unrecoverable from the BC side without direct DB manipulation (forbidden by template). BC has no clean way to amend, replace, or supersede a degraded/test/wrong-message-type response. consume_outbox is documented as freeing the slot in operator mental models but does not.

CHILD CONCERN 2 — lead-b3z (P2, defense-in-depth at CLI layer):
shop-msg respond work_done has no CLI-layer repeat-emit guard distinct from the lead-inbox collision check. The error message on collision ('refusing to overwrite existing response for work_id=X') does not name the existing row's existing message_type, status, or summary, so the BC has no way to tell whether the existing row is its own prior emit (legitimate re-emit attempt) or a different message altogether.

ARCHITECT'S PICK — candidate (a) from lead-2id: add explicit --force to shop-msg respond {clarify,work_done,mechanism_observation}.

RATIONALE for (a) over (b) and (c):
- (a) is additive — existing respond_*_collision.feature scenarios (35fece8e1f96e074, b6973413b7bfdd12, 7c7a6d58fa71a60c) keep pinning the default-refuse behavior; --force adds a new opt-in path.
- (b) would change consume_outbox semantics and require retiring or rewriting consume_outbox scenarios (edfe8c68149298fb, 40fb3c3e71063966, a2e50c164ead0531, abefd734c20344db) which currently treat consumption as a BC-side marker only.
- (c) introduces a new verb (respond amend) that overlaps confusingly with respond; --force is the conventional UNIX recovery affordance.

ACCEPTANCE on the messaging BC:
1. shop-msg respond {clarify,work_done,mechanism_observation} gains a --force flag that, when supplied, DELETEs any existing lead-inbox row with the same (bc, work_id, message_type) before INSERT, atomically (single transaction).
2. Without --force, all three commands continue to refuse on collision (existing scenarios 35fece8e1f96e074, b6973413b7bfdd12, 7c7a6d58fa71a60c remain green and authoritative).
3. The collision error message when --force is NOT supplied additionally names the existing row's message_type and indicates --force is available as an opt-in recovery path. This is the lead-b3z surface: BC sees a useful, actionable error.
4. New BDD scenarios pin: (i) --force on each of clarify/work_done/mechanism_observation succeeds when a prior row exists; (ii) the collision-without-force error message names the existing row's message_type and mentions --force.

CROSS-LINKS:
- lead-2id (child, P1, candidate-fix (a) picked)
- lead-b3z (child, P2, error-message-clarity surface)
- lead-cw7 (parent observation, reviewer-recovery mechanism)
- lead-plt (downstream emit-fidelity bead this collision enabled)

Implementer/reviewer pipeline will produce work_done; original lead-767 work_id closes via reconciliation when carrier work_done lands. title: [finding] bc-launcher must run claude as vscode user — --dangerously-skip-permissions refuses root
description:
Observed 2026-05-29 immediately after lead-5ig landed (bc-launcher 69e1ee3).

Setup: bc-container launch shopsystem-docs (no --startup-prompt; lead-5ig sequence engages).

Actual: tmux pane shows
'''
root ➜ /workspace (main) $ claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
--dangerously-skip-permissions cannot be used with root/sudo privileges for security reasons
root ➜ /workspace (main) $
'''

Launcher waited 60s for the "Accessing workspace:" marker which never appeared because claude exited instantly with the security refusal.

Root cause: the BC image runs as USER=root (lead-4tx fix routed HOME→/home/vscode for credential lookup, didn't change the running user). Claude Code refuses to drop permission checks when running as root — by design, since root has filesystem-level bypass anyway.

Fix: launcher must run `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` as the vscode user, NOT root. Three implementation options:

(a) tmux session itself as vscode: launcher's `tmux new-session -d -s agent` becomes `docker exec -u vscode bc-<bc> tmux new-session -d -s agent`. Most invasive but cleanest — the agent session is owned by vscode end-to-end. Requires /workspace to be writable by vscode (currently owned by root after git clone).

(b) su inside the tmux send-keys: tmux session stays as root, but the launcher sends `su vscode -c 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'` instead of bare `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`. Minimal change but the subprocess hierarchy is messy.

(c) docker exec -u vscode for the claude invocation only: launcher's `tmux send-keys 'claude'` becomes a docker exec -u vscode call that starts claude in a new tmux window/pane owned by vscode. Conflicts with the existing tmux model.

Recommended: (a). Side effect: also need to chown /workspace to vscode after git clone (or run the clone as vscode in the first place). The lead-4tx HOME=/home/vscode env already aligns with vscode home; this completes the picture by running the actual processes as vscode.

Live surface: post-lead-5ig launcher is non-functional end-to-end. Workaround: user starts claude manually inside tmux WITHOUT --dangerously-skip-permissions (the pre-lead-5ig manual path; functional but requires permission approvals).

File hints: src/bc_launcher/controller.py (tmux new-session call; git clone exec_run; claude send-keys; any chown /workspace step needed).
notes:
2026-05-29 update: empirical recovery attempt revealed an additional sub-defect not in original bd.

When manually starting `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` as vscode in the running container, Claude Code launched a fresh-user onboarding wizard (theme selection → login method → OAuth URL) instead of using the user's existing credentials. Root cause:

The launcher's `cp /tmp/host-gitconfig /home/vscode/.gitconfig` and `cp /home/vscode/.claude/.claude.json /home/vscode/.claude.json` exec_runs both run as root, producing root-owned files in vscode's home dir. When Claude Code starts as vscode, it cannot read these files (mode 0644 / no group read), so it treats the user as new and writes its own fresh /home/vscode/.claude.json (observed: original 23474 bytes overwritten with fresh 795-byte file at 2026-05-29T00:54).

The bind-mounted /home/vscode/.claude/ directory is correctly vscode-owned (host preserves ownership), so .credentials.json there IS accessible. But Claude Code reads /home/vscode/.claude.json (the root-of-home one), not /home/vscode/.claude/.claude.json. The cp puts content at the right path but with wrong ownership.

Two fix shapes for the cp step:
(a) After each cp, chown the destination to vscode:vscode.
(b) Run cp as vscode in the first place via docker exec -u vscode (requires the source /tmp/host-gitconfig and bind-mounted /home/vscode/.claude to be readable by vscode — currently both are).

Recommendation: (a). Minimal change; ensures destination ownership independent of cp source perms.

Adding to lead-d64's acceptance: after launch, `docker exec -u vscode bc-<bc> stat -c '%U' /home/vscode/.gitconfig /home/vscode/.claude.json` returns vscode for both.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] resolution carrier for lead-7v1 clarify: shop-msg watch LISTEN-drop contract = bounded reconnect then exit non-zero
description:
## Carrier dispatch resolving lead-7v1 clarify

Resolves the architectural question raised in shopsystem-messaging's clarify against lead-7v1: should the LISTEN-drop fix exit non-zero (a) or attempt bounded reconnect (b)?

## Architectural answer: HYBRID — bounded reconnect, then exit non-zero on exhaustion

The clarify correctly identified that (a) and (b) are materially different observable contracts. The right contract is the hybrid: keep transient drops self-healing without requiring a router-side re-arm, but make sustained outages loud (process death visible to the Monitor pipeline).

## Observable behavior contract

For BOTH watch_lead_inbox (storage.py:1013) and watch_inbox (storage.py:1135), symmetrically:

1. **Wrap the LISTEN loop in a try/except** that catches psycopg.OperationalError (and any underlying connection-loss signals psycopg surfaces: empty notifies() generator exhaustion with conn.broken True, IOError, etc. — collapse to one named handler).
2. **On drop detection**, emit ONE stdout line of the form:
       LISTEN_DROP attempt=<n>/<max> backoff=<seconds>s
   then sleep the backoff, then attempt to re-open the LISTEN connection (new psycopg.connect + LISTEN <channel>). On successful reconnect, emit:
       LISTEN_RECONNECTED
   and resume the notifies() loop.
3. **Bounded params**: max 5 attempts, exponential backoff starting at 1s (1, 2, 4, 8, 16 seconds — total ~31s before exhaustion).
4. **On exhaustion** (all 5 reconnect attempts failed): emit to STDERR a single line of the form:
       error: LISTEN watcher could not reconnect after 5 attempts; exiting
   and exit with code 2 (matches existing fail-fast convention used elsewhere in shop-msg for DB-unreachable).
5. **No drain on reconnect.** The post-reconnect path resumes at the bare notifies() loop only — it does NOT re-run the pre-LISTEN drain phase. Drain pollutes stdout with already-handled work_ids and breaks the Monitor's at-most-once-dispatch assumption. This is load-bearing: re-draining on reconnect would re-trigger router subagent dispatches for messages already processed.

## Acceptance criteria

- A test fixture that simulates connection drop mid-notifies() (e.g., terminating the backend connection from a second psql session, or monkeypatching psycopg.OperationalError into the generator) MUST produce: at least one 'LISTEN_DROP attempt=' stdout line, then either 'LISTEN_RECONNECTED' + resumed notifies, or 5 'LISTEN_DROP' lines followed by the stderr exit-named-failure line and exit code 2.
- The post-reconnect path MUST NOT print already-drained work_ids. Pinned by a fixture that pre-seeds an inbox row, observes 'READY' + the seeded row, simulates a drop, reconnects, and asserts the reconnected stdout does NOT re-print the seeded row.
- Symmetric coverage: both watch_lead_inbox and watch_inbox MUST pass the same shape of fixture (parameterized).

## Original lead-7v1 scope (preserved)

lead-7v1 originally reported: shop-msg watch silently continues on LISTEN connection drop with no output and no exit (psycopg3 notifies() generator silently starves). Pre-state confirmed via direct read of storage.py:1013 and 1135 — both still bare 'for notify in conn.notifies():' with no exception handler. The fix above closes that observable gap.

## Cross-link

Follow-up to lead-7v1 (BC clarify response). Carries the architectural decision + acceptance criteria + reconnect-no-re-drain constraint that the clarify could not have inferred.

## Dispatch vehicle

request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging (tightening existing unpinned LISTEN-loop behavior; the watch surface already exists, no new capability).
notes: 2026-05-29 (round 2): BC work_done arrived on lead-2ca carrier. Reviewer signed off, BUT did not commit due to lead-767 working-tree entanglement. Origin/main does not contain lead-2ca. Recommit carrier filed as lead-pfmb. lead-m32 remains IN_PROGRESS pending lead-pfmb push.
close_reason: Subsumed by carrier lead-xscs (b2649b6). lead-m32 backoff-values scenario landed via lead-2ca recommit in same commit; cascade-resolved.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: BC clarify resolved by carrier dispatch lead-2ca (request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging). Architectural answer: YES, pin the backoff values 1/2/4/8/16s — the ~31s self-heal budget is load-bearing in the hybrid contract rationale (not implementation detail) and an unpinned values field would let a refactor silently shrink the window. Adopted BC's proposed Then step verbatim against the failure-path Scenario Outline (hash 4dc858e1414bd682 → new canonical hash 07d405ae455c5553 per shop-msg's wrapped-form computation; description text references body-only hash 641bae76c069bf5b which is inert noise — load-bearing data on ScenarioPayload is correct). Pre-existing 4 collision/routing failures from lead-m32 work_done remain out of scope (cross-test DB leakage in lead-e9x routing tests). Clarify outbox row consumed.

title: [finding] bc-launcher must run claude --dangerously-skip-permissions AND use pre-trust readiness marker
description:
Observed 2026-05-29 after lead-3oi landed.

Setup: bc-container launch shopsystem-docs (no --startup-prompt, lead-3oi sequence engaged: send 'claude' → wait for marker 'Claude Code v' → ...).

Actual: launcher emitted 'warning: Claude Code did not become ready within 60s (marker "Claude Code v" not seen); startup prompt NOT injected'. Inspection of tmux pane shows Claude Code DID start but is sitting at the workspace-trust prompt:

'''
root ➜ /workspace (main) $ claude

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 Accessing workspace:
 /workspace
 Quick safety check: Is this a project you created or one you trust? ...
 ❯ 1. Yes, I trust this folder
   2. No, exit
 Enter to confirm · Esc to cancel
'''

Root cause: the 'Claude Code v' banner is only printed AFTER trust acceptance — but lead-3oi's sequence waits for that banner BEFORE sending Enter to accept trust. Chicken-and-egg.

Compound observation from user: Claude Code should run inside a container with permission bypass anyway — the container provides isolation that the prompts are meant to substitute for. Verbatim: "Claude should run with bypass permissions in docker to avoid getting hung up. It is in a container so the issue is reduced."

Combined fix (one bead, one dispatch):

(1) Launcher invokes Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions:
    Instead of send-keys 'claude' Enter, send-keys 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' Enter.

(2) Update readiness marker to one that appears BEFORE trust prompt (in case the flag doesn't skip trust):
    Candidates: 'Accessing workspace:' (line 1 of the trust prompt block) or 'Quick safety check'. Pick whichever is the most stable Claude Code output line that appears immediately after invocation.

(3) Re-evaluate the trust-accept Enter step in the sequence:
    If --dangerously-skip-permissions ALSO skips workspace trust, the Enter step is no-op. Otherwise, keep it but trigger after the pre-trust marker, not the banner.

Acceptance:
- bc-container launch <bc> with no --startup-prompt produces a tmux pane where Claude Code is at its main input prompt (the '❯' indicator), not at a trust prompt or a 'command not found' error.
- The default startup-prompt is injected into Claude Code's input and triggers the session-start sequence.
- 80+ existing pytest still pass; new tests cover the marker change.

Live surface: lead-3oi default startup-prompt currently fails to inject due to this marker mismatch. The previous workaround (user attaches and types 'claude' manually) doesn't even work without ALSO accepting trust manually.

File hints: src/bc_launcher/controller.py (CLAUDE_READY_MARKER constant, send-keys 'claude' invocation, trust-accept step in launch sequence).
notes:
Closed by lead-shop architect reconciliation 2026-05-29.

BC work_done received from shopsystem-bc-launcher; committed as 69e1ee3 to origin/main. All four expected changes verified against the commit:

(a) Step 1 invocation: 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' (controller.py:~380)
(b) CLAUDE_READY_MARKER changed POST-trust 'Claude Code v' -> PRE-trust 'Accessing workspace:' (controller.py:50)
(c) Step 3 (bare-Enter trust accept) RETAINED with empirical justification: --dangerously-skip-permissions does NOT bypass workspace trust (verified 2026-05-29 in devcontainer). Step 3 now correctly fires after PRE-trust marker.
(d) Tests added: test_claude_ready_marker_is_pre_trust_not_post_trust_banner, test_claude_input_ready_marker_is_unambiguously_post_trust, test_step_one_send_keys_includes_dangerously_skip_permissions_flag, test_step_three_bare_enter_trust_accept_is_retained. Full suite 84/84 green.

Bonus finding pinned by BC and surfaced as separate mechanism_observation (lead-5ig-obs-input-ready-marker-overlap): CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER changed from bare '❯' to 'bypass permissions on' because '❯' appears in PRE-trust pane as the trust-prompt selector arrow. See follow-up bead for the role-template guidance amendment proposed.

Working tree clean on origin/main.

title: [finding] bc-launcher injects --startup-prompt into bash, not Claude Code — agent never starts
description:
Observed 2026-05-29 immediately after lead-9sq landed (--startup-prompt default).

Setup: bc-container launch shopsystem-docs (no explicit --startup-prompt). Launcher resolved its new default to 'Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md: arm Monitor on shop-msg watch --bc shopsystem-docs, then drain pending inbox via shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-docs, then await user direction.' and injected it into tmux via send-keys.

Actual tmux pane after launch:
'''
DE.md: arm Monitor on shop-msg watch --bc shopsystem-docs, then drain pending in
box via shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-docs, then await user direction.
-bash: Run: command not found
root ➜ /workspace (main) $
'''

Root cause: controller.launch() creates a tmux session running bash (the default in 'tmux new-session -d -s agent'). The startup-prompt tmux send-keys then types the prompt text into bash, which interprets 'Run' as a command and fails. Claude Code is NEVER started inside the container. The session-start prompt mechanism is end-to-end broken.

Required behavior: the launcher must start Claude Code inside the tmux session BEFORE injecting the startup-prompt. Sequence should be:
1. tmux new-session -d -s agent (existing)
2. tmux send-keys 'claude' Enter
3. wait for Claude Code to be ready (poll tmux pane for prompt indicator? sleep?)
4. wait for trust prompt and auto-accept (claude requires 'Yes, I trust this folder' confirmation on first run)
5. THEN tmux send-keys <startup_prompt> Enter so it lands in Claude Code's input

Fix candidates:
(a) Launcher fix: after starting tmux, send 'claude' + Enter, sleep N seconds, send Enter to accept trust prompt, sleep M seconds, then send the startup prompt. Brittle (timing-dependent) but minimal.
(b) Launcher fix: same as (a) but poll tmux capture-pane until 'Claude Code' banner or specific marker appears before each next step. More robust.
(c) Image fix: bc-base image has a script that starts claude in agent session automatically; launcher's startup-prompt injection comes after. Requires devcontainer rebuild.

Recommendation: (b). Filed as child of lead-00b (parent finding) sibling to lead-9sq (which assumed the prompt would land in Claude Code).

LIVE SURFACE: lead-9sq's default --startup-prompt is currently non-functional; the docs BC sits with a bash prompt and no agent running on launch. Workaround: user must attach and type 'claude' manually (the pattern from yesterday's successful launch).
notes:
2026-05-29: Dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher.

Vehicle: request_bugfix — capability (startup-prompt injection) exists from lead-9sq but the integration seam (tmux session runs bash, send-keys lands in bash not Claude Code) is broken. No new scenarios; lead-9sq pinned acceptance remains the contract.

Pre-state empirically verified:
- shopsystem-bc-launcher origin/main HEAD = adbe100 (lead-9sq default). Clean working tree (only .specstory/ untracked).
- Reproduction (from bd description, confirmed): bc-container launch shopsystem-docs with no --startup-prompt produces tmux pane with '-bash: Run: command not found'. Claude Code never starts.

Dispatch description covers: integration seam diagnosis, required 5-step sequence (claude start → wait → trust accept → wait for input → inject prompt), recommended polling-based wait via tmux capture-pane (option (b) — Claude Code v banner + ❯ input marker, 60s timeout, stderr warning on timeout), acceptance (no bash error in pane; lead-9sq behaviors stay pinned; timeout produces clear warning), file hints (controller.py + driver.py polling helper), and lead-8lm precondition (commit to origin/main before work_done).

Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --work-id lead-3oi.
close_reason: Verified commit 7474795 on origin/main: (a) wait_for_pane_marker in driver.py Protocol + RealDockerDriver impl with polling tmux capture-pane; (b) CLAUDE_READY_MARKER='Claude Code v' and CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER='❯' constants in controller.py; (c) full sequence: send-keys 'claude' → wait ready → bare Enter (trust) → wait input → send-keys startup_prompt; (d) stderr warning lines naming the failing step on each timeout; (e) lead-9sq empty-prompt opt-out preserved. 80 tests pass (71 prior + 9 new in test_claude_code_startup_sequence.py). Working tree clean against origin/main.

title: request_bugfix umbrella: shopsystem-bc-launcher real-driver launch path defects (lead-1fd + lead-1bk + lead-4tx)
description:
Umbrella bead for the dispatched request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher that bundles three defects discovered 2026-05-28 while reaching end-to-end launch of shopsystem-docs from inside the lead-shop devcontainer. All three are gaps in real-driver coverage of capabilities the BC's existing features (bc_container.feature, bc_container_credentials.feature, bc_container_network.feature, bc_manifest.feature — 29 scenarios) already claim but exercise only under FakeDockerDriver. Vehicle: non-scenario request_bugfix.

Bundled defects (each filed separately, cross-linked as blocked-by):
  - lead-1fd: driver.run() omits CMD override -> detached bash exits before exec_run setup chain attaches. Fix: append ['sleep', 'infinity'] after image in cmd.
  - lead-1bk: credential mount sources are devcontainer-side paths Path.home() resolves to (/home/vscode/...) but host docker daemon cannot see those — needs /proc/self/mountinfo translation + optional BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME prefix substitution.
  - lead-4tx: image runs as USER=root; HOME defaults to /root; gh looks at /root/.config/gh and reports 'not logged in' despite credentials being mounted at /home/vscode/.config/gh. Fix: env['HOME'] = '/home/vscode'.

Working-tree fast-patches already in place on repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/ (load-bearing for the live docs BC launched 2026-05-28; do not revert). The canonical dispatch carries the patch summary forward so the BC Implementer commits to main.

Acceptance criteria for the dispatch:
  - Container stays alive >= 10s after 'docker run -d' (lead-1fd).
  - Mount sources passed to docker run resolve to host-visible absolute paths when launcher runs inside a devcontainer (lead-1bk).
  - gh auth status / git clone succeed inside the launched container without HOME workarounds (lead-4tx).

File hints embedded in the dispatch:
  - src/bc_launcher/driver.py:131 — sleep infinity append.
  - src/bc_launcher/controller.py:37-101 — _resolve_host_path helper.
  - src/bc_launcher/controller.py:218-224 — gitconfig source preference.
  - src/bc_launcher/controller.py:240-244 — HOME env.

NOTE on scenario coverage: the launcher BDD harness is vacuously green for these three concerns because tests inject FakeDockerDriver. lead-9r4 already names the broader 'driver-level shell-out is not integration-tested' assertion gap and is the natural follow-up for closing those scenario gaps; this umbrella does NOT try to pin sleep-infinity / mountinfo / HOME at the scenario level. Implementer commits patches → work_done.

DISPATCH STATE TBD — fill in this bead's notes after shop-msg send completes.
notes:
DISPATCHED 2026-05-28 via shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --work-id lead-3o0.
Vehicle: non-scenario request_bugfix (BC's existing features claim the capability; the three defects are real-driver-path gaps under FakeDockerDriver, not scenario-level pins).
Deposit verified: shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --work-id lead-3o0 returns 'valid request_bugfix from lead-3o0.yaml'.
Children (each --type blocks): lead-1fd, lead-1bk, lead-4tx.
Related (NOT bundled): lead-9r4 (broader real-driver assertion gap; out-of-scope follow-up).
Next: await BC work_done on lead-3o0; reconcile by confirming the three patches landed on shopsystem-bc-launcher origin/main per canonical bc-implementer work_done precondition.
close_reason: Canonical fix landed as d02f5b9 on shopsystem-bc-launcher origin/main covering all three defects + the gitconfig source preference. Working tree matches origin/main; 60/60 BDD pass; real-driver path empirically verified by today's docs BC launch + lead-gov round-trip. shop-msg consume outbox for lead-3o0 will be batched with the next lead-side drain.

title: [finding] bc-launcher must set HOME=/home/vscode in container env — image runs as root, credentials are vscode-owned
description:
Third launcher defect discovered while reaching launcher round-trip on 2026-05-28. After lead-1fd (CMD override) and lead-1bk (host-path resolution) were fast-patched, the container started cleanly and the credential mounts landed at the right host paths. But 'gh auth status' inside the container reported 'You are not logged into any GitHub hosts' despite hosts.yml being correctly present at /home/vscode/.config/gh/hosts.yml with a valid oauth_token.

Root cause: 'docker inspect ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest' returns USER=root. So 'docker exec' inside the container runs as uid=0, HOME=/root. gh looks at $HOME/.config/gh, finds nothing there, reports "not logged in". The hosts.yml at /home/vscode/.config/gh is invisible to root because $HOME points elsewhere.

Fast-patch applied at controller.py:165-170: add env['HOME'] = '/home/vscode' to the docker run env block, before SHOPMSG_DSN. Verified: after the patch, gh sees auth and 'git clone https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-docs.git /workspace' succeeds inside the launched container.

Two cleaner long-term fixes:
(a) Launcher sets HOME=/home/vscode (current fast-patch — minimal, works).
(b) Image sets USER=vscode and ensures /workspace is owned by vscode (canonical devcontainer pattern). Requires shopsystem-devcontainer rebuild + republish.

Recommendation: (a) is the bc-launcher canonical fix; (b) is a related shopsystem-devcontainer follow-up worth filing separately if vscode-as-default-user matters for other reasons (e.g., agent inside the BC running Claude Code shouldn't run as root).

Bundle with lead-1fd and lead-1bk as a single request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher.
notes: Bundled into umbrella lead-3o0 dispatched 2026-05-28 as non-scenario request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher. Fix already in working tree at controller.py:244 (env['HOME']='/home/vscode'). Awaiting BC commit + work_done.
close_reason: Canonical fix landed as d02f5b9 on shopsystem-bc-launcher origin/main covering all three defects + the gitconfig source preference. Working tree matches origin/main; 60/60 BDD pass; real-driver path empirically verified by today's docs BC launch + lead-gov round-trip. shop-msg consume outbox for lead-3o0 will be batched with the next lead-side drain.

title: [finding] bc-launcher mount paths must be host-resolved when run inside a devcontainer
description:
Discovered while trying to launch shopsystem-docs from the lead shop's devcontainer (2026-05-28). The launcher's controller.launch() resolves credential mount sources via Path.home() (controller.py:147), producing /home/vscode/.claude etc. — these paths are valid INSIDE the lead devcontainer but the docker daemon executing 'docker run' runs on the host, where those paths do not exist.

Concrete symptoms:
- bc-container launch shopsystem-docs --shopmsg-dsn '...' --network shopsystem succeeded in starting the container, but git clone inside failed with "could not read Username for 'https://github.com'".
- docker exec bc-shopsystem-docs ls /home/vscode/.config/gh → empty dir owned by root (docker auto-created the missing mount source).
- docker exec bc-shopsystem-docs gh auth status → "You are not logged into any GitHub hosts."

Why: this devcontainer mounts /dstengle/.claude → /home/vscode/.claude and /dstengle/.config/gh → /home/vscode/.config/gh via the devcontainer's own bind mounts (visible via /proc/self/mountinfo lines 16542 and 16546). The host has the real credentials at /dstengle/.claude etc.; the docker daemon needs THOSE paths as mount sources, not /home/vscode/* which doesn't exist on the host.

Fix: before passing mount sources to RealDockerDriver.run(), translate each devcontainer-side path to its host-visible source via /proc/self/mountinfo. For each candidate path, walk mountinfo lines (parts[3]=source-root, parts[4]=mount-point), find the longest mount-point prefix that contains the path, and substitute source-root for the prefix. Fall back to the original path if no bind mount covers it (i.e., we're not running inside a devcontainer).

Live surface: blocks 'bc-container launch' from any devcontainer that uses bind-mounted home dirs (which is the canonical layout for shopsystem-devcontainer-based environments — every BC launching from inside another BC's container is affected). lead-1fd unblocks the container starting; this bead unblocks credentials being usable inside the launched container.
notes: Bundled into umbrella lead-3o0 dispatched 2026-05-28 as non-scenario request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher. Fix already in working tree: _resolve_host_path helper at controller.py:37-101 + call sites at 256-258 + gitconfig source preference at 218-224. Awaiting BC commit + work_done.
close_reason: Canonical fix landed as d02f5b9 on shopsystem-bc-launcher origin/main covering all three defects + the gitconfig source preference. Working tree matches origin/main; 60/60 BDD pass; real-driver path empirically verified by today's docs BC launch + lead-gov round-trip. shop-msg consume outbox for lead-3o0 will be batched with the next lead-side drain.

title: [finding] bc-launcher driver.run() omits CMD override — container exits before exec_run setup steps can attach
description:
First end-to-end launch attempt of any BC (shopsystem-docs, 2026-05-28 post-lead-7gt) failed because the container immediately exited.

Root cause: repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/driver.py:118-132 builds 'docker run --name <n> -d -e ... --mount ... --network <n> <image>' with no command override and no -i/-t. The image ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest has CMD=[/bin/bash] ENTRYPOINT=[]; bash in detached mode without an attached TTY exits at once. The launcher's downstream exec_run() calls in controller.launch() (gitconfig copy, .claude.json copy, optional git clone, tmux new-session) then all fail against a dead container; observed manifestation today was 'container <hash> is not running' on the clone step.

Why this wasn't caught: BDD in repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/tests/ uses FakeDockerDriver. The driver-level shell-out is never integration-tested. lead-9r4 (already open) names a related assertion gap but not this lifecycle gap.

Two clean fix shapes:

(a) Launcher fix (canonical): driver.run() appends a long-running CMD like ['sleep', 'infinity'] (or 'tail -f /dev/null') when detach=True, so the container stays alive while controller.launch() does its exec_run setup chain. Then tmux session inside acts as the persistent process. This keeps the image minimal (a tools toolbox, not a daemon) and the launcher controls process lifecycle — matches the existing exec_run pattern.

(b) Image fix: ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest sets a long-running default CMD (e.g. 'sleep infinity', or a tini-based init). Requires devcontainer BC dispatch + republish.

Recommendation: (a) — one-liner in driver.run(), no image rebuild required, can be dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher with minimal scenarios. Image stays minimal.

LIVE SURFACE: blocks 'bc-container launch <any-bc>' until fixed. The entire launcher-transition critical path lead-xea → lead-jch → lead-7gt closed today is functionally NOT complete; the constant flip was correct but the run path is broken upstream of it.
notes: Bundled into umbrella lead-3o0 dispatched 2026-05-28 as non-scenario request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher. Fix already in working tree at driver.py:131. Awaiting BC commit + work_done.
close_reason: Canonical fix landed as d02f5b9 on shopsystem-bc-launcher origin/main covering all three defects + the gitconfig source preference. Working tree matches origin/main; 60/60 BDD pass; real-driver path empirically verified by today's docs BC launch + lead-gov round-trip. shop-msg consume outbox for lead-3o0 will be batched with the next lead-side drain.

title: lead-u16 reconciliation BLOCKED: shopsystem-docs BC emitted work_done with dirty tree (post-cw7-fix non-adoption)
description:
lead-u16 work_done arrived in lead inbox claiming canonical .claude/settings.json re-pour from shopsystem-templates@7ec5e75. Empirical verification on /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs:

  - Re-pour commit DID land: bfb52cb 'chore(lead-u16): re-pour .claude/settings.json from canonical bc.json' on main (1 commit ahead of origin/main — parent will push).
  - .claude/settings.json is byte-identical to canonical: 'bd prime' + bare 'shop-msg prime' (no --bc flag, no {{SHOP_NAME}} placeholder). Acceptance met for the deliverable.

HOWEVER the BC's working tree was DIRTY at the time of emit:
  - Modified (uncommitted): .claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md (+137 lines — appears to be a partial re-pour or local edit)
  - Untracked: .specstory/, features/, pyproject.toml, src/, tests/, walkthrough/

Per lead-cw7 (now CLOSED, fix landed in shopsystem-templates commit 3f74c52), the canonical bc-reviewer.md template SHOULD refuse to emit work_done when the working tree is dirty. The fact that shopsystem-docs emitted anyway means this BC has NOT picked up the new canonical bc-reviewer.md template — distinct from the original cw7 pattern (which was about absence of the precondition entirely).

RECONCILIATION DECISION: lead-u16 work_done IS the canonical fix on settings.json and the commit IS on the BC's main, so the core deliverable is met. But the dirty-emit pattern is a non-adoption finding for shopsystem-docs that needs to be surfaced. The +137-line modification to bc-reviewer.md is itself the canonical template re-pour the BC needs to pick up — possibly mid-edit. Recommended next action: separately dispatch the canonical bc-reviewer.md re-pour to shopsystem-docs (similar to the lead-u16 settings re-pour), and have the BC commit-or-revert its uncommitted bc-reviewer.md change.

Filed during lead-u16 reconciliation 2026-05-28.
close_reason: misframed — root cause is not cw7 non-adoption but cw7-only-covered-Reviewer-path. The Reviewer is not in the loop for request_maintenance work (only assign_scenarios); the dirty-tree emit on lead-u16 / lead-jch goes through the Implementer, whose work_done precondition is unfixed. Tracked under lead-8lm (Implementer-template symmetric coverage).

title: mechanism: shop-msg respond collision is unrecoverable — consume outbox does not free lead-inbox row for re-emit
description:
From mechanism_observation lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery (observed_during lead-cw7). The shopsystem-messaging BC's insert_bc_response collision-checks on the LEAD-INBOX row, while consume_outbox_message only flips consumed=TRUE on the BC-side OUTBOX marker (storage.py:444-466 vs 846-860). A BC that emits a degraded or test work_done (or wrong message_type) cannot amend or replace it; the only recovery is direct DB manipulation, which the BC template forbids. This is a one-shot trap that makes role discipline brittle in adversarial review.

CONCRETE EVIDENCE: lead-cw7 work_done payload landed with summary='test' and only 2/4 scenario_hashes during reviewer test invocation; the real complete response (95 hashes, full adversarial-probe trail, pre-emit verification evidence) was blocked by collision refusal. consume outbox did not free the slot. See lead-plt (the work_done emit-fidelity bead) for the downstream reconciliation problem this creates.

PROPOSED FIXES (any one; see lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery body for full rationale):
(a) Add --force to shop-msg respond {clarify,work_done,mechanism_observation} that DELETEs existing lead-inbox row before insert (gated; default safe).
(b) Make 'shop-msg consume outbox' also release the lead-inbox slot (single-source-of-truth: consumed responses are no longer authoritative; BC may re-emit).
(c) Add 'shop-msg respond amend' as a distinct verb.

ADDITIONALLY: bc-reviewer / bc-implementer templates should warn that ANY --status/--summary/--scenario-hash test invocation lands a permanent row.

Cross-links: lead-cw7 (parent), lead-plt (downstream emit-fidelity defect this enables).
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE: collision-recovery half shipped (respond --force, lead-767 @ b2649b6, help cites '(recovery path; lead-2id)'). Remaining direction-guard half is duplicate of lead-rl0f and folded there.
close_reason: Closed

The work_done payload accurately describes the worktree state but the work is NOT on main and the image is NOT published. AC #1, #2, #3 are met in worktree; AC #4 (image visible) cannot be met because nothing was pushed to trigger the workflow.

The BC's work_done summary itself notes 'AC #4 part-1 (committed to main) and part-2 (image observably present at ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest after a workflow run) remain on the lead/operator: BC role discipline does not push' — this articulates the exact dirty-tree non-adoption gap that lead-cw7 / lead-u16 / lead-b6x identify. That contradicts the canonical Implementer template (post-lead-cw7) which requires commit-on-origin/main as a pre-emit precondition.

title: PO authors bc-implementer.md work_done precondition scenarios (symmetric to lead-cw7 Reviewer coverage)
description:

The four scenarios dispatched in lead-cw7 (on-wire hashes 9457dfff7e3f9e90, 2b5d558d548b0606, 6d0a7a957b340274, 721dcf075edcd9c7) pin work_done pre-emit checks (clean working tree + work_id commit on origin/main) on bc-reviewer.md ONLY. title: mechanism: BC role template work_done precondition does not enforce 'committed to origin/main' before emit (3rd occurrence)
description:
REPEATING PATTERN observed across multiple BCs and round trips. The BC role template's work_done precondition checks that implementation is correct on the working tree but does NOT verify the change is committed (let alone pushed to origin/main) before allowing the BC to emit work_done. Result: Architect reconciliation discovers the work is on the working tree only, has to file a blocker bead, BC re-emits work_done after committing, lead has to re-reconcile. Multi-trip waste per dispatch.

OCCURRENCES TO DATE (2026-05-27 → 2026-05-28):
  1. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — first observation, captured by lead-b6x (2026-05-27 18:53Z).
  2. lead-33r / shopsystem-templates — same-day repeat on different BC, captured by lead-qwt (2026-05-27 ~21Z). Now closed: BC pushed commit 7ec5e75 and re-reconciliation succeeded.
  3. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — RE-FAILURE on the same bead 2026-05-28: BC re-emitted work_done a second time (visible in outbox), but HEAD on shopsystem-messaging is still b14b0ba (lead-e9x), no lead-0bw commit, conftest.py still modified +2211/-34 in working tree. lead-b6x remains the BC-specific blocker.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
The BC role templates (bc-implementer.md / bc-reviewer.md in shopsystem-templates) instruct the BC to verify scenario hashes, run BDD, summarize work — but do not list 'git log shows the lead-<id> commit on origin/main HEAD' as a precondition for emitting work_done via shop-msg respond work_done. The shop primer's session-close discipline ('Work is not done until git push succeeds') applies to lead-shop work but is not surfaced as a template-enforced check on the BC side.

CANDIDATE FIXES (PO/Architect to refine):
  (a) Add an explicit precondition to the BC Implementer template: 'Before composing work_done, run bb866b9 chore(bd): resubmit lead-33r; reopen lead-0bw; file lead-b6x and lead-9r4 and verify the lead-<id> commit is the latest (or present). If not, you have not finished work — commit and push first.'
  (b) Have shop-msg respond work_done check the BC's git state and refuse to send if HEAD is dirty or origin/main lacks an expected work_id-tagged commit. Heavier hammer; messaging BC must own the check.
  (c) Add a Reviewer-template gate: Reviewer refuses to approve work_done emit until the commit is on origin/main.

CROSS-LINKS:
  - lead-b6x (lead-0bw blocker, OPEN) — keep open; note today's re-failure.
  - lead-qwt (lead-33r blocker, CLOSED) — pattern captured.
  - This bead names the cross-BC pattern that lead-b6x and lead-qwt each captured as a single instance.

POSITIVE TEST FOR FIX: After role-template change, dispatch a fresh work item to a BC. Have the BC reach work_done emission without committing. The BC's tooling/template/Reviewer gate refuses the emit and surfaces 'commit + push first' as the required action.
notes: 2026-05-28 OCCURRENCE #5 (4th distinct BC): lead-jch / shopsystem-devcontainer emitted work_done with worktree-dirty and zero commits for lead-jch. HEAD = 6542ac4 (different work_id). All four files in worktree only; workflow on main still publishes old image name; ghcr image not published. Notably, the BC's work_done summary explicitly states it considers commit-and-push the lead's job — meaning canonical bc-implementer template adoption is still incomplete on shopsystem-devcontainer (parallels lead-u16 / shopsystem-docs which had not adopted the bc-reviewer commit-on-origin/main gate). lead-jch reconciliation BLOCKED; lead-7gt remains blocked transitively. See lead-jch notes for full evidence.
close_reason: Verified: all 4 scenarios (9457dfff7e3f9e90, 2b5d558d548b0606, 6d0a7a957b340274, 721dcf075edcd9c7) present in repos/shopsystem-templates/features/bc_reviewer_pre_emit_clean_tree_and_origin_main.feature with hash IDs matching dispatch; bc-reviewer.md template prose (137 lines) landed in commit 3f74c52 on shopsystem-templates main; BDD: 160 passed. NOTE: work_done payload itself has fidelity defect — summary='test' and only 2/4 scenario_hashes listed; filing follow-up.

title: BDD suite uses ephemeral postgres instance (not production shopsystem DB)
description:
Sibling to lead-6nt (registry surface). Today's Monitor event lead-dual-1 work_done from shopsystem-messaging revealed that BDD step defs in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/ invoke shop-msg respond using the canonical bc=shopsystem-messaging name (not the per-test tmp_path namespace), so the respond payload writes to the production lead inbox and broadcasts via LISTEN/NOTIFY.

POLLUTION CONFIRMED (lead inbox 2026-05-27): 16 orphan rows for synthetic work_ids — lead-dual-1, lead-901/902/903, lead-200, lead-301/302, lead-500/501/502, lead-col1/col2/col3, lead-001. None correspond to real beads. All map to feature files in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/ (consume_outbox, cwd_implicit_shop_resolution, pending_and_read_inbox, respond_*, send_request_maintenance*, watch).

WHY NOT FOLDED INTO LEAD-6NT
Scenarios 39-44 (queued under lead-6nt) constrain their assertions to shop_registry rows. The inbox/outbox surface is a different table with its own save/restore boundary. Critically, simply rewriting step defs to use tmp_path-namespaced bc args is NOT a valid fix: scenarios 25, 38, and others EXPLICITLY assert lead-inbox routing behavior with canonical names — that routing IS the contract under test.

CANDIDATE STRATEGIES (for PO to refine into scenarios):
  (a) Session-scoped save/restore of inbox/outbox rows for production canonical names the BDD suite touches (parallel to scenario 43's design for shop_registry).
  (b) Work_id prefix discipline (e.g., reserve a 'test-' prefix that the lead's pending views filter; rewrite fixture work_ids to use it).
  (c) Dedicated test-scope SHOPMSG_DSN distinct from production (most isolating; biggest refactor).
  (d) Per-test cleanup: at function-scoped teardown, delete inbox/outbox rows the test created via shop-msg respond/send (requires identifying them — possibly via a tag, possibly by snapshot/diff).

POSITIVE TEST FOR THE FIX: After a BDD session completes, shop-msg pending inbox --lead shopsystem-product returns no row whose work_id is not present in 'bd list' (i.e., is not a real bead).

ATTEMPTED CLEANUP TODAY
shop-msg consume outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-dual-1 --message-type work_done failed because the canonical name shopsystem-messaging currently resolves via shop_registry to a pytest-212 tmp_path — i.e., lead-6nt's registry corruption is also active. The two beads share the same root cause class but address distinct surfaces; lead-6nt repairs the registry, this bead drains the inbox/outbox.

OUT OF SCOPE
  - Editing repos/shopsystem-messaging directly (lead shop is hands-off code).
  - Dispatching a request_maintenance now — PO should first decide the strategy, then author scenarios to be dispatched under a normal lead-architect vehicle.

title: mechanism observation (lead-i18): test fixtures register production canonical names without save/restore
description:
BC shopsystem-messaging flagged in lead-i18 work_done summary that the test-suite step helper _register_shop (tests/conftest.py:3975) calls registry_add() against the postgres shop_registry using production canonical names (e.g., 'shopsystem-docs', 'unregistered-bc', 'shopsystem-product') derived from feature-file Given steps. The session-scoped fixture only saves/restores 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product'; it does NOT save/restore 'shopsystem-docs' or any other production BC name a future scenario may use.

LOAD-BEARING: The cwd_implicit_shop_resolution feature (just landed via lead-i18) registers 'shopsystem-docs' pointing at a tmp_path during the test run. If the test session crashes between registry_add and registry_remove, or if the cleanup path is bypassed, the production registry row for 'shopsystem-docs' is left pointing at a defunct pytest tmp_path. CWD-implicit shop-msg invocations from the real repos/shopsystem-docs/ working tree would then resolve via the corrupted registry entry. The mechanism (CWD-implicit resolution) is the very thing whose hygiene this fixture pattern threatens.

VERIFICATION ANCHORS (in repos/shopsystem-messaging):
- tests/conftest.py:3975 _register_shop -- direct registry_add, no save
- tests/conftest.py:157-174 _ensure_session_lead -- only 'shopsystem-product' / 'shopsystem product' saved
- features/cwd_implicit_shop_resolution.feature lines 6, 16, 54, 75 -- production canonical names appearing in Given steps

RECOMMENDED FOLLOW-UP (defer dispatch):
1. Extend _SAVED_PRODUCTION_ENTRIES initialization to cover every canonical name that step defs may pass to _register_shop, OR
2. Refactor _register_shop to use disambiguated test-only names (uuid suffix) when the scenario does not specifically require a production name, OR
3. Add a per-test cleanup (autouse function-scoped fixture) that snapshots & restores every name _register_shop touches during that test.

Likely vehicle: request_maintenance (flat refactor of test hygiene, no new scenarios) to shopsystem-messaging. Defer until either (a) a related scenario lands that would amplify the risk, or (b) an actual corruption event occurs.

ANCHORS:
- Source: lead-i18 work_done summary (consumed via shop-msg read outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-i18)
- Related: lead-tsj (postgres state-loss event) -- same registry, different failure mode
- Architecture: PDR-009 (CWD-implicit resolution; the resolution mechanism whose hygiene is at risk)
notes:
2026-05-27 ARCHITECT RECONCILIATION (work_done received and verified).

ADR-010 subset check: PASS. All 6 expected hashes
(2460440854300728, acd9e1c74ea1744a, 6dcbb68f89d527ec, 27c7804d2392736c,
420caad777af2152, e4263ccdca3b7a17) are pinned in
repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/test_fixture_hygiene_production_registry.feature
(untracked in BC working tree; BC has not committed yet — see follow-up note).

Implementation strategy: SAVE/RESTORE on production tables (not ephemeral
postgres). Verified by inspecting tests/conftest.py:
  - _SAVED_PRODUCTION_ENTRIES (line 99) — dict-based session snapshot
  - _per_test_registry_restore (line 224) — function-scoped autouse fixture
  - _registry_lookup(ignore_test_paths=True) (line 122) — narrow /pytest-
    filter for orphan-row self-heal
  - session_lead_shop teardown iterates the full _SAVED_PRODUCTION_ENTRIES
    dict (not just the lead aliases)

Implication for lead-0bw: lead-0bw is NOT trivially satisfied by lead-6nt's
work. The two address distinct surfaces (registry vs inbox/outbox); lead-0bw
remains independently load-bearing.

Suite verification (canonical DSN postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/shopsystem):
  - With patch: 89 passed / 10 failed (matches BC's reported count)
  - On main (git stash):     42 passed / 35 failed
  - The patch turns 47 tests green (25 of the 35 pre-existing failures are
    closed; 4 of those are the lead-6nt scenarios themselves; ~20 are
    collateral cleanup-discipline benefits)
  - All 6 lead-6nt fixture-hygiene scenarios PASS individually
  - Remaining 10 failures pre-exist on main; root cause is stale messages-table
    rows for hardcoded work_ids (lead-901/902/903/dual-1) — the lead-0bw
    surface, not lead-6nt's registry surface

Concrete leak-stopped check:
  - Production canonical names in shop-msg registry: ALL CLEAN, point at
    real /workspaces paths (shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-product,
    shopsystem-docs, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-devcontainer,
    shopsystem-bc-launcher all correct).
  - 333 tmp_path rows remain, but ALL under non-production names

  - 97 fxhyg-* synthetic rows persist with /workspaces paths (not

  - Lead-inbox pending fixture work_ids (lead-901..903, lead-dual-1,

    STILL PRESENT. lead-6nt did NOT drain these; that's lead-0bw's surface.

Mechanism observation received: lead-6nt-mech-1.
  - Subject: meta-tests for production-name hygiene introduce a parallel

  - Filed as follow-up bead: lead-497 (P2, open).

BC WORKING TREE NOTE: BC has not committed lead-6nt's work yet. The feature
file (features/test_fixture_hygiene_production_registry.feature) and
