title: Reconcile lead-km9: BC delivered only the replacement scenario (lead-b34) — 4 of 5 original scenarios still unpinned in shopsystem-docs
notes:
Reconciliation finding from lead-b34 work_done (2026-05-27).

ORIGINAL DISPATCH (lead-km9, 2026-05-22): assign_scenarios carrying 5 scenarios to shopsystem-docs:
  - 7ac28115fe1a2a33 (entry doc)
  - b0fe0666bcaa2398 (end-to-end runtime — superseded by lead-b34)
  - 6055501e8a23a652 (plain markdown only)
  - 8c55526c7a83bcd0 (agent-consumable walkthrough structure)
  - 3874a7f3c34e4f60 (launcher gap honestly named)

BC's clarify on lead-km9 explained that b0fe0666 was not establishable in its pytest-bdd harness, but said nothing about the other four being blocked. PO + Architect dispatched lead-b34 carrying only the b0fe0666 replacement; the other four were expected to land via the original lead-km9 dispatch.

OBSERVED STATE (BC repo /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs):
  - features/ contains exactly ONE file: adoption_walkthrough_terminal_section.feature, pinning 56babfaeec4514a3 only.
  - grep '@scenario_hash' in BC features/ returns only 56babfaeec4514a3.
  - BC work_done echoes the 4 other hashes, but they are not in the BC's features/ tree.
  - lead-km9 has no recorded work_done in the BC outbox (shop-msg read outbox for lead-km9 returns only the BC's clarify).

INTERPRETATION: per ADR-009 vehicle (b), the BC was expected to emit ONE consolidated work_done covering 56babfaeec4514a3 (the lead-b34 replacement) plus the four unchanged originals — addressed to the lead-b34 work_id. The BC did echo all five hashes in scenario_hashes:, but in fact only landed the replacement; the four others were not implemented. The work_done's hash list misrepresents BC state.

POSSIBLE CAUSES:
  1. BC understood the dispatch as ONLY covering the replacement (lead-b34 description framed it as additive — 'carries only the revised scenario'). BC may have inferred that the other four would be tracked via a separate work_done on lead-km9, and is still in flight on those.
  2. BC mechanically copied the hash list from the lead-b34 dispatch payload into work_done.scenario_hashes without verifying each hash had a corresponding feature file. (ADR-009 v2 candidate: tighten the BC-side requirement that scenario_hashes in work_done must be observably pinned in features/.)

FOLLOW-UP OPTIONS:
  (a) Send request_shop_card to shopsystem-docs to read its authoritative scenario register — confirms what BC believes it has pinned.
  (b) If BC is genuinely still in flight on the four originals, leave lead-km9 open and wait.
  (c) If BC considers lead-km9 closed (because lead-b34 work_done echoed all hashes), the four originals need to be re-dispatched. Architect to decide vehicle (likely a fresh request_bugfix or assign_scenarios depending on whether BC has any partial work).

ALSO: BC's working tree has uncommitted untracked files (features/, src/, tests/, walkthrough/) — orthogonal hygiene concern.

ADR-009 v2 FRICTION: this is the first BC work_done landing against the canonized vehicle-(b) workaround. The friction point: the BC's work_done.scenario_hashes echoed the entire original dispatch's hash list when only one was actually pinned. The pattern as canonized does not unambiguously tell the BC which hashes it must observably pin to declare work_done — particularly when the bugfix dispatch is additive vs. replacement. Candidate for ADR-009 v2: spell out the BC's work_done scenario_hashes contract explicitly (must be a strict subset of BC's pinned @scenario_hash set in features/), and clarify whether work_done on a clarify-resolution bead is allowed to claim coverage of unchanged sibling hashes from the original dispatch.
close_reason:
Superseded by lead-eqx (re-dispatch of the four unpinned scenarios via assign_scenarios, dispatched 2026-05-27) and ADR-010 (canonizes the work_done.scenario_hashes strict-subset rule that prevents the lead-b34 echo pattern in future).

lead-apk's tracking role is closed; ongoing reconciliation responsibility moves to lead-eqx. lead-km9 remains open per ADR-010 consequences (expected closure path: superseded by lead-eqx once its work_done lands).

Template follow-up filed as lead-83l (P2).

title: request_bugfix: shopsystem-templates — canonical settings.json hooks must not embed shop identity
description: The canonical .claude/settings.json templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json must not require shop-identity substitution. Today the BC variant ships 'shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}'; bootstrap pours it byte-for-byte (cli.py:423), the placeholder is never substituted, and the Claude Code harness executes the literal command — producing 'shop name {{SHOP_NAME}} is not registered'. This bug is currently latent for every BC that hasn't had a Claude Code session opened in its own repo dir; surfaced when starting shopsystem-docs. After B-MESSAGING (implicit CWD resolution) lands, the canonical hook becomes bare 'shop-msg prime' for both shop types. Scenarios to author: (1) canonical bc.json hook command is exactly 'shop-msg prime' with no addressing flags and no placeholders; (2) canonical lead.json hook command is exactly 'shop-msg prime' (currently 'bd prime' — verify whether bd prime stays alongside or is replaced; PO judgment); (3) a poured BC's .claude/settings.json contains no {{...}} placeholders; (4) repour via 'shop-templates update' replaces the old hook content. Discriminator: capability exists (canonical bootstrap pours settings.json) but the hook-content tightening is unpinned. Vehicle: request_bugfix. Anchor scenarios that need re-examination: 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 / cad9ccb5b462978d (byte-for-byte pour — these remain correct; the new scenarios sit alongside them, pinning the body). Depends on: B1 (shop-msg implicit lookup must exist first).
notes: RECONCILIATION FAILED 2026-05-27: BC emitted valid work_done with correct scenario hashes and retirement, AND working-tree post-state of bc.json/lead.json/features is correct, BUT none of it is committed or on origin/main (HEAD == origin/main == c4716c0). Same failure mode as lead-0bw earlier today. Blocked on lead-qwt: BC must commit and push. Downstream re-pour beads (lead-0u2 / lead-yhr / lead-u16 / lead-8yl) remain blocked.
close_reason: Reconciled 2026-05-28: shopsystem-templates commit 7ec5e75 ('feat(lead-33r): pin PDR-009 canonical SessionStart hooks (bd prime + bare shop-msg prime)') lands the BC work on main. All 5 scenario hashes verified present in repos/shopsystem-templates/features/canonical_settings_hooks_no_shop_identity_pdr009.feature (0c157533eb3145c8 / 9317b34e56712c7c / d3cc63377ac86cce / e74510bc2af8f058 / ef68be19d7b3a3bb), and 71797e9017c95fed retired in brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature with the supersession note. Commit content matches the work_done summary (bc.json + lead.json + features + retirement). Downstream re-pour beads (lead-0u2 / lead-yhr / lead-u16 / lead-8yl) now unblocked.

title: assign_scenarios: shopsystem-messaging — shop-msg implicit CWD-based shop resolution
description: Add implicit shop resolution to shop-msg: when called without --bc/--lead, walk up from CWD looking for .claude/shop/name.md and .claude/shop/type.md. Read type.md to choose BC vs lead mode; read name.md to resolve the canonical name; use the registry to find the path as today. Explicit --bc <name> / --lead <name> remain unchanged for cross-shop addressing. This eliminates the need for the canonical BC settings.json SessionStart hook to embed the shop name. Anchors: PDR-007 (name-based addressing — this extends it with a CWD-implicit form, does not reverse it). Prior art: bd discovers .beads/ from CWD using the same walk-up pattern. Discriminator: NEW capability — implicit CWD resolution does not exist today; calling shop-msg prime with no addressing flags currently errors via the mutually-exclusive arg group. Vehicle: assign_scenarios. PO must author scenarios pinning: (a) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a BC when CWD is inside a BC shop dir, (b) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a lead when CWD is inside a lead shop dir, (c) error case when CWD is not inside any shop, (d) explicit --bc/--lead still works unchanged. Suggested generalization to other subcommands (pending, respond, watch) — PO judgment whether to bundle.
notes:
RECONCILIATION COMPLETE 2026-05-27.

Item 1 investigation (second Monitor event): the second `lead-i18 work_done` event was NOT a redelivery. Two distinct rows landed in the lead inbox for the same work_id under cross-type multi-emit (allow_multi_type=True; pinned via lead-bhp scenario hash 39566e8d2d7768e4):

  - id=689: message_type=work_done (status=complete, scenario_hashes [9/9] match dispatched hashes)

Both rows confirmed in shop-msg dump --direction inbox; both keyed at the lead path /workspaces/shopsystem-product (correct, post-registry-repair).

Note on outbox: id=688 (mech_obs) and id=690 (work_done) outbox rows are keyed at the corrupted tmp_path bc=/tmp/pytest-of-vscode/pytest-199/test_an_explicit_bc_name_or_le0/shop_shopsystem-messaging_other. These are orphaned because the registry no longer maps that path to any canonical name. They no longer surface in `pending outbox --lead`. Not consumable via `shop-msg consume outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging` because the canonical-name -> path lookup now points elsewhere. Logged as garbage DB rows; harmless to operations.

All 9 dispatched scenario hashes (2d9704fcf6181cf9, 79c5b97fde233351, bf89761a1a0b3254, 0eabd60b92f570c5, 490432bb7431ed7d, 305c6fc26c7f1bfe, 2e0dd03be908e0fe, 4bd7aaf110961822, 6492effd22a6d3e7) match the work_done's reported set exactly AND are pinned in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/cwd_implicit_shop_resolution.feature.

Item 2 ops: registry repaired (shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-docs repointed; bc-alpha leak removed). See lead-6nt for the full repair record and follow-up plan.

CLOSING. lead-33r unblocked.

title: Bootstrap prerequisite: create shopsystem-docs BC repo + scaffold + register
description: Before any assign_scenarios dispatch for brief 007 (adoption documentation) can be sent to the new shopsystem-docs BC, the BC repo must exist, be bootstrapped with shop-templates, cloned into repos/shopsystem-docs/, and registered with shop-msg. Anchors: brief 007 (resolved Q4 commits new-BC topology; boundary 10 commits v1 plain-markdown publishing), ADR-008 (introduces the docs BC; same precedent as ADR-004's lead-mxy bootstrap blocker). Host-side steps (Decision A: host-operator pattern, same as lead-mxy): (1) gh repo create dstengle/shopsystem-docs --private (matches the existing org convention; shopsystem-bc-launcher is PRIVATE per gh repo view). (2) clone the empty repo into repos/shopsystem-docs/. (3) shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name shopsystem-docs --target /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs. (4) first commit + push the poured scaffold. (5) shop-msg registry add shopsystem-docs /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs and verify via shop-msg registry list. title: Lead subagent templates missing Claude Code YAML frontmatter
description:
The canonical templates for lead-po.md and lead-architect.md (in repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/) lack the YAML frontmatter (---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n[tools: ...]\n---) that Claude Code requires to register a markdown file as an invokable subagent. As a result, when shop-templates bootstrap pours a lead shop, the resulting .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md are present on disk but unregistered: the runtime reports 'Agent type "lead-po" not found' (and likewise for lead-architect) when the router attempts to dispatch.

Empirical demonstration: from /workspaces/shopsystem-product, an Agent invocation with subagent_type=lead-po failed with the registration error above; the available-agents list contained only the runtime defaults (claude-code-guide, Explore, general-purpose, Plan, statusline-setup) and none of the lead subagents.

This blocks the entire lead-shop role-isolation pattern documented in lead-primer.md §'Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents.' The router cannot delegate PO or architect work, which means briefs/PDRs/scenarios cannot be PO-authored and request_bugfix/assign_scenarios/request_maintenance dispatches cannot be architect-composed.

The BC subagent templates in the same directory (bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md) DO carry the required frontmatter — they are the pattern to match. The fix is to add an analogous frontmatter block to lead-po.md and lead-architect.md whose name/description fields accurately describe each lead-shop role's invocation criteria.

This bug was likely introduced in commit 88ca83c ('agents: lead-po and lead-architect subagents (PDR-002 path a)').

NOTE on dispatch protocol: this request_bugfix is being filed router-direct rather than architect-composed because the architect subagent is itself one of the unreachable subagents — composing the dispatch through the proper vehicle requires the very capability this bugfix restores. Router-direct is the one-off needed to bootstrap the architect role itself; future dispatches return to architect-composed once shopsystem-templates ships the fix and we re-pour.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27: shopsystem-templates work_done verified. .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md frontmatter (name/description/tools) confirmed well-formed and matching the canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-{po,architect}.md. Re-pour already applied locally via commit 15037cf. No further re-pour to other shops needed (sibling BC repos don't use lead subagents). Outbox row already consumed. No scenarios, no register check; bug fix is doc-shape only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: messaging test suite clobbers production registry on every run
description: The shopsystem-messaging pytest suite overwrites production shop_registry entries on every run. In tests/conftest.py the session-scoped _ensure_session_lead fixture (lines 87-114) calls registry_add('shopsystem-product', str(root.resolve()), shop_type='lead') and registry_add('shopsystem product', str(root.resolve()), shop_type='lead') with a tmp_path as the shop_root. registry_add uses ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE (storage.py lines 630-640), so these calls overwrite existing production entries pointing to /workspaces/shopsystem-product. After any test run, shop-msg registry list shows 'shopsystem product' and 'shopsystem-product' pointing at /tmp/pytest-of-vscode/pytest-NNN/... paths. The teardown at line 128 only removes the random test-lead-session-* entry; the two named entries are never cleaned up. Similarly, the step-def at line 3439 (given_bc_registered) registers any bc_name passed in from a feature file under a tmp_path with no teardown. Operational effect: shop-msg pending outbox --lead 'shopsystem product' silently returns empty; resolve_lead_shop() routes BC responses to a tmp path. Recovery requires manually re-running shop-msg registry add after every test run. Fix: the test suite must not upsert well-known production names. Either (a) use a separate database or schema for tests (SHOPMSG_DSN already supports this via env var), (b) save and restore the original registry values around the session fixture, or (c) add a finalizer that removes the two named entries and re-inserts the original values. Source: tests/conftest.py lines 87-128 and 3439-3448; storage.py registry_add lines 622-642.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27: shopsystem-messaging work_done verified. Pre-existing 48 failures (subprocess PATH gap, unrelated). Probes all green. Production registry names shopsystem-product/shopsystem product correctly restored after suite run. Outbox row already consumed prior to this reconciliation session. Mechanism observation: synthetic-name fixtures (bc-alpha/bc-beta/ghost-lead) lack finalizers — filed lead-vvz P3.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg pending inbox --bc returns empty despite unconsumed rows
description: shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name> resolves the BC path via resolve_shop_name() (storage.py:732-747) and then runs query_pending_inbox(bc_root) (storage.py:277-302). That query filters by bc = <resolved_path>. When the registry entry for the BC name has been overwritten by test fixtures (Bug D), resolve_shop_name() returns a tmp path and the query finds zero matching rows even though the real inbox row exists under the production path. Additionally, query_pending_inbox uses NOT EXISTS (outbox row) as the pending predicate, but the messages table also has a consumed column; if consumed semantics are used on the inbox side in future this predicate diverges. Observed: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher returned empty while DB row (id=7, bc=/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher, work_id=lead-1wv, direction=inbox, consumed=False) existed. Note: lead-yaj covers only the outbox direction (pending outbox --lead filtering); this is the separate inbox direction bug. Source: src/shop_msg/storage.py lines 277-302 and src/shop_msg/cli.py lines 168-182 (_resolve_bc) and 585-596 (_cmd_pending_inbox).
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg pending outbox --lead returns empty despite unconsumed rows in DB
description: shop-msg pending outbox --lead <name> calls query_pending_outbox(lead_root) in storage.py lines 305-361. The query fetches ALL outbox rows with consumed=FALSE (lines 338-346), then Python-filters for rows whose bc column starts with lead_root + '/repos/' (lines 356-359). The filter fails when the BC's registered shop_root in the registry does not start with that prefix — for example, when insert_bc_response writes the BC-side outbox marker using the path stored in the registry, and that path is the canonical filesystem path to the BC clone. If the path stored in the registry differs from <lead_root>/repos/<bc-name> (e.g., an absolute path outside that tree), all rows are filtered out silently. Observed: three unconsumed rows (lead-80i, lead-9d3, lead-dzv) were present in the DB with consumed=False; shop-msg read outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id> read them correctly; shop-msg pending outbox --lead returned empty. Fix: query_pending_outbox must either (a) join against shop_registry to enumerate BCs registered under the lead, or (b) use the registry path to correctly scope the bc-column filter.
close_reason: Cannot reproduce 2026-05-29. Empirical check: 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product' just returned 'lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery mechanism_observation shopsystem-templates' — non-empty, with the expected canonical-name + work_id + message_type triple. The filter-mismatch bug described (DB has unconsumed rows but pending outbox returns empty) is NOT triggering today. The original observation cited rows lead-80i, lead-9d3, lead-dzv as visible via 'shop-msg read outbox' but absent from 'pending outbox' — those rows have since been drained and the current code path is producing rows correctly. Likely root cause: bc-column filter scoping was corrected by a separate commit (probably as a side effect of one of the post-2026-05-21 shop-msg fixes — pending outbox refactor land sequence not enumerated here), OR the registry-path canonicalization that triggered the filter mismatch is no longer in effect on the current data set. If the bug recurs, file a fresh bead with the new observation; do not reopen this one. Related-discoverability note in COMMENTS (unified 'pending --lead' command merging inbox and outbox) is preserved in bd record and is independently worth its own bead if the router-mental-model concern resurfaces.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: Reconciliation observation 2026-05-22: Monitor stream at session start flushed 'lead-1t2 clarify' but 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead' did not list it. Investigation: lead-1t2 is in 'pending inbox --lead' (a BC->lead clarify routed to lead inbox), NOT in 'pending outbox --lead' (which tracks BC outbox rows). The two commands surface different message-flow directions, which is consistent with the data model — but the router's mental model 'lead-1t2 should be in pending outbox' suggests a follow-up bead may be warranted: a unified 'pending --lead' that merges inbox-clarifies-from-bcs and outbox-responses-from-bcs into one list, so the operator does not have to remember to run two commands. This is orthogonal to lead-yaj's outbox-filter-scoping bug (lead_root + /repos/), filed here as a related-discoverability note.

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg watch silently continues on LISTEN connection drop without exiting
description: shop-msg watch --lead holds a psycopg3 autocommit connection and blocks in conn.notifies() (storage.py:watch_lead_inbox lines 1013-1029 and watch_inbox lines 1135-1156). If the TCP connection to Postgres drops mid-session, psycopg3 does not raise immediately — the notifies() generator silently starves with no output and no exit. There is no exception handler around the LISTEN loop, no timeout, no keepalive-expiry detection, and no reconnection logic. The watcher appears running (process alive, no stderr) but delivers no notifications. Recovery requires manual monitor restart. Fix: detect connection drop (OperationalError or generator exhaustion) and either exit non-zero with a clear stderr message, or attempt bounded reconnection with per-attempt stdout logging.
notes: 2026-05-29: chain status — lead-7v1 -> lead-m32 -> lead-2ca (BC signed off, NOT on origin/main, entangled with lead-767) -> lead-pfmb (recommit carrier filed). lead-7v1 remains IN_PROGRESS pending lead-pfmb push.
close_reason: Subsumed by carrier lead-xscs (b2649b6). Original LISTEN-drop fix arrives via lead-m32->lead-2ca chain, all carried in b2649b6 on origin/main.

title: [bug] request_maintenance: tighten scenario 72 instruction-substring step-defs to sentence-scoped containment (lead-pn9 §4.4 closure)
description:
§4.4 closure on lead-pn9. The Reviewer signed off 5/6 scenarios (71, 73, 74, 75, 76) but flagged scenario 72 (d87ccb133fa64d2f — 'canonical primer activation instruction names prereqs and failure mode') as having loose step-defs in tests/conftest.py:

- then_body_directs_path_verification (L5451-5479): whole-body 3-token conjunction (verify-verb OR 'command -v') AND ('path' substring) AND ('before' substring + 'monitor' substring), with no proximity/sentence binding.
- then_body_directs_refuse_with_diagnostic (L5487-5530): same whole-body conjunction shape (refuse_verb + diagnostic_marker + missing_marker), no sentence binding.

Reviewer demonstrated false-positive: bare-stripped primer body 'Before you read further: if inotifywait and stdbuf are missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a diagnostic.' still passes because (a) 'sufficiency check' elsewhere supplies has_verify_verb via 'check', (b) 'PATH' satisfies has_path_token via the host-prereq bullet ('packages being present on PATH'), (c) 'before' anywhere + 'monitor' anywhere satisfies has_monitor_temporal.

Fix shape per Reviewer: mirror scenario 71's exclusion-clause step-def at L5398-5439 (sentence-aware regex scan over sentence-broken body, requires the activation-verb + hook-pattern coupling within a single sentence). Tighten both L5451-5479 and L5487-5530 to require their full token-conjunction within a single sentence.

Gherkin text is FINE. Canonical primer is FINE. Only the step-defs are too permissive.

Vehicle: request_maintenance — flat test-infrastructure tightening; no new scenarios; existing scenario 72 pinned behavior unchanged.

Parent: lead-pn9. This dispatch's terminal work_done implicitly closes lead-pn9's chain.

title: brief-003 realization revision: replace SessionStart-hook activation with CLAUDE.md-primer instruction (router arms Monitor at session start)
description: Test-harness BC diagnosed a hook-model mismatch in brief 003's canonical templates: Claude Code awaits SessionStart hooks synchronously, so a foreground 'inotifywait -m' invocation never returns and startup hangs / times out. Schema + host prereqs are fine; the mismatch is structural — synchronous hook delivery is incompatible with a long-running watcher. Brief 003's two invariants (every shop reactive on session start; activation honors brief 001 encapsulation) still hold. The realization changes: in place of a SessionStart hook running inotifywait, the canonical CLAUDE.md primer carries an explicit instruction telling the router (Claude session) to arm the Monitor tool on a 'stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to <watch-dir>/' pipeline at session start. Monitor IS the in-session primitive that delivers the 'background process whose stdout streams as notifications' semantic the hook was faking. This bead carries the revision: PO authors superseding scenarios (numbered 71+, tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates) that retire the hook-model-bound scenarios (57/58/59/65/68 wholesale; 70 keeps as schema-conformance with shrunk scope) and pin the new realization (CLAUDE.md primer per shop type contains activation-instruction substring; instruction names host prereqs + failure mode; bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md contains the instruction). Architect handles the request_bugfix dispatch carrying this round.
notes:
PO authored 6 new scenarios at features/templates/71-76 (Scenario Outlines where shop-type-parametrized, single Scenarios where shop-type-specific). Tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates at dispatch time by Architect. Hash computation deferred to Architect per role contract.

Retire-with-successor map:
- 57 (d4b8aac19b34ea8c, BC-side 1621b59b0ea8b20b) → superseded by 73 (bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md carries activation instruction); settings.json package-data surface survives but is no longer the activation home.
- 58 (2a09d13c21d48aa0, BC-side 679d227f04533ad4) → superseded by 71 (canonical CLAUDE.md primer contains activation-instruction substring).
- 59 (21769e2a22c36bc7, BC-side c1e7f31eeef73e05) → superseded by 75 (canonical lead settings.json declares 'bd prime' as sole SessionStart inner-hook, no activation hook).
- 65 (a9379ab3a162158d, BC-side a9379ab3a162158d) → superseded by 72 (activation instruction names prereqs + refuse-with-diagnostic failure mode).
- 68 (a3338bb773fda65e, BC-side 287e6a4f31533336) → superseded by 74 (canonical bc settings.json carries no SessionStart entries; subsumes 68's 'no other-shop-type watch target' clause).
- 70 (f2a7de49d80332c1, BC-side f2a7de49d80332c1) → superseded by 76 (schema conformance conditional on SessionStart being present; bc-variant antecedent is vacuously satisfied).

Keep unchanged: 60, 61, 62 (bootstrap/update mechanics for settings.json — still apply byte-for-byte to the new canonical contents pinned by 74 and 75); 63, 64 (CLAUDE.md primer + bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md name host prereqs — substrings inotify-tools/coreutils/inotifywait/stdbuf still required by router's Monitor invocation, motivation now consistent with 72); 66, 67 (editable-install discipline, unaffected); 53 (CLAUDE.md references bd discipline, parallel structural pin to 73 for activation).

PO stance committed on the open question (canonical bc.json shape): option (b) — bc.json ships as a minimal hooks wrapper with no SessionStart entries. Rationale: preserves managed-update workflow (scenarios 60/61/62 still apply byte-for-byte to the new content); leaves the slot available for future terminate-cleanly BC-side hooks without committing speculative content now; avoids the destructive cost of retiring 60/61/62 wholesale (option a) and avoids the speculative-content failure mode of shipping hooks before a use case exists (option c). lead.json ships with 'bd prime' as the sole SessionStart inner-hook (the prior two-hook composition under scenario 59 collapses to a one-hook shape since the activation hook is gone).

No intent gaps surfaced during authoring. The realization revision is internally consistent: 71+72 pin the new realization home (CLAUDE.md primer), 73 pins it surviving the bootstrap pour, 74+75 pin the now-cleared-out settings.json shape per shop type, 76 carries forward the schema-conformance invariant under the new constraint. Architect handles request_bugfix dispatch carrying this round; the dispatch description should instruct the Implementer to retire the 6 listed BC-side scenarios when landing 71-76, mirror the supersedes-via-Feature-description-block convention established in scenario 69.
close_reason: Brief-003 realization revision complete. New canonical realization: CLAUDE.md-primer-instructs-router-to-arm-Monitor (in place of SessionStart-hook-runs-inotifywait). Step-defs pinning scenario 72 are now sentence-scoped with markdown-soft-wrap normalization, immune to the false-positive Reviewer demonstrated. title: request_bugfix: brief 003 implementation (12 scenarios 57-68, post-supersession re-dispatch)
description:
Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 scenarios (57-68) to shopsystem-templates, carried as request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation (BC already has bootstrap capability; brief 003 extends it).

BACKGROUND
==========
The lead-xkh chain hit a coordination gap. Sequence:

- lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios 57-68 for brief 003: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json, bootstrap extension) → BC clarified on contradiction with prior-pinned scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin in bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature).
- lead-5mz (request_bugfix, carried only scenario 69 — the supersession removing scenario 51's settings.json clause, leaving AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) → BC work_done complete, scenario 1a6e90189f9c2ade attested, supersession landed clean.

But: lead-5mz carried ONLY scenario 69. Brief 003's 12 positive pins (.claude/settings.json writing, prereqs naming, venv-sync, etc.) were never delivered to the BC. lead-xkh still has a clarify in its outbox, so the BC's router will not re-process it. To get brief 003 implemented, the lead must send a NEW dispatch carrying the 12 brief 003 scenarios.

This is a protocol gap shape: the §4.4 closure assumption that lead-5mz's terminal work_done closes lead-xkh's chain doesn't hold because lead-5mz only carried the supersession scenario, not the brief 003 positive pins. Prior chains (lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e) worked because each follow-up bundled the prior scenarios. The lead-5mz dispatch instruction was wrong on this — it should have bundled.

LINKAGE
=======
- Predecessors: lead-xkh (assign_scenarios for brief 003, clarify-stalled in BC outbox), lead-5mz (closed; clean supersession of scenario 51).
- lead-xkh remains in_progress with a pointer to this new bead. lead-xkh closes implicitly when this new work_id's terminal work_done arrives in the BC outbox.

SUPERSESSION STATE
==================
- Prior scenario 51 (hash 7d64bb8ed5a3f656, compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin) is retired. Its settings.json clause was removed by lead-5mz.

============================

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (previously prepared, surfaced here since assign_scenarios was retired in favour of request_bugfix and request_bugfix's description IS the carrier for procedural framing)
=========================================================================================================================================================================================

- Scope item E realization framing: scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under repos/ on session start). Scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv). The two compose: 67 is one valid realization of 66, but 66 is the durable contract the Reviewer attests against, and 67 names the specific mechanism the canonical templates encode today. If a future realization replaces editable installs with a different live-source resolution (e.g., a path-aware shim), 66 remains intact and 67 gets superseded.

- Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait): the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait is missing on PATH; silently degrading to a no-watcher state is the failure mode this scenario pins against. The canonical settings.json should encode this property via the hook's startup command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary), not via a separate runtime probe.

- Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition): the lead-shop's canonical settings.json composes both the inotifywait activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart. Concretely the SessionStart entry has multiple hook commands, not a single replaced command. This is so the activation hook does NOT clobber bd prime (operational hygiene).

================================

- Verified BC does NOT pin .claude/settings.json today: grep '.claude/settings.json' across BC features/ produces no hits after lead-5mz landed. So the specific extension brief 003 names is unpinned — Q2 (does any scenario currently pin it?) is NO. Tightening, not flat change.
- Therefore: request_bugfix is the correct vehicle.

title: scenarios 57-68: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)
description:
Brief 003 (lead-0h1) — Event-driven shop activation. 12 scenarios extending the
shop-templates bootstrap surface to ship a canonical `.claude/settings.json`
per shop type plus host-prereq naming, and to install sibling BC clones
editable from the lead bootstrap.

The brief carries two invariants and five scope items:

- Invariant 1: every shop is reactive on session start (BC watches own
  `inbox/`; lead watches `repos/*/outbox/`).
- Invariant 2: activation honors brief 001's encapsulation — the
  watcher fires a wake signal; reading content still goes through
  `shop-msg`.
- Scope A: canonical `.claude/settings.json` per shop type (scenarios 57, 68).
- Scope B: SessionStart hook content with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait,
  alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged 2>&1 for startup-chatter
  visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming (scenarios 58, 59, 65).
- Scope C: bootstrap writes settings.json; update re-pours it as a
  bootstrap-managed file (scenarios 60, 61, 62).
- Scope D: prereq naming (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`,
  `stdbuf`) in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` and the canonical primer
  (scenarios 63, 64).
- Scope E: live-source resolution outcome + lead bootstrap installs
  sibling BC clones editable into the product venv (scenarios 66, 67).

## Empirically validated context

This session validated the stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipeline end-to-end
on lead-79q reconciliation: sub-second event-fire on `work_done`
arrival via the reference pipeline named in scope item B. The pattern
works. Scope items A/B/C/D codify the canonical shape so every
bootstrapped shop gets it by default.

## Pre-state cited

Pre-state verification on shopsystem-templates current state:

- Package data at `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files
  (`bc-implementer.md`, `bc-reviewer.md`, `lead-po.md`, `lead-architect.md`)
  + 2 CLAUDE.md primers under `templates/claude/` (`bc.md`, `lead.md`) +
  `gitignore.template`. No `.claude/settings.json` template exists.
- `src/shop_templates/cli.py`: bootstrap writes `.claude/agents/*.md`,
  top-level `CLAUDE.md` (from primer + `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution),
  top-level `.gitignore`, and invokes `bd init --skip-agents`. No
  `.claude/settings.json` is written. `update` re-pours managed
  agent files only — no settings.json handling.
- Empirical bootstrap of a probe directory
  (`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe
  --target /tmp/probe-003`) produced exactly: `CLAUDE.md`,
  `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md`, `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`.
  No `.claude/settings.json`. Exit 0.
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` scan: no existing scenarios
  pin SessionStart hooks, settings.json content, Monitor harness,
  inotifywait, or venv install mode.

All 12 scenarios target net-new capability — `assign_scenarios` is the
correct vehicle.

## Implementation notes (informational; bodies are the contract)

- Scope item E is realization-pinned by the PO via two scenarios:
  scenario 66 pins the outcome (live-source resolution on session
  start) and scenario 67 pins the install-shape (lead bootstrap
  installs sibling clones editable into the product venv). Scenario 67
  alone is the PO's leaning shape; the Implementer is free to add a
  hook-time refresh on top if pre-state on E surfaces drift cases not
  covered by 67 alone, or to treat 67 as sufficient.

- Scenario 65 pins the "loud fail on missing `inotifywait`" property.
  The Implementer must select a hook shape such that a host with no
  `inotifywait` on PATH surfaces a visible diagnostic — not a silent
  no-watcher state. A polling fallback that silently activates
  violates the invariant; if the Implementer commits a fallback, it
  must itself be a visible notification.

- Scenario 59 specifies the canonical lead variant **composes** with the
  existing `bd prime` `SessionStart` hook — two distinct entries under
  `SessionStart`, not a replacement.

- Scope D's prereq naming surface is the Implementer's call between
  `CLAUDE.md` primer (named in scenarios 63 + 64 as the realization)
  vs other places. The scenarios commit primer + bootstrap-generated
  CLAUDE.md as the surface; the Implementer must satisfy both.

## Procedural reminders

- Hash discipline: per `lead-wgv`, verify computed hashes against
  wire form, not against the body source pre-CLI. The `shop-msg send`
  CLI computes hashes from canonicalized wire form; the Implementer's
  BDD harness must read from the same wire form so verification holds.

- Mailbox encapsulation: `shop-msg` is the only sanctioned mailbox
  boundary on both sides. Do not hand-edit mailbox YAML; do not `cat`
  it. If `shop-msg pending` fails on a stale venv, fix the install —
  do not bypass the CLI (per `lead-xq0`).

- Bead ID: `lead-xkh` is the lead-shop work_id for this assignment.
  No dotted suffix; non-dotted lead-shop bead IDs per `lead-4wy`.

title: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: scenario 46 closes lead-bwu clarify (per-step shop-msg read outbox in lead-architect 'Responding to BC clarify' subsection)
description:

Parent: lead-bwu (request_bugfix carrying 9 scenarios for brief lead-231 items B+D). Grandparent: lead-231 (brief 001).

Trigger: lead-bwu's clarify (BC Implementer/Reviewer in shopsystem-templates) flagged an asymmetry between lead-architect.md line 272 (bare 'Read the clarify' opening step in 'Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' subsection — no shop-msg read outbox cite on the same step) and lead-po.md lines 160-162 (explicit 'Read the BC's clarify via shop-msg read outbox --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>' form). Scenario 56b3ef5f48342b9b (= features/templates/20) pins template-wide invariants but does not pin per-step CLI naming inside procedural subsections.

Router scope ruling: in scope of brief lead-231 items B+D. Invariant 1 of brief 001 ('shop-msg is sole inter-shop messaging surface; consumers never touch storage directly') is per-step discipline; a Constraints clause is too weak to prevent regression.

Vehicle: request_bugfix. Discriminator:
- Q1 (capability exists?): YES. Empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-architect.md line 272 and lead-po.md lines 160-162; the asymmetry exists exactly as the clarify describes. Lead-architect 'Sending a message' subsection lines 261-266 already names shop-msg read inbox explicitly, demonstrating the BC template's authors knew the explicit form — the omission in the Responding subsection is a gap, not a pattern.
- Q2 (pinned?): NO at the procedural-step granularity inside the Responding subsection.
- Q3 (behavioral or flat?): behavioral — tightens existing unpinned discipline. Behavioral → request_bugfix.

Carries: features/templates/46-lead-architect-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin (PO-authored; tightened beyond the BC's draft to add a third Then condition pinning that path-shape references like 'outbox/', '<BC root>/outbox/', 'the outbox file' also cannot appear without naming the CLI on the same step).

§4.4 closure signaling: when shopsystem-templates emits work_done on THIS work_id, that work_done implicitly sign-offs the original 9 scenarios dispatched under lead-bwu (per shop-system §4.4 loop closure shape). notes:
2026-05-12 (PO subagent, brief 001 items B+D, round 3 follow-up) — BC clarify on lead-e4g (request_bugfix carrying scenario 46) confirmed the regression vulnerability is present in three sister subsections that scenario 46 does not pin: lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' (line 158), bc-implementer '## Your job' (line 26), bc-reviewer '## What you read' (line 15). The Implementer's textual FIX extended across all four templates correctly, but coverage only pinned one. Without parallel scenarios, future edits to the other three subsections could regress to bare-verb 'Read the inbox YAML' (or similar) and only the template-wide encapsulation scenarios (6adb97ba0d0498c5, ff97230bf1e36a81, f279d66b8313e3fc) would gate them — and the BC's clarify text correctly notes those are too weak for per-step discipline (a bare 'Read' step coexists with 'shop-msg read inbox' elsewhere in the same template, satisfying template-wide substring presence while leaving the procedural step ambiguous).

Scope decision (router): option (a) accepted — three parallel scenarios mirroring 46's shape. Not (b) (BC's textual fix needs scenario coverage to be load-bearing). Not (c) (tightening the template-wide encapsulation scenarios with per-step granularity would lose the presence/absence checks they currently make; per-subsection scenarios are the cleaner shape). Outline-form consolidation deferred — scenario 46 is already dispatched + hashed; a brief revision can fold all four into a Scenario Outline if the pattern continues to recur.

Authored: features/templates/47-lead-po-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/48-bc-implementer-your-job-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/49-bc-reviewer-what-you-read-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin. Vary only by template name, subsection heading text, and target substring (read outbox vs. read inbox).

Pre-state verification of BC's sketch: subsection headings as sketched (verified by grep). Two asymmetries noted but kept out of scope of this dispatch:
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 1 (line 28) names BOTH 'shop-msg read inbox' (reading) AND 'shop-msg pending inbox' (listing). The new scenario 48 pins only the read-inbox action, parallel to scenario 46's narrow target. The pending-inbox-listing action is already covered template-wide by scenarios 17/18 (uses-shop-msg-cli-for-mailbox-access). Same shape applies to bc-reviewer 'What you read' step 1 (lines 17-20) — scenario 49 pins only read-inbox.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' is H2 (## Your job); lead-po's target is H3 (### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI); bc-reviewer's target is H2 (## What you read); lead-architect's target (scenario 46) is H3. Scenario 46's locator clause 'ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###)' handles both depths uniformly; new scenarios reuse the same clause verbatim. Consistent.

Round-4 outlook (flag): With round 3 closing the four-template per-subsection-CLI-naming regression mode, the remaining brief-001 B+D regression-mode surface is:
- Procedural CLI naming for the OTHER mailbox actions inside the same/adjacent procedural subsections. Specifically: writing the outbox response. lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify'; lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4 cites the same. But no scenario currently pins per-step CLI naming for the RESPOND action. Same regression mode: a future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only the template-wide scenarios would gate it. If round 4 is desired, the same Scenario-Outline-amenable shape applies across all four templates' write-outbox / write-inbox actions.
- Procedural CLI naming for 'shop-msg respond work_done' (Implementer/Reviewer) and 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' (Implementer). bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 names 'shop-msg respond work_done'; 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section names 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation'; bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' names both 'shop-msg respond work_done' and 'shop-msg respond clarify'. None of these are pinned by per-step scenarios.

Recommendation: defer round 4 to a deliberate brief revision rather than chasing it dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline — and the cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly. Until then, the four parallel scenarios are correct and load-bearing.

Did NOT dispatch. Did NOT compose shop-msg send. Architect opens a request_bugfix dispatch bead and sends the three new scenarios as additive to lead-e4g's coverage. (Open question for Architect: dispatch as a separate work_id, or amend lead-e4g? The BC's clarify is open against lead-e4g; the cleanest closure is one new request_bugfix work_id whose description references lead-e4g's clarify as the trigger.)

title: no first-class lead->BC 'answer a clarify' vehicle — shop-msg respond is BC->lead only; lead must re-dispatch (heavyweight) or nudge (transmission-only, not consumed)
description:
MECHANISM FINDING (surfaced answering shopsystem-bc-launcher's clarify on lead-uol6, 2026-06-12).

EMPIRICAL CLI BEHAVIOR:
- shop-msg respond REFUSES a lead-side caller: 'respond' is BC->lead only (BC answers a lead, or emits work_done/clarify TO the lead). The lead has no 'respond clarify' path to answer a BC's clarify.
- The only lead->BC paths that exist are: (a) RE-DISPATCH a fresh work_id carrying the refined directive (heavyweight: new bead, full request_bugfix/assign_scenarios, supersedes the prior work_id); (b) NUDGE (ADR-015) — transmission-only, general (not bound to a work_id), and may never be consumed by the BC's gated loop.
- In lead-uol6 we used both: a nudge was sent first (best-effort), then the authoritative answer was re-dispatched as lead-53y0 (the refined WS-1.3).

QUESTION FOR THE FRAMEWORK:
Is re-dispatch-with-refined-scope the INTENDED clarify-answer mechanism — i.e. the lead REFINES (authors a sharper directive) and never 'chats' a free-text answer back to the BC — making 'no lead->BC clarify-response' a deliberate design property? OR is a first-class lead->BC clarify-response message type MISSING from the §5.3 message-type catalogue (BC asks, lead answers in-band on the same work_id, BC's gated loop consumes the answer and proceeds without a new bead)?

Tension: re-dispatch is correct when the clarify reveals genuinely underspecified scope (lead-uol6 case — the answer changed the contract). But for a pure information clarify ('which env var?', 'does X exist?') re-dispatch is heavyweight and nudge is unreliable (not guaranteed consumed). A bounded in-band lead->BC answer would close that gap.

RELATES: §5.3 message-type catalogue / lead-vqg9; ADR-015 (nudge).
ROUTE NOTE: if a first-class vehicle is wanted, it is a messaging-BC capability -> assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-messaging (BC has no such message type today; new behavior). If the answer is 're-dispatch IS the intended mechanism', this resolves as a doctrine/§5.3 note, no code.

title: architect pre-state discipline: verify 'constant X consumed by function Y' against Y's BODY, not just X's presence (lead-rfc5 caught a dead-constant mis-statement)
description: From lead-rfc5 mechanism_observation (2026-06-12): the dispatch PRE-STATE asserted BC_NAME_RE (manifest.py:34) was 'consumed by validate() at :170' and thus gated the CLI; in fact validate() never referenced it — the constant was DEAD CODE, only asserted in tests/conftest.py. So the change was net-new behavior dressed as a tightening. Outcome was fine (BC wired the check into validate() with revert-teeth binding the wired call site), but a less-careful implementer could have parameterized the dead constant and shipped a no-op. DISCIPLINE: when an architect pre-state claims 'constant consumed by function F', confirm by reading F's body before classifying the surface as pinned-existing vs net-new; ensure revert-teeth bind the actual call site. Pairs with lead-n64d (recompute pre-state from real source, not local/assumed).

TWO recurring, load-bearing risks (neither blocked lead-8z3f): (A) STALE GLOBAL CLI: subprocess-based BDD steps resolve 'scenarios' from PATH (/usr/local/bin/scenarios), a stale install lacking the 'journal' subcommand the repo src/scenarios/cli.py already ships -> 3 spurious red failures (journal_query x2, journal_rebuild) on EVERY review of this BC; risks masking a real regression. Fix surface: pin test env to repo-current entrypoint (editable install or 'python -m scenarios') instead of whatever 'scenarios' is on PATH. (B) DIRTY CANONICAL-MANAGED FILES: .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md, .claude/skills/bc-router/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/writing-plans-bdd/SKILL.md show locally-modified though canonical-managed (poured by shop-templates update, 'do not edit in place') -> trip the work-done-gate clean-tree check for any future work_id, forcing every reviewer to hand-separate work artifacts from drifted-pour noise. Fix: re-pour or commit the pour so clean-tree gate stays meaningful. ROUTING: both are shopsystem-scenarios BC-local concerns; (A) is a BC test-harness fix (likely request_maintenance: test env plumbing), (B) is a re-pour/commit hygiene action. Source: mechanism_observation id=277 on lead-8z3f outbox.

title: template-release discipline: lead's installed shop-templates package (v0.2.0) lags templates BC origin/main — BC template changes don't reach lead/new products until a release is cut+reinstalled
description: Surfaced during lead-f3gm reconciliation 2026-06-12. The BC commits canonical-template changes to the templates repo origin/main (e.g. the WS-4 Part 1 PRIME DIRECTIVE pour, integration 9beb031), but the lead host's pip-installed shop-templates is v0.2.0 (cut 2026-06-04) and does NOT carry them. Also: 'shop-templates show' serves only role templates (bc-implementer/bc-reviewer/lead-architect/lead-po), NOT the CLAUDE.md primers, and there is no read_claude_md_primer export — so the lead cannot inspect canonical primer changes via the installed package at all. Consequence: a freshly-instantiated product won't inherit BC-committed template IP until a templates release is cut AND the lead reinstalls. Needs: (1) a republish/release cadence (or version bump) after canonical-template request_bugfix lands; (2) expose the CLAUDE.md primers via 'shop-templates show' so they're inspectable. Relates WS-4 (lead-hyxx), lead-n64d, lead-f3gm.
notes: RESOLVED (2026-06-12) by ADR-039 + executed: version-bump-is-part-of-the-fix doctrine + all 3 packages released (messaging v0.3.0, bc-launcher v0.3.0, templates v0.4.0) + lead reinstalled via plain pip -U (no --force). Delivery-currency gap closed end-to-end; cadence PROVEN. Fleet re-pour tracked on lead-n4r2.

title: Architect doctrine: recompute canonical-template pre-state from package data, not lead's local primer copy, when composing request_bugfix
description:
DOCTRINE FIX surfaced by lead-f3gm mechanism_observation (id 266, 'DELTA EVIDENCE stale vs canonical lead.md'). The lead-f3gm request_bugfix DELTA EVIDENCE described a pre-state that did NOT match canonical templates/claude/lead.md on origin/main at dispatch (base 2872e61): it claimed the PRIME DIRECTIVE block was ENTIRELY ABSENT (in fact it existed but was MISPOSITIONED after the SHOP_NAME heading), and claimed the choice-suppression body was the older prohibition-framed version (in fact commit 2730ea0 had already partially reframed it to an intermediate Carve-out + 2-trigger body). The acceptance pins were genuinely unmet so the BC reached them IDEMPOTENTLY by inspecting the actual file first — the fix was real, not a no-op — but the stale DELTA EVIDENCE described a partly-satisfied pre-state.

ROOT CAUSE: the architect computed pre-state delta against the lead shop's LOCAL .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md copy (and an assumed 'shop-templates show lead' output), which had DRIFTED AHEAD of what canonical package data actually carried. Compounding: the installed shop-templates is v0.2.0 (cut 2026-06-04), whose claude/lead.md has NO PRIME DIRECTIVE at all — so even the installed package data lags origin/main.

DOCTRINE: When composing a request_bugfix that revises a CANONICAL template (claude/<shop>.md, lead-architect.md, lead-po.md, bc-*.md), the architect MUST recompute pre-state from actual package data at compose time — i.e. read the canonical template the BC will edit (e.g. installed claude/lead.md package file or the BC's reported origin/main state), NOT the lead host's local .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md copy, which is a derived/poured artifact that can outrun or lag canonical. The local primer is the POUR SOURCE (intended end-state), not the canonical PRE-STATE. Cite the package-data pre-state in DELTA EVIDENCE, distinct from the pour-source intent.

CAVEAT discovered during reconciliation: installed shop-templates package data itself may be stale relative to BC origin/main (v0.2.0 vs the 9beb031 the BC just integrated). Per ADR-018 the lead cannot git-observe the BC tree. So 'package data at compose time' = installed package data + the BC's last reported origin/main commit, reconciled — and the DELTA EVIDENCE should note when these diverge.

Related by TEXT (no bd edge, keep dispatchable): lead-hyxx (WS-4 prep), lead-f3gm (WS-4 part 1, the dispatch this observation came from). Load-bearing for WS-4 part 2+ (lead-po.md / lead-architect.md / bc-*.md canonical pours).

title: bd-dolt .beads/issues.jsonl created_by re-export normalization (source-side fix for the non-idempotent clean-tree churn) — bd-fork track, NOT shopsystem-templates
description: Split off from lead-bdqm prong (A) during 2026-06-11 triage (lead-architect). The bd-dolt backend re-exports the tracked .beads/issues.jsonl on every bd-op and the created_by metadata flips (e.g. 'David Stenglein' -> 'unknown'), so a committed tree re-dirties immediately and git status --porcelain is never clean. This is the SOURCE of the non-idempotent churn that the pre-emit clean-tree gate trips on (lead-bdqm). Two complementary durable fixes; this bead owns the SOURCE prong: either (i) normalize created_by deterministically so it does not flip, or (ii) gitignore / .gitattributes-filter the volatile export field so a committed tree stays clean across bd-ops. Coordinate with bd-fork tracking (cf lead-vlsu upstream bd work). NOT shopsystem-templates scope — the templates BC owns the gate, but the gate carve-out (prong B) is already folded into lead-m56e; THIS bead is the orthogonal source-hygiene fix on the bd/.beads surface. Routing: bd-fork track owner, not a BC dispatch via shop-msg.

title: FINDING: bd-dolt .beads/issues.jsonl created_by re-export churn perpetually re-dirties the tracked file, tripping the BC pre-emit gate clean-tree (Check 1) — a verified-COMMITTED fix cannot satisfy the gate
description:
RECURRING TRAP, surfaced on lead-rply (bc-shopsystem-bc-launcher) reconciliation 2026-06-11; also seen on lead-if8q (lead-vhfa defect 2). Would hit EVERY BC.

SYMPTOM: After a BC commits its work product, `git status --porcelain` keeps showing `M .beads/issues.jsonl`. The bd-dolt backend re-exports the tracked .beads/issues.jsonl on every commit/bd-op, and the created_by metadata flips (e.g. 'David Stenglein' -> 'unknown'), so the tracked file re-dirties immediately after each commit. The BC Implementer pre-emit gate's clean-working-tree precondition (Check 1) therefore trips PERPETUALLY — even though the actual fix IS committed and reachable on origin/main. The BC emits work_done(status:blocked) for a deliverable that is genuinely done and green. On lead-rply this forced a lead steer (bc-container inject) + a false-block loop before the BC self-resolved; the deliverable (adf7b2d) was verified-green (167 passed) and reachable the whole time.

WHY IT IS A REAL GATE BUG, not just BC discipline: the file CANNOT be made clean by committing it — the next bd-op re-dirties it. So the BC cannot satisfy Check 1 by self-resolving in the normal commit-your-own-state way; the churn is non-idempotent. The clean-tree gate must treat .beads metadata-export churn as ambient (carve-out), OR the .beads created_by re-export must be normalized/gitignored so it stops re-dirtying.

DECIDE THE DURABLE TARGET (two complementary fixes):
  (A) bd / .beads hygiene: stop the created_by re-export churn at the source — either normalize created_by deterministically (don't flip David Stenglein->unknown), or gitignore/.gitattributes-filter the volatile export field so a committed tree stays clean across bd-ops. (Coordinate with bd-fork tracking, cf. lead-vlsu upstream bd work.)
  (B) pre-emit gate carve-out: the clean-working-tree precondition (Check 1) must add .beads/issues.jsonl metadata-export churn to the ambient-artifact carve-out set (cf. lead-8lm .specstory / .scheduled_tasks.lock precedent named in lead-vhfa). Feeds the bc-emit work-done precondition wrapper (lead-m56e / ADR-036) and the gate-redesign (lead-o6tp).

LINKS: relates lead-vhfa (same clean-tree false-block class; its defect 2 already names the .beads/issues.jsonl carve-out need), lead-oyaj (self-resolve doctrine — but note: this churn is NOT self-resolvable by commit, so it specifically needs a carve-out, not just discipline), lead-m56e/ADR-036 (precondition-wrapper target), lead-o6tp (gate redesign). References BC-side bclaunch-27p (the launcher BC flagged + closed its instance on origin/main 18ecee4).
notes:
TRIAGE 2026-06-11 (lead-architect). Discriminator vs contract surface (ADR-036 D1, features/templates/105-112, ADR-018). DISPOSITION = TWO-PRONGED, NEITHER prong is a clean bugfix to shopsystem-templates today: (A) bd/.beads created_by re-export churn fix is a bd-dolt / .gitattributes hygiene change at SOURCE — NOT in shopsystem-templates scope; it belongs with the bd-fork tracking (cf lead-vlsu upstream bd work), routed to whichever surface owns the bd-dolt backend / repo .gitattributes, not to the templates BC. Recommend a separate bd-hygiene bead/dispatch off the bd-fork track. (B) pre-emit gate clean-tree carve-out for .beads/issues.jsonl metadata churn = a wrapper-scenario requirement folded into lead-m56e step 1 (same carve-out set as lead-vhfa (b); cf lead-8lm .specstory/.scheduled_tasks.lock precedent). KEY: this churn is NON-idempotent, so it is NOT self-resolvable by commit (distinguishes it from lead-oyaj) — it specifically needs the carve-out (B), authored by lead-po into the wrapper scenarios. Both prongs tracked; (B) consolidated into lead-m56e, (A) needs its own bd-hygiene routing.
Prong (A) source-fix split off to lead-kf36 (bd-fork track). Prong (B) gate carve-out folded into lead-m56e wrapper scenarios. This finding stays open as the umbrella until both land.

title: [bug] bd init-safety guard deadlock: empty local Dolt + configured sync.remote has NO non-destructive prefix-adoption path (all 5 recovery commands mutually block)
description:
DESIGN GAP in the bd (beads) tool itself, surfaced by the shopsystem-templates mechanism_observation on lead-2bdq. Distinct from lead-rply (which fixes OUR launcher so the wedge never occurs); this bead captures that bd offers NO non-destructive escape once the wedge HAS occurred.

STATE THAT DEADLOCKS: a local embedded-Dolt bd DB that is (a) empty in the working set, (b) has NO issue_prefix set, but (c) HAS a configured sync.remote pointing at a -beads repo whose committed issues.jsonl carries a definite prefix. In this exact state bd v1.0.3 blocks ALL five documented recovery commands:
  1. bd init --prefix X            -> 'already initialized'
  2. bd init --force/--reinit-local-> refuses ('remote origin already has Dolt history; would orphan-push')
  3. bd bootstrap                  -> 'database exists'
  4. bd config set issue_prefix    -> 'issue_prefix cannot be set via bd config set'
  5. bd rename-prefix X            -> 'failed to get current prefix: <nil>' (no current prefix to rename FROM)

So a user with a perfectly recoverable situation (committed jsonl + remote both name the prefix) has no documented forward path. This is upstream bd behavior, NOT shopsystem code — route as an UPSTREAM bd issue / our own bd-fork tracking, NOT to a BC dispatch. Lead-level finding; do not assign_scenarios to a BC.

EMPIRICAL NOTE / the accidental escape hatch: 'bd config set issue_prefix tmpl' FAILS its stated operation but as a SIDE EFFECT triggers bd's 'auto-import-on-empty-database', which imported the 23 committed issues AND adopted prefix 'tmpl' from them — leaving the DB fully operational. So path 4 is a de-facto (undocumented, side-effect) recovery. The proper fix is for bd to make empty-local + configured-remote NON-destructively adopt the remote/committed prefix via an explicit command (e.g. bd bootstrap should detect empty working set and import+adopt rather than erroring 'database exists').

DOWNSTREAM: once lead-rply (launcher provisioning) lands, this wedge state should never arise from our launch path; this bead remains as the upstream-robustness backstop.
notes:
RESOLVED-BY-UNDERSTANDING (lead empirical proof, 2026-06-11): bd is NOT broken — it already provides the non-destructive adoption path. PROVEN: on a fresh BC clone, reset .beads to committed state (issues.jsonl present, NO bd-created dolt working set), then 'bd bootstrap' -> 'Bootstrap plan: import from git-tracked JSONL ... Imported 21 issues' -> embeddeddolt/ created -> bd create/ready WRITE-READY, fully non-destructive. The 'deadlock' was SELF-INFLICTED: the launcher ran 'bd dolt pull' FIRST (creating an empty local DB) which made bootstrap say 'already exists, nothing to do'. FIX = launcher provisions via 'bd bootstrap' on the fresh clone, NOT dolt-pull-then-import (lead-kjv7). No bd code change needed. Downgrading from P0. Residual nit (NOT a blocker): bootstrap derives new-issue prefix from db-name ('scenarios') not the committed ids ('shopsystem-scenarios') — minor consistency, bd still functional; track separately if it matters.
Re-confirmed RESOLVED-BY-UNDERSTANDING and the fix is now in flight as lead-ezzr (request_bugfix -> bc-launcher, provision via bd bootstrap). bd is NOT broken; the deadlock was self-inflicted by launcher's bd dolt pull. Stays P2 as upstream-robustness backstop; not a BC dispatch.
close_reason: RESOLVED by v0.2.8: bd bootstrap IS the non-destructive prefix-adoption path; deadlock was self-inflicted by dolt-pull-first. Launcher now provisions via bd bootstrap as vscode. Premise dissolved.

title: Brokered boot exceeds launcher 60s readiness gate -> startup prompt not injected
description: Observed in the v0.2.6 live fleet-flip proof 2026-06-10. On a brokered boot, claude DOES boot to the agent REPL (proven: answered FLIPREADY end-to-end), but takes >60s, so the launcher's 60s readiness gate times out before claude is ready and the startup-prompt is therefore never injected into the tmux session. The agent boots correctly; only the automated startup-prompt injection is missed because the gate fires early. Fix direction: extend/relax the readiness barrier for brokered boots (or make the gate key on an observable readiness signal rather than a fixed 60s deadline) so the startup prompt lands. Relates lead-5jbc (fleet-flip rollout). Found alongside lead-2s6y/lead-3q12 (both closed in v0.2.6).

description:

title: bc-container launch: configured beads issue_prefix does not take in the container — forces manual bd init/bootstrap on every bring-up
description:
CONFIRMED TWICE 2026-06-09 (bc-launcher + templates bring-ups). bc-container launch prints 'Configured beads issue_prefix

in container', but inside the container 'bd' reports 'database not initialized: issue_prefix config is missing'. Every fresh bring-up therefore stalls the BC at a beads-registry gate until the lead steers it to run bd bootstrap / bd init --prefix. The launch DOES run 'bd dolt pull' (so the dolt data is present) — only the local issue_prefix config doesn't persist/take. Blocks autonomous bring-up + inbox drain. FIX: bc-container launch must actually establish a working bd config (bootstrap from the pulled remote, or set+verify issue_prefix) before injecting the startup prompt, and verify 'bd ready' works as a readiness gate. Relates to bring-up reliability (lead-n5r1 lineage) and the recurring BC self-resolve gap (lead-oyaj).

title: agent-vault design decisions before implementation: server topology, zero-bind-mount, two-server bring-up
description: Resolve BEFORE lead-v4ih authoring. (A) SERVER: broker must run as a container on the shopsystem network (spike-proven); decide owner/lifecycle (compose service like postgres vs launcher-managed) + master-password-on-restart + one-time human secret-load. (B) ZERO BIND MOUNTS (dave's hard goal): bake non-secret static config (placeholder .credentials.json, .claude.json, gitconfig) into bc-base; route gh creds through an agent-vault GitHub service; CA fetched at runtime by 'agent-vault run' (spike: self-bootstraps, no mount); inject ONLY AGENT_VAULT_* env -> delete _resolve_host_path/BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME. (GAP) TWO SERVERS: postgres + agent-vault broker arranged as one lead-shop supporting-services compose on shopsystem net w/ persistent volumes + restart; bc-launcher gains agent_vault_reachable readiness gate; bin/shop-shell brings both up. See findings/agent-vault-credential-spike.md.
notes: DECISIONS LOCKED 2026-06-09 (dave). ZERO MOUNTS: gh token via agent-vault GitHub service (NOT treated as needing proof); gitconfig via GIT_AUTHOR_*/GIT_COMMITTER_* env sourced from a lead .env file; placeholder .credentials.json + .claude.json BAKED into bc-base; CA fetched at runtime by 'agent-vault run'. Launch injects ONLY AGENT_VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN/VAULT env -> delete _resolve_host_path + BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME. SERVER: broker is a SUPPORTING-SERVICES COMPOSE service (extend compose.yaml alongside postgres), NOT bc-launcher-owned (launcher is a tool to start BCs, not a service owner). AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN = launch env var (MVP). Master password = auto-unlock from a .env file (MVP). SCOPE: BCs only; lead host stays launched as-is. PREREQUISITE: bc-base rebuilds MUST be solved first (gates the baking).
close_reason: Design decisions resolved (see notes). lead-v4ih authoring is design-unblocked but SEQUENCED AFTER bc-base rebuild is solved (dave: before any other work).

Candidates from the 'declarative agent workflows' GitHub search, filtered to relatively-simple + self-hostable: Pipelex/pipelex (682*, MIT, Python), mhingston/keystone-cli (TS/Bun, local-first), asaficontact/DKMV (Python, declarative workflows FOR Claude Code/Codex), levelplaneai/agent-runtime (Go runtime), graphsentinel/agentic-lab (Python, behavior-as-code). Each evaluated on the SAME frame as fabro: SEAM(a) BC launch, SEAM(b) Monitor->loop (external async arrival), three invariant-leaks (bd authority / name-registry addressing ADR-006/020 / ADR-018 empirical-verification), sharpest-risk (ADR-012 bd-first 2PC vs checkpoint), self-hostability+simplicity, kill-or-confirm spike, go/no-go. Comparative scorecard vs fabro at the end. Part of the iterative-experimentation initiative lead-odqd.
close_reason: Delivered: fabro-level evaluation + comparative scorecard for pipelex/keystone-cli/DKMV/agent-runtime/agentic-lab. Conclusion: none beats fabro on simple+self-hostable; all REFUTE seam-b. findings/substrate-candidate-comparison-vs-fabro.md. Follow-ups: lead-fih1/jzef/sr36/qheu.

title: Design: first-class iterative-experimentation process for shopsystem (spike vehicle / extend PDR-014 graduation path)
description: Meta-deliverable of the experimentation initiative. The framework has no spike/experiment message-type vehicle (only assign_scenarios/request_bugfix/request_maintenance/request_scenario/request_shop) but DOES have experimental-first adoption + PDR-014 skill-graduation. Synthesize from how the agent-vault + fabro spikes actually run: what does shopsystem need to support iterative experimentation as a first-class thing? (request_spike vehicle? findings/-driven spike->ADR graduation? worktree throwaway branches? human-in-the-loop interactive-step protocol?). Output: proposed process + ADR/PDR skeleton + scenario outlines for Phase 2.
close_reason: Delivered design synthesis: findings/iterative-experimentation-capability.md (mechanism DECISION = extend PDR-014 graduation, no request_spike vehicle; 6-stage spike lifecycle; 4-value verdict vocab; human-in-the-loop wall protocol; PDR-016+ADR-026-029 skeleton + 8 scenario outlines). Graduates to Phase-2 authoring bead.

title: Spike: replace host-FS credential bind-mount with agent-vault (OAuth proxy + placeholder creds) in bc-launcher
description: Throwaway spike. agent-vault replaces the ~/.claude bind-mount + BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME path with AGENT_VAULT_* env + a __PLACEHOLDER__ .credentials.json + a proxy that substitutes real tokens at the Anthropic API boundary (container never holds real creds). Spike goal: prove how far the BC-launch path can swap bind-mount->agent-vault non-interactively, and document the interactive/human-gated steps (one-time token paste, standing up an agent-vault instance, proxy-role agent token). THROW AWAY after; real impl via ADR+scenarios. Part of the iterative-experimentation initiative.
close_reason: CONFIRM (go-with-caveats). Real throwaway experiment passed all 5 assertions: no real-cred mount, placeholder in container, proxy injects HTTPS_PROXY+CA, creds-free 401 kill-line through MITM, no BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME needed. Broker must run as a shopsystem-network container. findings/agent-vault-credential-spike.md. Graduates to Phase-2 ADR+scenarios bead.

title: Initiative: iterative-experimentation capability for shopsystem — proven via agent-vault creds + fabro 2PC-as-steps spikes
description: User 2026-06-09: extend the shopsystem framework to handle ITERATIVE EXPERIMENTATION as a first-class capability. Method: spike -> learn limitations through real experimentation -> throw the spike away -> implement via ADRs+scenarios. Two parallel experiments serve as the first cases AND as the proving ground for the experimentation process itself: (A) replace the awkward host-filesystem credential bind-mount (~/.claude + BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME, see reference_bclauncher_host_home_required) with agent-vault (docs.agent-vault.dev/guides/oauth-claude-code: OAuth proxy + __PLACEHOLDER__ creds, no real creds in container); (B) re-open fabro.sh (lead-f6ta) and spike moving the shop-msg bd-first two-phase commit (ADR-012) out into SEPARATE fabro steps to resolve the documented checkpoint-collision hazard. Run Phase-1 spikes via workflow; graduate to ADRs+scenarios via lead-po/lead-architect in Phase 2.
notes: SCOPE CLARIFIED 2026-06-09 (dave): this initiative is the EXPERIMENT DISCIPLINE only (the meta-capability). agent-vault (initiative lead-mdng) and fabro/substrate (lead-f6ta) are SEPARATE tracks that merely served as proving cases. Remaining child: lead-95q5 (experiment-discipline Phase-2 authoring).

title: bc-reviewer must block work_done when a retirement-named hash is still reachable under features/ (retired-scenario removal enforcement)
description: GAP found during lead-5p07 journal design (dave, 2026-06-08). Symmetric to scenario 116 (orphan/strict-subset) and 114 (stale-hash), but for RETIREMENT: today no BC-side reviewer gate enforces that a hash the consumed dispatch named for retirement is actually removed from the BC features/ tree. Failure mode: BC ADDS a new scenario block (new body+tag) while LEAVING the old retired block (old body+old tag) in place -> 114 passes (tag matches body), 116 passes (hash on disk), old test keeps running green -> increasing superset of stale scenarios/tests for retired behavior. Retirement currently relies on the architect's explicit retirement instruction (scenarios 95/128) + Implementer discipline, with NO enforced invariant. PROPOSED: a bc-reviewer scenario mirroring 116 -- block work_done --status complete when any @scenario_hash tag for a hash the consumed dispatch named for retirement is still reachable under the as-committed features/ tree; name work_id + the un-removed retired hash in the blocked response. Lead-side DETECTION already covered by lead-5p07 scenario 07 (orphan-anomaly flags retired-but-not-removed completions in the lead view); THIS bead is the BC-side ENFORCEMENT so the superset is stopped at source, not just detected. Eventual vehicle: assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates (bc-reviewer template). Related: lead-5p07, scenarios 114/116/95/128/117-E, ADR-010 §4.

title: bc-implementer/reviewer discipline: self-resolve fixable procedural gate failures before work_done
description: Doctrine decision (operator 2026-06-05, from first dispatch lead-y8rz). Red-before-green commit-ordering gate stays HARD (not softened). But the BC must SELF-RESOLVE fixable procedural gate failures (close own sub-issues, insert/reorder test(red) commit, clean tree) BEFORE emitting work_done — never emit work_done(blocked) for self-fixable process artifacts. work_done(blocked) reserved for things needing lead input (scope/contract/architecture/vocabulary). Authoring: PDR + bc-implementer/bc-reviewer Gherkin scenarios -> assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates. See memory feedback_bc_self_resolves_procedural_gates. Relates lead-fnj5 (the dispatch_state guard bug that also bit this dispatch).
notes:
2026-06-11 FOURTH live instance (bc-launcher lead-rply beads-provisioning bugfix): BC emitted work_done(blocked) for a fully GREEN, committable fix (167 passed) whose ONLY blocker was its own uncommitted/unpushed worktree — entire fix dirty, local HEAD==origin/main==5ac19e9. Self-fixable: discard the .beads/issues.jsonl created_by re-export churn (BC-side bclaunch-27p) + commit 4 fix paths + push + re-review. Required a lead steer (bc-container inject) to self-complete. Reinforces: the self-resolve discipline MUST be baked into bc-implementer/bc-reviewer template — work_done(blocked) is reserved for lead-input needs, never self-fixable commit/push steps.
TRIAGE 2026-06-11 (lead-architect). Discriminator vs contract surface (ADR-036, features/templates/105-116, bd-show own description). DISPOSITION: the bead's own ASK is correct and already names the vehicle — 'Authoring: PDR + bc-implementer/bc-reviewer Gherkin scenarios -> assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates'. This is NEW capability framing (self-resolve discipline as authored scenarios/prose), so the vehicle is assign_scenarios, which requires lead-po authoring FIRST — STOP, not architect-dispatchable today. RELATIONSHIP TO WRAPPER: post-ADR-036 the mechanically-checkable preconditions move to the bc-emit wrapper (lead-m56e); the self-resolve DISCIPLINE (close own sub-issues, reorder red commit, commit/push own green fix before emitting blocked) is a JUDGMENT behavior that STAYS template-prose (ADR-036 D1 prose-layer: not mechanically checkable). So lead-oyaj is the prose-layer cousin: lead-po authors a bc-implementer/bc-reviewer prose pin (+ optional scenario) that blocked is reserved for genuine lead-input needs, never self-fixable commit/push/sub-issue steps. NOTE: the lead-bdqm .beads churn is the EXCEPTION (non-idempotent, needs carve-out not discipline). RECOMMEND: lead-po authoring item — self-resolve-discipline prose pin for bc-implementer/bc-reviewer; can ride alongside lead-m56e wrapper dispatch or as its own assign_scenarios. Gated on lead-yxsr before dispatch.
