Contradicts scenario 39 (proposed amendment relocates preservation contract to .claude/shop/primer.md). 15 new scenarios named. Migration UX: refuse-with-diagnostic for legacy shops (this lead shop is one). Architect picks up acceptance + amend-vs-supersede call on 39 + Phrasing A/B on 45/71/72/73 + dispatch.

title: PO: revise PDR-003 — use @-import mechanism + separate canonical files (not sentinels)
description: User rejected sentinel-delimited region (alternative C) in PDR-003. New direction (2026-05-14): canonical primer content lives in separate files (e.g., under .claude/canonical/ or similar), and CLAUDE.md uses Claude Code's @<filename> import mechanism to include them. Update overwrites the canonical files; CLAUDE.md itself remains non-touch (scenario 39 preserved unchanged). PO must revise PDR-003 to add this as alternative E and recommend it instead of C, re-evaluate which scenarios are contradicted (likely none of 39/56 now — only NEW scenarios needed), and name the new scenarios that pin the import-based contract.
close_reason: PDR-003 revised in place: alternative E (canonical primer files at .claude/canonical/<shop-type>-primer.md, imported into CLAUDE.md via Claude Code @-directive) recommended. Contradicts no existing scenarios — 39 and 56 preserved unchanged. 7 new scenarios named for post-acceptance authoring, including migration-UX scenario for legacy shops. Architect picks up: pre-state verification, Phrasing A/B call for 71-73 (storage-location vs agent-observable-behavior), migration-UX shape (non-zero exit vs WARNING line).

title: PO: temporal self-sufficiency — CLAUDE.md must be provided AND updated, not maintained locally
description: User directive (2026-05-14): CLAUDE.md should be provided and updated by templates mechanism, not maintained locally. Currently update() explicitly does NOT touch CLAUDE.md (cli.py:585-587 names a 'non-touch invariant'). Scenario 39 (features/templates/39-update-preserves-shop-owned-claude-md-edits.gherkin) pins exactly that invariant. User's intent contradicts scenario 39 as written. PO must: (a) author the new intent for canonical-CLAUDE.md propagation through update, (b) name which existing scenarios that contradicts (39 is the obvious one; check 45's shop-identity-in-CLAUDE.md angle and 56's 'unchanged from before' assertion), and (c) decide what the new contract should be (e.g., merge strategy with shop identity preserved, full replace, sectioned merge, etc.).
close_reason: PO authored PDR-003 (pdr/003-claude-md-update-propagation.md). Recommends sentinel-delimited canonical region; contradicts scenarios 39 and 56; preserves 44, 45, 31, 71-73, 40, 41. Six new scenarios named in PDR, not pre-authored pending sentinel-vs-parameterization decision. Architect now picks up vehicle choice (request_bugfix against templates BC suggested).

title: PO: bootstrap self-sufficiency of router prompt (Monitor-arming + repos/ unavailable)
description: Gap exposed in session 2026-05-14: the lead-shop router's session-start Monitor-arming directive lives in repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md, but the lead-shop's own CLAUDE.md does not contain it. On a fresh clone, repos/ is gitignored and the product venv is not yet installed, so a bootstrap session has no in-repo signal telling the router to arm inotifywait on repos/*/outbox/. PDR-001 (templates role-complete restructure) and PDR-002 (router/subagent inline via path (a)) are adjacent; brief 003 (event-driven activation) is adjacent. PO must determine whether this is (a) already covered by in-flight scenarios, (b) a sharpening to PDR-002 / brief 003, or (c) net-new intent, and author accordingly.
close_reason: PO determined gap is already pinned by scenarios 71/72/73 (templates BC), authored under brief 003 realization revision (lead-o00, now closed). No new authoring needed. Closing operational gap (refreshing this repo's CLAUDE.md from updated canonical templates) is a separate router-level step, not PO intent.

title: request_bugfix dispatched to shopsystem-templates: canonical settings.json hook-schema wrapper (scenario 70)
description:
Child of lead-bvj. Dispatches scenario 70 + canonical template content fix to shopsystem-templates BC.

Carries:
- Scenario 70 (Scenario Outline bc+lead) pinning Claude Code hook-schema wrapper shape: hooks.SessionStart is array of {matcher: string, hooks: array}, each inner hook is {type: string, command: string}.
- Description instructs Implementer to (a) correct both canonical templates at src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json to use {matcher: '', hooks: [{type, command}]} wrappers, AND (b) implement JSON-parsing step defs needed for scenario 70.

Pre-state verified empirically: read repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/bc.json and lead.json — both use bare {type, command} SessionStart entries (not matcher+hooks wrapper). Scenario 70 step 6 ('matcher key whose value is a string') fails today.

Discriminator: capability exists (templates+scenarios 57-68 pin SessionStart content substrings) but new structural depth is unpinned → request_bugfix tightening, scenario-carrying.

Reference shape: shopsystem-test-harness commit 6865ff0 hand-patched the file locally with the correct schema — that is the shape to canonicalize.

Procedural: wire-form hashing per lead-wgv (shop-msg send computes); non-dotted ID per lead-4wy; venv-activated invocation per lead-zmi.
close_reason: Reconciled. BC reviewer's work_done complete on lead-9ht verified: scenario hash f2a7de49d80332c1 in work_done matches dispatch; teeth-probed (reverting bc.json wrapper to bare {type,command} fails the [bc] row of scenario 70). Post-state spot-check of canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc.json,lead.json}: both use matcher+hooks wrapper — hooks.SessionStart[*] are objects with matcher (string) and hooks (array of {type, command}). lead.json preserves two distinct SessionStart wrappers (bd prime + inotifywait); bc.json has the inotifywait wrapper. Bootstrap probe (shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe-fix --target /tmp/probe-bvj) pours canonical schema correctly into generated .claude/settings.json. BDD: 131/0. Brief-003 substring pins (c1e7f31eeef73e05, a9379ab3a162158d, f83e03ee69261242) re-attested through conftest flattening logic.

title: Brief 003 canonical settings.json templates use wrong Claude Code hook schema (bare type+command vs matcher+hooks wrapper)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 when user attempted 'claude' session start in shopsystem-test-harness after brief 003's bootstrap update.

Claude Code rejects the canonical settings.json with validation error:
  /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-test-harness/.claude/settings.json
   └ hooks → SessionStart → 0 → hooks: Expected array, but received undefined

Cause: brief 003's canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json wrote SessionStart entries in this shape:
  { 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }
But Claude Code's actual schema requires the matcher+hooks wrapper:
  { 'matcher': '', 'hooks': [{ 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }] }

The scenarios 57-68 pinned content substrings (SessionStart, stdbuf -oL inotifywait, watch-target tokens) but NOT the structural schema Claude Code validates against. The BC's Reviewer signed off because all 12 scenarios passed against the static-content checks; nothing exercised the file against real Claude Code.

Two facets:
(a) Canonical template content correction — fix the bc.json and lead.json package data to use matcher+hooks wrappers. shop-templates update propagates to existing shops.
(b) Scenario tightening — add a new scenario pinning the actual Claude Code hook schema (e.g., 'every SessionStart entry has a matcher key and a hooks array'). Without this, future regressions of the canonical templates won't be caught.

Resolution shape: dispatch request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates carrying both the canonical fix and the scenario tightening.

Local workaround applied today: hand-patched /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-test-harness/.claude/settings.json with the correct schema (commit 6865ff0 in the BC). Other already-bootstrapped shops will inherit the bug until canonical templates are fixed AND 'shop-templates update' is run.

Sister concern: brief 003's empirical validation focused on stdbuf-vs-buffering correctness (the lead-79q reconciliation pattern) but not on whether Claude Code itself would accept the file. The 'pre-state empirical verification' invariant from brief 002/003 should grow a hook-schema-validates-against-claude-code check for any future SessionStart-touching brief.

Related: brief 003 (lead-0h1, closed), lead-i8u (bootstrap-existing-beads gap).
close_reason: Resolved via child lead-9ht (closed). Canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc.json,lead.json} now carry the matcher+hooks wrapper Claude Code requires; scenario 70 (hash f2a7de49d80332c1) is in the BC's feature set as a structural pin guarding against regression to the bare {type,command} shape. shop-templates bootstrap verified to pour the corrected canonical. Residual note (no action): test-harness BC's local settings.json was hand-patched in commit 6865ff0; matches canonical shape, so a future shop-templates update will pour idempotently. Meta-concern for the record (no action): pre-state empirical verification for SessionStart-touching briefs would benefit from a Claude-Code-binary acceptance step — invariant evolution, follow-on work.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: PO authored scenario 70 (Scenario Outline over bc+lead) pinning the Claude Code hook-schema wrapper shape: hooks.SessionStart is an array of {matcher: string, hooks: array}, and each inner hooks element is {type: string, command: string}. File: features/templates/70-canonical-claude-settings-json-conforms-to-claude-code-hook-schema-wrapper-shape.gherkin. Deferred the optional E2E Claude-Code-binary validation scenario: invoking the closed-binary validator from BC BDD couples the templates suite to an out-of-band tool; the bead already names this as a sister meta-concern better placed as a pre-state-empirical-verification invariant on the lead-shop side. Existing scenarios 57-68 stay valid (their substring checks pass regardless of wrapper shape); 70 adds the structural depth that was missing. Architect to dispatch request_bugfix (canonical template content fix + scenario 70) to shopsystem-templates.

title: Clarify protocol gap — clarify-resolution requires explicit re-dispatch carrying original scenarios
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-xkh→lead-5mz→lead-1pi chain. Sister concern to lead-rv9 (work_done blocked).

Pattern observed: BC clarifies on a dispatch (lead-xkh). Lead resolves the clarify by dispatching a follow-up (lead-5mz) carrying ONLY the resolution scenario (scenario 69). BC processes lead-5mz cleanly but does NOT re-process lead-xkh — because lead-xkh has a clarify in outbox, it's no longer 'pending' from BC's view. The original scenarios in lead-xkh remain unimplemented until the lead sends ANOTHER dispatch (lead-1pi) carrying them.

This is a coordination gap:
- The §4.4 closure narrative says 'terminal work_done implicitly closes the chain.'
- But that only works when the follow-up CARRIES the prior scenarios (as lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e did via accumulation).
- If the follow-up carries ONLY the resolution (e.g., a small supersession), the original scenarios are stranded.

Two design questions:
1. Should the BC's protocol re-process an inbox message after a clarify is responded to? (e.g., 'clarify in outbox does not finalize pending state; lead's next dispatch with matching work_id signals re-process')
2. Or should the lead-side protocol explicitly carry-forward prior unimplemented scenarios in every follow-up? (current de facto pattern, but easy to miss)
3. Or should we have a new message type 'clarify_resolved' (or similar) that signals 'process the originally-dispatched scenarios now'?

The current workaround (lead-1pi re-dispatches the 12 brief 003 scenarios + supersession context in description) works but produces wire-form hash drift (because the feature-title wrapping changes between dispatches) and isn't machine-readable for tracking which dispatch carries which scenarios.

Sister concerns:
- lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap)
- lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption)
- lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry)
- lead-yug (@supersedes tag convention)

All four-plus are accumulating in a 'catalog protocol gaps' cluster that would benefit from a coordinated brief revision once the test-harness or other priority work creates pressure to consolidate.

Concrete impact on lead-1pi: wire-form hashes for scenarios 57-68 differ from lead-xkh dispatch (e.g., 57 was d4b8aac19b34ea8c then; 1621b59b0ea8b20b now). Implementer's BDD attestation will use the new hashes. The brief 003 intent semantically equivalent.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE (resolved): ADR-009 (accepted 2026-05-27) decides path (b) — re-dispatch via existing catalog types — as the clarify-resolution vehicle. Native clarify_response primitive explicitly DEFERRED to lead-ox8 (P3 OPEN), which carries the only residual. Dedupe into lead-ox8.
close_reason: Closed

title: Product venv installs drift from BC source after BC work lands (no auto-reinstall)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-79q dispatch.

The product venv at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/ has shop_msg installed as a regular package (not editable). When BCs update their source (lead-231.1 added 'pending' subcommand; lead-bwu/14e/ijr updated CLI behavior), the venv install does NOT update automatically. Lead-shop CLI invocations against shop-msg (and likely shop-templates similarly) see the OLDER installed surface, not the BC's live source.

Concrete impact today: shop-msg pending didn't exist in the venv install despite being present in BC source. Architect for lead-79q hit this and worked around by direct ls of inbox/outbox — a violation of brief 001 invariant 1 (encapsulation; CLI is sole messaging surface) forced by tooling staleness. Re-installed manually via 'pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging' which fixed it. shop-templates likely has the same issue (Architect for lead-79q also noted product venv shop-templates lacks bootstrap/update).

Two facets:
(a) Install mode: BCs should be installed editable (-e) so source changes flow without reinstall. Currently appears to be non-editable.
(b) Reinstall trigger: even if editable, fresh BC clones / version changes need a way to refresh. SessionStart hook in brief 003 could include 'pip install -e repos/*/' to keep the venv synced.

Adjacent / related:
- lead-zmi (shop-msg PATH ergonomics) — same theme, different facet.
- Brief 003 SessionStart hook design — should include venv-resync as part of activating each shop.

The 'ls workaround' the Architect used is a discipline concern: the lead-architect template's encapsulation guidance should be tightened to say 'if shop-msg pending fails, FIX THE TOOLING (reinstall/PATH/version) not bypass'. Worth a separate template-tightening bead if recurring.
notes: 2026-06-09 CONCRETE BITE: the lead's installed shopsystem-messaging==0.2.1 LACKS the lead-fnj5 dependency-guard fix (fix landed in messaging origin/main 1fae384 but not reinstalled). This caused the rl0f dispatch to false-refuse ('predecessor at dispatch_state=closed, not closed') and --queue-on-dependency stranded rl0f (outbox_pending, no payload) — required manual unset-metadata recovery. Durable fix = reinstall shop-msg from current messaging main; until then EVERY dependency-gated dispatch from the lead still hits the (already-fixed-at-source) guard bug.

title: bc-implementer template should require hash-recompute verification after @scenario_hash tag edits (drain of messaging-d64)
description:
Drain of messaging-d64 mechanism_observation from shopsystem-messaging, observed during lead-5cv.

Finding: bc-implementer canonical template does not require the Implementer to verify a @scenario_hash:<value> tag by piping the file through 'scenarios hash' before claiming the tag is correct. In lead-231.2 the Implementer wrote 4 fabricated hash values; Reviewer caught it but only via lead-side reconciliation cost (full §4.4 round-trip via lead-5cv).

BC's proposed action: Add a 'verify hash tags' step in the bc-implementer template's 'Doing the work' sections for assign_scenarios and request_bugfix (and an equivalent clause for request_maintenance when it touches scenario tags) requiring 'scenarios hash' recompute equality before terminal response.

Target BC: shopsystem-templates (the canonical bc-implementer template is the templates BC's product; messaging BC cannot self-fix).

Vehicle hint: request_bugfix (template-language tightening of unpinned existing behavior).

Natural fold-in with lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook for brief 001 — per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions). Both items tighten the bc-implementer canonical template via additional procedural discipline; both could be carried by one brief revision that consolidates 46-49 + write-mailbox cases + hash-verify step into a coordinated dispatch. Worth considering together when the next brief revision is authored.

Also adjacent: lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw vs wire form). Tightening hash-verify discipline in the template is load-bearing; ensuring the BC's bc-implementer knows WHICH form to verify against requires lead-wgv to be resolved first (or the discipline step to be explicit about it).

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook), lead-wgv (canonicalization), lead-5cv (original incident).
notes: 2026-06-09 NOW DISPATCH-READY: blocker lead-wgv ('which form to verify against') is CLOSED (resolved by feature 117 block-only + ADR-019). Vehicle: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates (bc-implementer hash-recompute-equality after @scenario_hash edits). Consider folding with lead-6t3.

title: Round-4 outlook: per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions (shop-msg respond clarify | work_done | mechanism_observation) across all four canonical templates
description:
Follow-on refinement that emerged from brief 001 items B+D §4.4 process (rounds 2-3 dispatch chain lead-bwu -> lead-e4g -> lead-14e closed; scenarios 46-49 landed pinning per-step CLI naming for READ-mailbox actions across all four canonical templates).

Remaining regression surface flagged in lead-231 round-4 outlook notes:

- WRITING outbox responses: lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify' but no scenario pins per-step CLI naming for the respond action. Same regression mode as scenarios 46-49: future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only template-wide scenarios would gate it.
- Same applies to lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 ('shop-msg respond work_done') and 'Surfacing mechanism observations' ('shop-msg respond mechanism_observation') — not pinned per-step.
- bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' ('shop-msg respond work_done' / 'shop-msg respond clarify') — not pinned per-step.

Recommendation from lead-231 notes: defer to a deliberate brief revision rather than dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline. Cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly.

Vehicle when dispatched: likely request_bugfix (behavior exists in templates, no scenario pins it at per-step granularity — finding-5 territory). PO authors a Scenario Outline; Architect verifies BC pre-state empirically (read each respond-step in each template, confirm CLI is named) and dispatches.

Not blocking anything. Brief 001 (lead-231) is being closed as substantively complete; this is a separate follow-on concern.

title: scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during lead-e4g dispatch (request_bugfix closing lead-bwu §4.4 loop).

The Architect observed: 'scenarios hash < features/templates/46-*.gherkin' (against the raw scenario body) produces 06328160e83d2995, while the hash 'shop-msg send' persists on the wire (validated by 'shop-msg read') is e6bdf2f33bfae0d1. The CLI computes against a wrapped+sentinel-tagged form, not the raw body.

This is the Slice-16-shaped mismatch named in the architect's role contract anti-rationalization section. Same shape almost surfaced in lead-231.2 / lead-5cv (Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values; Reviewer caught via 'cat file | scenarios hash' which DID match the post-edit canonical) — but that case's reconciliation worked because both Implementer and Reviewer used raw-body hashing on already-tagged feature files, where the @scenario_hash tag had been hand-edited. The two-canonicalization-domains problem didn't bite because both parties stayed within one domain.

For lead-e4g and any future dispatch, the canonical question matters:
- The BC's ScenarioPayload.hash == compute_scenario_hash(gherkin) enforcement uses which canonicalization?
- The 'scenarios hash' CLI computes which form?
- Implementers verifying tags need to match against what the catalog enforces.

Three resolution shapes:
(a) One canonical form across both. 'scenarios hash' wraps its input the same way shop-msg does; tag values across feature files / inboxes / outboxes are byte-identical for identical scenario semantic content.
(b) Two named forms with clear scope. 'scenarios hash --raw' and 'scenarios hash --wire' (or similar); documented when each applies.
(c) Status quo + documentation. Names the two domains and where each is canonical; templates and procedural reminders cite the right form.

(a) is the cleanest invariant; (b) is most explicit; (c) is lowest-touch but most failure-prone.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap), lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption) — same shape of latent protocol/canonicalization constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Adjacent: lead-5cv's Implementer-defect-and-Reviewer-catch worked because both used raw-body hashing on already-tagged files (within one canonicalization domain). A future dispatch where one party uses the wire-form hash and the other uses raw-body would silently disagree.

Concrete impact for current work: lead-e4g's @scenario_hash tag in the BC inbox is the wire form. The BC's Implementer / Reviewer must validate against the wire form, not via raw-body 'scenarios hash'. Worth a procedural reminder in the dispatch description (already included by Architect for lead-e4g).
close_reason: Already done. Canonicalization ambiguity (raw body vs wrapped+tagged) resolved at contract level by scenario 117 (exactly one block-only canonical hash text) and at implementation level by the ADR-019 fix (verified live 2026-06-08).

title: work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).
notes: 2026-06-09 RE-SCOPE: template-level work_done(blocked) pathway LANDED (bc-reviewer template summary-shape constraint). RESIDUAL: the lead-SIDE response-shape decision (is 'request_maintenance for Reviewer-cited value fixes' the canonical pathway?) is unrecorded in any ADR/PDR. Narrow to that spec/discriminator decision.

title: Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept
description:
Surfaced by Architect during brief-001 items A+C dispatch on 2026-05-12.

During dispatch of lead-231.1 (AssignScenarios) and lead-231.2 (RequestBugfix) to shopsystem-messaging, the Architect used dotted child bead IDs (lead-231.1, lead-231.2) as work_ids. Both schemas accepted them — neither AssignScenarios.work_id nor RequestBugfix.work_id carries a pattern constraint (just str). But Clarify.work_id has pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which rejects dots.

This is a latent failure: if the BC sends Clarify on either lead-231.1 or lead-231.2, the BC-side 'shop-msg respond clarify --work-id lead-231.1' will fail schema validation. The protocol's symmetry across message types breaks at clarify-time.

What to do: align work_id patterns across catalog schemas. Three shapes worth considering — (a) tighten AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix/RequestMaintenance/WorkDone/MechanismObservation to share Clarify's pattern, constraining work_id naming uniformly; (b) loosen Clarify to match the others (just str), accepting more chaos but unblocking; (c) pick a shared canonical pattern (probably (a)'s shape with an explicit grammar) and apply uniformly with a single Pydantic type alias. Architect's call after pre-state verification.

Adjacent finding (do not scope here, just note): shop-msg has no recall command for in-flight dispatches, and the encapsulation invariant from lead-231 prohibits rm-ing inbox files. The two existing lead-231.1/.2 dispatches will tolerate this gap because they're more likely to round-trip via work_done than clarify, but recall-side encapsulation is a structural concern worth its own future brief.

Related: lead-231 (brief that surfaced this).

title: Restructure lead-po and lead-architect templates to be role-complete (PDR-001)
description:
Lead-shop work item tracking the dispatch to shopsystem-templates BC for the
role-complete restructure of lead-po.md and lead-architect.md.

Intent driver: PDR-001 (pdr/001-role-templates-role-complete.md).

The PO subagent authors scenarios in this repo's features/templates/ pinning
what role-completeness means against §3.2's activity catalogue. The Architect
subagent then verifies the templates BC's pre-state empirically, applies the
message-type discriminator, and dispatches via shop-msg send to the
shopsystem-templates BC.

This beads ID is the work_id that flows outward in the inter-shop message
per §6.
notes:
Architect dispatched assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates BC on 2026-05-12.

Empirical pre-state verification: ran `shop-templates show lead-po` and
`shop-templates show lead-architect`. Confirmed: neither template names the
§3.2 activities the role-complete restructure requires (Interview stakeholder,
Maintain product brief, Write PDR, Reconcile scenario registers, Write ADRs,
Maintain structurizr, BC decomposition). The capability genuinely does not
exist today — discriminator Q1 → assign_scenarios.

Scenarios 09-16 all passed well-formed Gherkin sufficiency check. CLI computed
hashes and added @bc/@scenario_hash tags. Message deposited at
repos/shopsystem-templates/inbox/lead-kq0.yaml.

Scenario hashes (CLI-computed):
- 09 f1ac9534c1d58318
- 10 dddd6c3b2eed7e45
- 11 9a9421ad59ee5d67
- 12 e522f7393dfcd1c1
- 13 a481db51463526d2
- 14 0aea22a97e63d4f8
- 15 5ccb3fb1122f9341
- 16 a6b3e510821aba52

PO's 4 open questions accepted as-is by Architect with rationale (BC has no
existing 'guidance pending' convention; depth-3+ is a meaningful step; both
short and full §3.2 phrasing appear across scenarios; PDR-001 permits
guidance-pending marker for activities lacking sufficiency-check shape).

Awaiting work_done from BC. Close lead-kq0 on reconciliation.

title: shop-msg send dependency gate compares dispatch_state, not bd status — bd authoring deps wrongly block dispatch

Workaround was removing the satisfied authoring-sequencing edge. Real gap: the gate conflates bd status with dispatch_state, so any bd authoring/sequencing dependency will spuriously block a downstream dispatch. Likely routes to shopsystem-messaging. Router to decide vehicle/sequencing.

Not scenario territory — single-edit content correction in a shop-owned file. Router or architect may handle directly.

title: features/templates/123 scenario e3f961 uses illustrative --note flag; real shop-msg respond mechanism_observation surface is --subject/--body

- features/bc_side_bead_creation_on_inbox_observation.feature:51 (BC-side)
- repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/shop_msg/cli.py:1751 (real CLI surface)

Cross-link [[lead-sn1e]] (parent dispatch).

title: shop-msg send <verb> lacks --description / --rationale flag for lead-side dispatch context
description:
## Empirical finding

Today's PDR-010 dispatch (5 assign_scenarios to shopsystem-messaging: lead-tuu5, lead-eow5, lead-98kk, lead-1w7r, lead-sn1e) needed to carry: (a) ADR citations (ADR-011/012/013/014/015/016/017) for each dispatched file; (b) sequencing notes (lead-tuu5 is foundation; lead-eow5/98kk/1w7r/sn1e depend on it); (c) cross-file context (e.g., 'bd_facade module introduced in 119 is reused by 121/122/123'). The current shop-msg send assign_scenarios surface has no --description or --rationale flag; the descriptive context could only land in the lead-side carrier bead's description + notes. The BC receives the scenarios and the work_id but no lead-side prose.

This was reconciled today by reading ADR-009/010 (scenarios ARE the contract; lead description is lead-side context for tracking, not BC-required input) and proceeding with the existing surface. That reading holds for *contract* purposes — scenarios are sufficient for BC implementation. But it does NOT cover the operational use cases where lead-authored prose IS what the BC needs:

- Dispatch sequencing hints ('land lead-tuu5 first; lead-eow5 depends-on it but ships with parallel inbox row')
- Cross-ADR context ('this file pins ADR-013 which assumes the bd_facade from ADR-016 is in place')
- Carrier-bead summaries that the BC's Implementer would otherwise have to assemble by reading 5 separate carrier beads

## Proposed mechanism

Add to shop-msg send {assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_maintenance}:

- --description <text>  (inline string)
- --description-file <path>  (path to a markdown file)

The dispatched message gains a 'description' field that flows through the postgres deposit and is surfaced via shop-msg read inbox.

## Rationale

Some BC-relevant context is lead-side authored prose that doesn't fit in scenarios — dispatch sequencing hints, cross-ADR context, carrier-bead summaries. Today this context is lead-side-only (carrier bead description + notes), unreachable by the BC Implementer reading the inbox row. The Implementer's loop currently has to either (a) work without that context, (b) request it via clarify (round trip), or (c) the lead architect has to remember to drop a parallel mechanism_observation. None of these are clean.

## Cross-links

Dispatches that surfaced this gap: [[lead-tuu5]] [[lead-eow5]] [[lead-98kk]] [[lead-1w7r]] [[lead-sn1e]].

title: mechanism: parallel in-flight dispatches to same BC create commit-sequencing deadlock
description:
## Pattern observation

Two distinct dispatches to shopsystem-messaging in close temporal proximity have now produced the IDENTICAL block reason: BC completes work and verifies ACs empirically, but cannot commit because the working tree shares files with another in-flight dispatch whose own work has not yet been signed off.

### Instances

1. **lead-2ca** (watch-reconnect tightening, BC reviewer signed off but explicitly DEFERRED commit) — blocked on lead-767 (--force respond work) entanglement: shared storage.py, conftest.py, watch_listen_drop_reconnect.feature.
2. **lead-2122** (fxhyg meta-test hygiene fix) — same blocker: shared tests/conftest.py, plus dependence on uncommitted lead-0bw ephemeral-DB infra and uncommitted lead-6nt _snapshot_production_name / _PER_TEST_MUTATED_NAMES infra. Storage.py allow_multi_type and cli.py _walk_up_resolve_shop are lead-767/lead-b3z.

### Mechanism

When the lead dispatches multiple bugfix/maintenance items to the same BC before earlier dispatches are reviewer-signed-off and committed, the BC's working tree accumulates interleaved changes across files like tests/conftest.py, src/shop_msg/storage.py, src/shop_msg/cli.py. Each new dispatch's fix lands as additional hunks; the BC cannot commit any single item because git-add-p hunk slicing leaves the resulting HEAD referencing functions/imports/features from the still-uncommitted siblings, violating 'leave main green'.

### Why the carrier pattern (lead-pfmb) is not a fix, only mitigation

lead-pfmb sequences the eventual joint push once lead-767 signs off — but it does not prevent the next dispatch (lead-Nxx) from re-entering the same trap. The carrier mounts higher each cycle.

### Candidate remediations (architect to evaluate; not in scope for this bead)

- (a) Lead-side discipline: serialize bugfix dispatches per BC; do not dispatch lead-N to BC X until lead-(N-1) is signed off AND committed.
- (b) BC-side discipline: per-dispatch branch (BC creates a branch per work_id, commits there, lead carrier merges branches in dependency order).
- (c) Architecture: split tests/conftest.py into per-concern modules so per-work hunks land in disjoint files.
- (d) Accept the carrier pattern; codify it as the canonical resolution path with a carrier-creation reflex on every blocked work_done.

### Cross-links

- [[lead-pfmb]] — carrier for lead-2ca + lead-2122 (scope updated 2026-05-29).
- [[lead-2122]] — second instance, fxhyg fix.
- [[lead-2ca]] — first instance, watch-reconnect tightening.
- [[lead-767]] — the umbrella blocking both.

### Not dispatched

This is an observation about the dispatch pattern itself; not a BC-side fix. Architect to decide whether to file an ADR, a process-discipline note, or a redecomposition PDR.
notes: 2026-05-29: Cure pattern empirically validated twice — (1) lead-2ca recovery via lead-pfmb→lead-xscs carrier; (2) lead-nn5f direct single-dispatch after BC tree clean (no entanglement). Mechanism understood; cure pattern observed working. Lowering P2→P3. Keep open as architectural observation seeding the upcoming PDR/ADR. Open question for that PDR: enshrine carrier pattern in role-discipline prose, OR provide a CLI affordance (e.g. 'shop-msg dispatch-after <work_id>' deferring send until predecessor lands).

title: shopsystem-docs: walkthrough parser _FENCE_RE skips indented fences inside numbered list items
description:
Mechanism observation flagged by shopsystem-docs in lead-eqx work_done (2026-05-27).

OBSERVATION:
src/shopsystem_docs/walkthrough.py:_FENCE_RE uses ^``` (line-start anchor), so fenced code blocks indented inside numbered list items are NOT enumerated by the parser. The four docker fences in Move 1 of the walkthrough are skipped.

BC ASSESSMENT (within scenario tolerance):
- Scenario 4d4a451e51d9ce3b (agent-executable walkthrough structure) does not currently pin parser completeness for indented fences.
- Move 1 commands are also valid as numbered-list-item content, so under the scenario as written this is not a defect.
- BC flagged for FUTURE TIGHTENING if the docs BC ever needs to assert structural properties on indented fences (e.g., that every docker command in Move 1 must parse as an executable fence).

TRIAGE:
- Not blocking lead-eqx work_done acceptance.
- Tracking-only: if/when a future doc-structure tightening requires indented-fence enumeration, this bead is the seed for the request_bugfix or assign_scenarios that would re-pin the structure.
- No dispatch needed today.

REFERENCES:
- lead-eqx (closed work_done with this observation in summary)
- shopsystem-docs file: src/shopsystem_docs/walkthrough.py (_FENCE_RE)
close_reason: Walkthrough parser _FENCE_RE accepts indented fences with indent-symmetry back-ref; 5 BDD + 8 unit tests green; commit 548c511 on origin/main.

title: mechanism_observation: cross-tracker work_id pattern (shopsystem-templates-918 from shopsystem-bc-launcher)
description:
Observed during shopsystem-templates-918 clarify drain (2026-05-22, reconfirmed 2026-05-27): the work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' is prefixed with one BC's tracker name (shopsystem-templates) while the actual sender of the assign_scenarios was a different BC (shopsystem-bc-launcher). 'bd search 918' returns no lead-side bead; the lead-gua bead's NOTES treat 918 as a BC-side tracker ID.

This is a cross-tracker work_id pattern: the on-wire work_id is the issuing BC's bead ID, not the lead's bead ID. §6 of the spec names the lead's bead ID as the canonical work_id, so this is an asymmetry worth pinning.

Related: lead-4wy (Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept). That bead documents catalog-schema validation asymmetry; this bead documents the *source-of-truth* asymmetry — which tracker's beads supply the work_id when the dispatch crosses BCs without going through the lead.

In this specific case the dispatch was actually lead-side (lead-gua) but the on-wire work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' was set to the BC-side tracker reference. Whether that was an intentional cross-reference or a misuse is unclear from the surviving evidence.

A formal mechanism_observation cannot be emitted from the lead (the respond subcommands are BC-side). This bead is the lead-side equivalent record. If a future BC re-encounters the same pattern, it should emit a mechanism_observation through 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' citing this bead.

No immediate action required; observation only.

title: Future: shop-msg send clarify_response primitive (ADR-009 path a)
description:
Tracks the future implementation of a lead-side 'shop-msg send clarify_response --bc <name> --work-id <id> --resolution <text> [--scenario-file ...]' primitive per ADR-009 path (a).

Scope (coordinated change):
1. New ClarifyResponse Pydantic schema in repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/catalog/.
2. The lead-to-BC send path opts into allow_multi_type=True for clarify_response (storage.py:210-223) so the row can coexist with the original assign_scenarios/request_bugfix/request_maintenance row on the same work_id.
3. BC-side semantics decision (cf. lead-d6o design question #3): does receipt of clarify_response re-open the original dispatch for re-processing, or stand alone as a pinned answer?
4. shop-msg CLI surface: 'shop-msg send clarify_response' subcommand wired through send.py with --bc/--work-id/--resolution and optional --scenario-file repeats.
5. BC-side read path: 'shop-msg pending inbox' surfaces the clarify_response alongside the original dispatch.
6. Scenarios pinning (a) ClarifyResponse round-trips through the catalog schema, (b) lead-to-BC send with allow_multi_type writes both rows, (c) the BC reads both rows independently, (d) attempt to send clarify_response without prior clarify exit-codes appropriately (or is permissive — TBD).

Depends on:
- ADR-009 (this bead is the implementation target for the deferred path a).
- lead-d6o (multi-turn clarify semantics design).
- lead-8z1 (outbox uniqueness — adjacent: clarify rows in outbox).
- lead-4wy (catalog work_id pattern asymmetry).

Vehicle when implemented: assign_scenarios to shopsystem-messaging with PO-authored scenarios pending. PO authoring blocked on a coordinated brief revision that batches this with lead-d6o/lead-8z1/lead-rv9/lead-4wy per ADR-009 'Alternatives considered → Option A'.

Empirical pre-state (verified 2026-05-27):
- shop-msg send --help shows {request_maintenance, assign_scenarios, request_bugfix} only; no clarify_response.
- shop-msg respond clarify --help confirms BC-side direction (caller is the BC).
- storage.py:178,186-191 already carries allow_multi_type=True for BC-to-lead writes; the gate widening for lead-to-BC writes is the load-bearing change.

NOT blocking ADR-009's operational layer (b) — the de-facto re-dispatch pattern is canonical now and works without this primitive.
notes: Filed 2026-05-27 alongside ADR-009. Tracks the future implementation of 'shop-msg send clarify_response' primitive per ADR-009 path (a). Wired as blocked by lead-d6o, lead-8z1, lead-4wy — those design questions must resolve before this implementation target can be scoped. Operational use of clarify-resolution is canonized in ADR-009 path (b) and does not require this bead to ship.

title: [mechanism] shopsystem-messaging conftest synthetic-name fixtures lack finalizers (bc-alpha/bc-beta/ghost-lead)
description: Mechanism observation surfaced during lead-6c5 work_done. The fix added finalizers protecting production registry names; synthetic test-only names (bc-alpha, bc-beta, ghost-lead) remain registered without restoration. BC explicitly accepted this as a gap (synthetic names are not in the production registry, so test pollution is bounded to the test DB row count, not operational impact). Filed at P3 so if/when registry growth or test-isolation concerns surface, the cleanup is queued. No dispatch yet. Source: lead-6c5 work_done summary probes (5) and (6).

title: [mechanism] bc-launcher crashes with AttributeError on non-string product field in bc-manifest.yaml
description: Mechanism observation surfaced during lead-e6j work_done (shopsystem-bc-launcher). The slugify path on the product field assumes a string; when the manifest contains a non-string YAML scalar (e.g. 'product: 123') the controller crashes with AttributeError rather than emitting a graceful 'non-string product' error message. BC dismissed it as not pinnable (not a realistic user mistake, no committed lead behavior, no existing scenario exercises it). Filed at P3 so the option is queued: if/when we tighten manifest validation broadly (schema, type-checking on string-shaped fields), add a scenario for non-string product alongside it. No dispatch yet; lead has not committed to a behavior. Source: lead-e6j work_done summary probe (6).
close_reason: verified: caf62f5 on origin/main, _read_product_from_manifest raises ManifestProductTypeError for non-string product (int/bool/null/float/list/dict), controller.launch translates to CommandResult(exit_code=1) with single-line stderr; --debug flag re-raises for traceback. 18/18 unit tests pass.

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — allow multiple clarify rows per work_id in outbox
description: The postgres UNIQUE constraint on (bc, work_id, direction, message_type) prevents a BC from filing a second clarify response on the same work_id. This is an implementation artifact — §5 of the spec places no cardinality constraint on clarify messages. Multi-turn clarify exchanges (BC asks, lead answers, BC asks again) are a legitimate protocol flow but are currently blocked, forcing workarounds (mechanism_observation, fresh work_id). Fix: relax or drop the uniqueness on message_type within the outbox direction, or scope the constraint to only message types where one-per-work_id is actually required (work_done).
notes: 2026-06-09 DEMOTE P2->P3: ADR-009 fresh-work_id workaround is the sanctioned path (layer b), removing urgency; lead-8z1 only gates the DEFERRED lead-ox8 clarify_response primitive (ADR-009 path a). Couples to lead-ox8.

title: shop-templates bootstrap fails on existing .beads/ — needs idempotent or skip-bd-init mode
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 setting up shopsystem-test-harness as a canonical shop.

shop-templates bootstrap shells out to 'bd init --skip-agents'. When the target dir already has an initialized .beads/, bd init errors with 'Found existing Dolt database... Aborting.' Bootstrap exits 1 with no scaffold written.

This blocks bootstrap on existing shops with a beads workspace — exactly the 'wrap existing repo' case brief 002 was scoped to handle.

Workaround today: 'shop-templates update' (doesn't shell to bd init) creates .claude/agents/ + canonical settings.json; CLAUDE.md re-rendered manually via python invocation of _render_claude_md() since update is init-only on CLAUDE.md per brief 002 item D.

Resolution shapes:
(a) Bootstrap detects existing .beads/ and skips bd init (idempotent). Matches brief 002's 'wrap existing repo' invariant.
(b) Bootstrap gains --skip-bd-init flag for explicit user control.
(c) Update gains optional CLAUDE.md re-render flag for the case of already-bd-init'd shops migrating to canonical.

(a) + (c) together cover the wrap-existing case cleanly. Worth a brief revision in the bootstrap area.

title: Framework convention: @supersedes:<hash> tag for scenario replacement in inter-shop messaging
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-5mz dispatch (brief 003 §4.4 closure for lead-xkh).

When a new scenario supersedes a previously-dispatched one, the relationship is currently expressed only in prose (Gherkin Feature description block, dispatch description). The Architect dispatching lead-5mz noted that adding an @supersedes:<hash> tag at dispatch time would make the relationship machine-readable in the BC's scenario register, rather than buried in prose.

Concrete shape: catalog schema's @bc:<name> and @scenario_hash:<hash> are existing tag conventions injected by shop-msg send. @supersedes:<hash> would be a parallel injection at dispatch time (could be a --supersedes flag on shop-msg send, or auto-detected from scenario body, or set via PO's authoring tag).

Benefits:
- Machine-readable retire-from-feature-file instruction for the BC's Implementer.
- Audit trail in the BC's outbox when work_done attests against new scenarios.
- Potentially enables a future 'scenario register' surface (lead-otu) to track supersede chains.

Tradeoffs:
- Introduces a new tag convention; requires shop-msg schema or send-side tooling change.
- The prose mechanism works today; this is an ergonomic / discipline improvement.

Worth designing alongside the round-4 consolidation brief (lead-6t3) — that brief will already touch scenario discipline broadly. Could fold.

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook consolidation), lead-otu (scenario register surface), lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization).

title: request_bugfix dispatched: shop-templates bootstrap+update reject missing --target
description: Dispatch of lead-03r consistency-pass to shopsystem-templates BC. Two adjacent missing-required-arg gaps tightened: bootstrap missing --target (TypeError at cli.py:272 Path(None)) and update missing --target (TypeError at cli.py:322 Path(None)). Scenarios 55 and 56 in features/templates/. Precedent: lead-ijr added _validate_shop_name at cli.py:232-250 mirroring _validate_shop_type; Implementer will likely add _validate_target the same shape.
close_reason: work_done complete from shopsystem-templates BC: 108 passed/0 failed; 2 dispatched scenario hashes (fe59a11a88a9ab60 bootstrap, 8fe363bd46cb766c update) match the dispatch; 5 regression-attested pins re-verified. Post-state spot-check confirmed: _validate_target helper added at cli.py:253 paralleling _validate_shop_name; _cmd_bootstrap (line 301) and _cmd_update (line 361) invoke it before any Path() construction. Re-ran both failing invocations: each exits 2 cleanly with stderr naming --target and no traceback. Reviewer's adversarial probes (mutate _validate_target to no-op, mutate diagnostic to omit --target, byte+mtime snapshot of pre-existing scaffold) all confirmed teeth. Monitor pattern milestone: work_done delivered via real-time event notification before any polling.

title: shop-templates bootstrap/update missing --target raises TypeError (consistency pass adjacent to lead-1uo)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 by lead-1uo PO + Architect during scenario 54 authoring/dispatch.

Two adjacent missing-required-arg gaps in shop-templates CLI, same shape as lead-1uo (which only covers --shop-name on bootstrap):

1. shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name foo (missing --target)
   → TypeError at cli.py:242 (Path(args.target) with None).

2. shop-templates update --shop-type bc (missing --target)
   → TypeError at cli.py:292 (Path(args.target) with None).

Note: shop-templates update missing --shop-type already produces a clean diagnostic (_validate_shop_type catches it at lines 394-406). So this consistency pass is narrower than 'audit all subcommands' — only --target on bootstrap + update need tightening.

Vehicle: request_bugfix (same shape as lead-ijr currently in flight). Could fold with lead-ijr's fix if its Implementer's solution generalizes (uniform _validate-style missing-arg diagnostic across CLI); otherwise dispatch separately after lead-ijr round-trip surfaces the chosen pattern.

Defer dispatch until lead-ijr (the lead-1uo fix) returns work_done — then decide whether the Implementer's fix shape extends naturally or whether a separate dispatch is cleaner.

Related: lead-1uo (--shop-name parent), lead-ijr (in-flight fix dispatch).
close_reason: Consistency-pass closure. Both gaps tightened: (1) shop-templates bootstrap missing --target now exits 2 cleanly with stderr naming --target (scenario fe59a11a88a9ab60); (2) shop-templates update missing --target same shape (scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c). Dispatched as lead-79q request_bugfix; Implementer added _validate_target helper paralleling _validate_shop_name from lead-ijr. work_done complete from BC. With this closure, brief 002's bootstrap surface has all known missing-required-arg gaps pinned with clean-error discipline (--shop-type, --shop-name, --target across bootstrap+update). Reviewer flagged that empty-string and nonexistent-path cases for --target are out-of-scope and consistent with --shop-name precedent (same shape, not gaps).

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: PO authored scenarios 55 and 56 (parallel scenarios, not Scenario Outline). Scenario 55: bootstrap rejects missing --target with argparse-style error; mirrors scenario 54 (--shop-name) shape — exit 2, stderr names --target, no traceback / no TypeError, witness target dir untouched (no .claude/agents, no .beads, no CLAUDE.md, no .gitignore). Scenario 56: update rejects missing --target; same exit-2 + named-arg + no-traceback discipline, but the negation pins 'pre-existing shop scaffold is unchanged' (byte+mtime snapshot of .claude/agents/, plus CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/ unchanged), reflecting that update operates on an existing scaffold rather than creating one. Rationale for parallel-over-outline: (1) scenario 54 is flat, parallel preserves symmetry; (2) the negations meaningfully diverge — bootstrap asserts nothing-created against an empty dir, update asserts nothing-modified against an existing scaffold; (3) per-row readability matters for future debugging. Files: features/templates/55-bootstrap-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error.gherkin, features/templates/56-update-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error.gherkin. Tags (@scenario_hash + @bc:shopsystem-templates) deferred to Architect at dispatch. Pre-state confirmed in cli.py: bootstrap_cmd / update_cmd both declare --target with default=None and no required=True (lines 442-446 and 453-457); _cmd_bootstrap at line 272 and _cmd_update at line 322 both call Path(args.target) which raises TypeError on None. Implementation pattern likely mirrors _validate_shop_name (cli.py:232-250): add _validate_target helper invoked early in each _cmd_*, before any Path() construction. No further missing-required-arg gaps known in shop-templates CLI after these scenarios land.

title: bootstrap-rejects-missing-shop-name-with-argparse-error (dispatch of scenario 54 to shopsystem-templates)
description: Dispatch child of lead-1uo (parent P3 bug). Scope: scenario 54 only (tight scope; Monitor-pattern test dispatch). Tightening of unpinned existing behavior — bootstrap currently raises TypeError at cli.py:167 when --shop-name omitted; scenario 54 pins exit 2, argparse-shaped stderr naming --shop-name, no traceback, no scaffold written. Empirically verified by Architect 2026-05-13 via /tmp/probe-lead-1uo probe (TypeError reproduced). Adjacent same-shape gaps (missing --target on bootstrap; missing --target on update) deliberately NOT folded — separate P3 beads to follow. Parent: lead-1uo. Related scenario file: features/templates/54-bootstrap-rejects-missing-shop-name-with-argparse-error.gherkin.
close_reason: work_done from shopsystem-templates verified: status complete, scenario hash 3c8612d20608e9a3 matches dispatch. Post-state spot-check: _validate_shop_name exists at cli.py:232 with the diagnostic shape scenario 54 pins; _cmd_bootstrap invokes it at cli.py:268 returning exit 2 before any scaffold side effects. Empirical re-probe: 'shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --target /tmp/probe-reconcile-ijr' (omitting --shop-name) exits 2, stderr names --shop-name, no traceback, target directory remains empty. 106 BDD passing (was 105+1). No regression on adjacent scenarios (d6e935bca62d0039, 7941cdc591cd2c3b, 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, 32d99f6d4a2dad37). Adjacent same-shape gap on --target (cli.py:272) deferred to lead-03r per separate dispatch.

title: shop-templates bootstrap raises TypeError when --shop-name omitted (should be argparse error or default-derived)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during brief-002 reconciliation by Architect. Empirical probe ran 'shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --target .' (without --shop-name) and got TypeError (None passed to str.replace) instead of an argparse-style error.

Not a regression introduced by brief-002 work — pre-existing surface gap. Either:
(a) --shop-name should be argparse-required, raising a clean parse error.
(b) --shop-name should default-derive (e.g., from --target basename).

P3 — ergonomic, not blocking. Adjacent to brief-002's CLI surface but out of scope of its committed invariants. Surfaced during post-state probe; not part of any in-flight dispatch.

Related: lead-3d7 (brief 002 — closed).
notes: Scenario 54 authored by lead-po (PDR-002 path-a subagent) 2026-05-13. File: features/templates/54-bootstrap-rejects-missing-shop-name-with-argparse-error.gherkin. Realization pinned: --shop-name argparse-required; bootstrap exits 2 with argparse-shaped error naming --shop-name; stderr contains no TypeError / traceback; no scaffold written. Tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates at dispatch by Architect. Adjacent gaps spotted (NOT folded in, flagged for Architect): bootstrap without --target raises TypeError; update without --target raises TypeError. Same shape (None reaches Path() or str.replace() instead of clean argparse-style error). Architect may decide whether to expand scope of dispatch or open separate beads.
close_reason: Parent intent delivered. Child dispatch lead-ijr closed: scenario 54 (3c8612d20608e9a3) pinned and implemented in shopsystem-templates. Realization: --shop-name validated manually with argparse-shaped diagnostic (option (a) from the issue). Post-state empirically verified — exit 2, named flag in stderr, no traceback, no scaffold side effects. Adjacent same-shape gaps on --target (bootstrap and update) flagged in NOTES and tracked separately under lead-03r.

title: bc-implementer singleton-inbox presumption constrains lead-shop dispatch cadence
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during brief-002 Architect pre-state verification.

The bc-implementer canonical template at repos/shopsystem-templates/.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md line 49 says verbatim 'There is exactly one file there.' The Implementer subagent's role contract presumes a singleton inbox. The bc-router CLAUDE.md does not enforce serialization either — its dispatch rule is 'any unprocessed inbox dispatches the Implementer' with no ordering guarantee.

Concrete consequence: the lead shop cannot dispatch a new message to a BC while another work_id is still in flight (still in the BC's inbox, not yet processed to outbox). Architect for brief-002 detected this and held dispatch because lead-bwu was still pending in the templates BC inbox.

Design question: should the protocol/template
- (a) explicitly enforce serial in-flight (one message at a time per BC; lead shop blocks on outbox before next send), formalizing the current presumption?
- (b) relax the singleton presumption (Implementer template handles N-message inbox; BC-router picks one to dispatch per turn)?
- (c) introduce a queue concept in shop-msg (lead can deposit multiple; BC processes in FIFO order; protocol exposes 'next' subcommand)?

(a) is the lowest-touch and matches current template behavior. (c) is the most flexible and may align with the brief-001 invariant (shop-msg encapsulates storage). (b) is a middle ground.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done(blocked) protocol gap) — same shape of latent protocol constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Currently lead-bwu remains pending in templates BC inbox; brief-002 dispatch is held until it lands. This bead captures the structural concern, not the operational hold.

title: shop-msg not on default PATH — requires venv activation for cold dispatches
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

shop-msg is installed at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg but the venv is not activated by default. Any agent dispatched cold (fresh subagent, new shell) must 'source .venv/bin/activate' first or invoke the absolute path; otherwise 'shop-msg ...' fails with command-not-found. The lead-architect's reconciliation pass hit this and worked around it.

What needs to be done: pick one of (a) ensure venv activation is automatic for any lead-shop session (devcontainer postCreate / shell rc), (b) install shop-msg outside the venv so it is on PATH unconditionally, (c) update subagent templates and CLAUDE.md to always invoke the absolute path or activate first. (c) is the lowest-touch / no install changes; (a) or (b) are more systemic. Choose during pickup.

title: Scenario register surface for templates BC (lead-architect activity currently degenerate)
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

The post-lead-kq0 lead-architect template names 'Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work' as a §3.2 activity. No register surface exists under features/templates/ — only the raw .gherkin files (01-16). The activity is structurally degenerate for the templates BC until a register is introduced; the role names a responsibility the mechanism doesn't yet realize.

What needs to be done: decide between (a) introduce a per-BC scenario register surface in the lead shop carrying authored / dispatched / signed-off status (designed once for all BCs, not just templates), or (b) accept reconciliation against the templates BC as degenerate by design and document why. If (a), the design likely interacts with the lead-shop drain workflow noted as deferred in findings/from-mechanism-observation-v1.md §6.

value: shop-msg CLI quick-ref for shopsystem-product lead: binary at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg (NOT on PATH — activate venv or use absolute path). Lead canonical name in registry is the SLUG 'shopsystem-product' (with hyphen), even though .claude/shop/name.md displays 'shopsystem product' (with space). Use --lead shopsystem-product everywhere. Common surface: 'shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product' (Monitor-compatible, persistent); 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product [--bc-name <bc>]' (BC responses pending consume; --bc-name not --bc); 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead shopsystem-product' (BC clarifies); 'shop-msg read outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id>' (read a specific response; uses --bc not --bc-name); 'shop-msg consume outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id> --message-type <clarify|work_done|mechanism_observation>'; 'shop-msg send <verb>' for lead dispatches. 'shop-msg prime' is BROKEN by the name.md slug mismatch — don't rely on it for orientation.

BC bd backend can wedge on launch: empty Dolt working set + no issue_prefix despite committed .beads/issues.jsonl carrying a prefix (launcher derives name-prefix e.g. 'templates'/'shopsystem_templates' but repo uses 'tmpl'). All 5 bd recovery cmds mutually block. ESCAPE HATCH (verified bd v1.0.3): 'bd config set issue_prefix

' FAILS its op but side-effect auto-imports issues.jsonl into empty DB AND adopts the prefix -> backend operational. Wedge breaks work-done-gate Check 4 (no plan sub-issues possible) -> forces all emissions to blocked. Root causes: lead-rply (launcher provisioning, ->bc-launcher) + lead-vlsu (upstream bd init-safety). Inspect/fix via docker exec into bc- (operational state, ADR-018 admissible).

bd uses CWD to locate .beads/, NOT a fixed repo path. When CWD drifts into a sibling repo (e.g. repos/shopsystem-templates/), bd operations hit THAT repo's bd, not the lead shop's bd — and you see different prefixes (shopsystem-templates-*) and different issue sets. Symptom this session (2026-05-22): I cd'd into repos/shopsystem-templates early on, never cd'd back, then spent multiple turns convinced sub-agents were 'confabulating' lead-* IDs that bd 'didn't have'. They weren't confabulating — they were in the right CWD (lead repo) and I was in the wrong one. Workaround: pin every bd/shop-msg/git invocation to /workspaces/shopsystem-product as CWD (use cd or git -C), and treat 'bd show X says no issue found' as a CWD-check trigger before assuming the issue doesn't exist.

Docker-only onboarding prototype in lead repo (updated 2026-06-01 lead-bbjx): compose.yaml (postgres + shopsystem network + pgdata bind to $SHOPSYSTEM_DATA default $HOME/.local/share/shopsystem); bin/shop-shell (brings up postgres if needed, then docker run shopsystem-shell:dev with /workspace + docker.sock + ~/.claude + ~/.gitconfig mounts AND --group-add $(stat -L -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock) so the non-root vscode user can reach the mode-660 root:docker socket — without this, docker is permission-denied inside the shell); Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell now REBASED onto ghcr.io/dstengle/devcontainer-python-node-claude:latest (was python:3.12-slim), which already ships claude + bd + docker CLI + tmux + node + Microsoft docker-init.sh entrypoint, so the Dockerfile only pipx-installs the framework CLIs (scenarios v0.1.0, shop-templates d1a8937, shopsystem-messaging 1514e8a, pinned to pyproject.toml); bc-launcher still OMITTED (private repo, no creds in image). Build: docker build -t shopsystem-shell:dev -f Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell . These three files are now shop-owned ops scaffolding rendered by shopsystem-templates bootstrap (per lead-xjsq); 'shop-templates update' won't clobber them but emits a drift advisory. The socket-perms + claude fixes should propagate UP into the canonical template via a request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates.

Launcher mission COMPLETE 2026-05-30: bc-launcher 9cbb9c1 ships both the launch-step-5 and inject paths using two discrete tmux send-keys invocations (text alone, then bare Enter). Empirically verified at lead — bc-container launch shopsystem-templates with default startup prompt produces an ENGAGED agent at +15s (Monitor armed, pending inbox drained, prose 'Session-start sequence complete' emitted) with no manual empty-inject workaround required. Beads lead-xsmn / lead-9q0f / lead-hyee / lead-lez1 all closed. The 'launch + send-work end-to-end' goal in feedback_autonomous_launcher_mission is satisfied; resume normal lead-shop discipline (no direct edits to repos/*).

lead-ybxs dispatched: shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-templates --work-id lead-ybxs (2 fixes to ops scaffolding, ref impl f551e30). Pre-state verified against installed shop_templates package data ops/shop-shell + ops/Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell. Conflict enum: 134 (d7f3b1b3118f6a88) additive, 135 (2dfa4b5ab79dd829) CONTRADICTED by docker-ce-cli removal -> needs lead-side PO body revision, 137 (f706707653aa646a) unaffected. NOTE: DB registry was empty this session; re-added shopsystem-product (lead) and shopsystem-templates via shop-msg registry add over DSN postgresql://postgres:postgres@shopsystem-messaging-postgres-1:5432/shopsystem (container-network address; localhost:5432 refused, SHOPMSG_DSN unset in lead-host shell).

Manual propagation workaround for missing 'shop-templates update' (lead-xjsq): refresh canonical lead-primer and inline agent files by 'cp $PKG/templates/claude/lead.md .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md' and 'shop-templates show <role> > .claude/agents/<role>.md'. The package data path is $VENV/lib/python<ver>/site-packages/shop_templates/templates. Used to close lead-6gaj's propagation gap.

Dispatch hazard found 2026-06-02 (during lead-tk0f): the INSTALLED shop-msg send / scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash canonicalizes by wrapping the gherkin body with a 'Feature:' header and hashing the Feature-line-INCLUDED text. Canonical scenario features/templates/117 REQUIRES scenario-block-only hashing (Feature line NOT included) — so the installed tool violates 117. Empirical: bare scenario block of 135 hashes 9bc85eced7685a40; same block with a Feature: line hashes differently (transient 0ff0711ff6334e47 seen via --scenario-file). The ScenarioPayload validator enforces hash==compute_scenario_hash(gherkin), so passing Feature-line-bearing gherkin forces the WRONG hash and disagrees with on-disk @scenario_hash: tags. WORKAROUND when dispatching: build the payload with a Feature-line-FREE gherkin block and use 'shop-msg send --payload <file>' (not --scenario-file) so the pinned hash matches the canonical scenario-block-only value. PROPER FIX is an architect-deferred decision (117 lines 46-59): which BC owns canonicalization — shopsystem-scenarios or shopsystem-messaging — then request_bugfix. Not yet filed.

shopsystem-bc-launcher is a PRIVATE github repo as of 2026-06-01. Host devcontainer has cached git creds so 'pip install git+https://...bc-launcher' works; fresh containers without creds get 'fatal: could not read Username for https://github.com'. Affects: Dockerfile pre-warm layers, bootstrap from any non-credentialed environment. Decision deferred to user via lead-aj5f (P1, labeled human): make repo public, or document credential mounting.

Deferred revised sc06 body (5174e405a19358fa) per ADR-024 D2 / lead-8z1o:
Scenario: the system-wide outstanding view counts a canonical scenario absent from every BC journal and the lead snapshot as outstanding
  Given a canonical scenario authored under this repo's features with block-only canonical hash "h6"
  And no BC journal records "h6" as completed and the lead snapshot does not record "h6" as completed
  When the system-wide outstanding view is computed over all canonical scenarios under features
  Then the outstanding view lists the scenario with block-only canonical hash "h6" as outstanding
  And the scenario with hash "h6" is counted in the outstanding denominator on its absence from every BC journal and the lead snapshot alone, independent of whether any work_done ever landed for it

PyYAML 1.1 coerces GitHub Actions 'on' key to boolean True. In step defs parsing workflow YAML with yaml.safe_load(), use wf.get(True) not wf.get('on'). The shopsystem-devcontainer BC uses a _get_workflow_on() helper (tests/conftest.py:820) as the fix. Consider ruamel.yaml (YAML 1.2) to avoid this permanently.

shopsystem-bc-launcher: assert_docker_run_includes_flag step does not assert container_name against the run_cmd — latent fragility if multiple containers run in same test. BC noted this in lead-0eu work_done summary. Queue a request_bugfix once lead-90z and lead-cyo are processed.
